metadata.natural_key: (vr_id, vc_id) — one row per active verse_context occurrence
metadata.purpose: Baseline for progressive AI-driven cleanup of verse to sub-group to VSG assignments. The 'current' object holds the live DB analytical state; refinement layers should be added under sibling keys (e.g. 'proposed', 'issues', 'review'). Two vr_ids have duplicate active verse_context rows — those are data-integrity issues already on the cleanup list and are surfaced rather than hidden.

verses.verse_text: Mar 9:32 But they did not understand the saying , and were afraid to ask him .

meaning.text: agnoeō names the disciples' inner failure of comprehension — they do not understand the passion prediction. P-status: the span is not carrying inner-being content distinctively beyond the suniēmi parallel.

verses.verse_text: Luk 9:45 But they did not understand this saying , and it was concealed from them , so that they might not perceive it . And they were afraid to ask him about this saying .

meaning.text: agnoeō names the judicial concealment of understanding — the disciples could not grasp what was being said. P-status.

verses.verse_text: Act 13:27 For those who live in Jerusalem and their rulers , because they did not recognize him nor understand the utterances of the prophets , which are read every Sabbath , fulfilled them by condemning him.

current.analysis_note: "did not recognise him... fulfilled prophecies by condemning him" — culpable spiritual ignorance producing the crucifixion

meaning.text: agnoeō names the culpable inner failure to recognise Jesus — the Jerusalem rulers' ignorance of both Jesus and the prophets. This ignorance becomes the mechanism of fulfilment.

new_vcg.title: agnoeō: culpable spiritual ignorance of God's ways and character
new_vcg.description: The inner condition of not knowing God's ways, righteousness, or purposes — when the ignorance is morally significant, not merely informational.

db.description: Term names culpable spiritual ignorance — the inner condition of not knowing God's character, ways, or will, which becomes the basis of moral failure or self-deception

new.title: agnoeō: culpable spiritual ignorance of God's ways and character
new.description: The inner condition of not knowing God's ways, righteousness, or purposes — when the ignorance is morally significant, not merely informational.

verses.verse_text: Act 17:23 For as I passed along and observed the objects of your worship , I found also an altar with this inscription , ‘To the unknown god .’ What therefore you worship as unknown , this I proclaim to you .

current.analysis_note: "to the unknown god" — agnoeō as the Gentiles' ignorance of the God Paul now proclaims

meaning.text: agnoeō names the worshippers' inner ignorance of the one they worship — the Athenians have a devotion without knowing its object. Paul names this as the condition he addresses.

verses.verse_text: Rom 1:13 I do not want you to be unaware , brothers , that I have often intended to come to you ( but thus far have been prevented ), in order that I may reap some harvest among you as well as among the rest of the Gentiles .

new_vcg.title: Purposive inner intention set before the will: protithēmi as planned goal
new_vcg.description: The act of setting a goal or purpose before the will — either human (a travel intention repeatedly frustrated) or divine (God setting forth Christ as propitiation from an eternal purpose).

new.title: Purposive inner intention set before the will: protithēmi as planned goal
new.description: The act of setting a goal or purpose before the will — either human (a travel intention repeatedly frustrated) or divine (God setting forth Christ as propitiation from an eternal purpose).

verses.verse_text: Rom 2:4 Or do you presume on the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience , not knowing that God’s kindness is meant to lead you to repentance ?

current.analysis_note: "not knowing that God's kindness is meant to lead you to repentance" — agnoeō as the inner condition that produces presumptuousness

meaning.text: agnoeō names culpable ignorance of God's purpose — the presumptuous person does not know that divine patience is intended as a call to repentance.

verses.verse_text: Rom 6:3 Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death ?
Rom 7:1 Or do you not know , brothers — for I am speaking to those who know the law — that the law is binding on a person only as long as he lives ?
Rom 10:3 For , being ignorant of the righteousness of God , and seeking to establish their own , they did not submit to God’s righteousness .

current.analysis_note: "ignorant of the righteousness of God" — agnoeō of God's righteousness producing self-righteousness

meaning.text: agnoeō names the inner ignorance of God's righteousness as the root of Israel's failure — not knowing God's righteousness, they pursue self-righteousness instead.

verses.verse_text: Rom 11:25 Lest you be wise in your own sight, I do not want you to be unaware of this mystery , brothers : a partial hardening has come upon Israel , until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in .
1Cor 10:1 For I do not want you to be unaware , brothers , that our fathers were all under the cloud , and all passed through the sea ,
1Cor 12:1 Now concerning spiritual gifts , brothers , I do not want you to be uninformed .
1Cor 14:38 If anyone does not recognize this, he is not recognized .
2Cor 1:8 For we do not want you to be unaware , brothers , of the affliction we experienced in Asia . For we were so utterly burdened beyond our strength that we despaired of life itself .
2Cor 2:11 so that we would not be outwitted by Satan ; for we are not ignorant of his designs .

current.analysis_note: "we are not ignorant of his designs" — negative: Paul and his co-workers do not have agnoeō of Satan's schemes

meaning.text: agnoeō names culpable ignorance of Satan's strategies — Paul and his companions are not in this condition; they know Satan's designs. The inner knowing protects against being outwitted.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 6:9 as unknown , and yet well known ; as dying , and behold , we live ; as punished , and yet not killed ;
Gal 1:22 And I was still unknown in person to the churches of Judea that are in Christ .
1Th 4:13 But we do not want you to be uninformed , brothers , about those who are asleep , that you may not grieve as others do who have no hope .
1Ti 1:13 though formerly I was a blasphemer , persecutor , and insolent opponent . But I received mercy because I had acted ignorantly in unbelief ,

current.analysis_note: "I received mercy because I had acted ignorantly in unbelief" — agnoeō as the condition in which Paul's persecution took place; mitigating but not excusing

meaning.text: agnoeō names the culpable inner ignorance of Paul's pre-conversion persecution — not knowing what he was doing becomes the grounds of mercy.

verses.verse_text: Heb 5:2 He can deal gently with the ignorant and wayward , since he himself is beset with weakness .

2Pe 2:12 But these , like irrational animals , creatures of instinct, born to be caught and destroyed , blaspheming about matters of which they are ignorant , will also be destroyed in their destruction ,

current.analysis_note: "blaspheming about matters of which they are ignorant" — agnoeō producing blasphemy

meaning.text: agnoeō names the culpable inner ignorance of false teachers — they blaspheme what they do not know, and their ignorance does not excuse them.

verses.verse_text: Heb 5:14 But solid food is for the mature , for those who have their powers of discernment trained by constant practice to distinguish good from evil .

current.analysis_note: "powers of discernment trained by constant practice to distinguish good from evil" — aisthētērion as the inner faculty of moral-perceptive discrimination; it is developable through practice; solid food (mature teaching) produces it

meaning.text: aisthētērion names the inner faculty of moral discernment — specifically as a trained capacity, developed through practice. The faculty is not automatic but cultivated by repeated exercise.

new_vcg.title: aisthētērion: the cultivated inner faculty of moral discernment
new_vcg.description: Single verse. Names moral discernment as a trainable inner faculty — not innate but developed through practice. The mature person's ability to distinguish good from evil is an acquired capacity.

db.description: Term names the inner faculty of moral discernment — the trained and exercised capacity to distinguish good from evil; discernment as a developed inner capability

new.title: aisthētērion: the cultivated inner faculty of moral discernment
new.description: Single verse. Names moral discernment as a trainable inner faculty — not innate but developed through practice. The mature person's ability to distinguish good from evil is an acquired capacity.

verses.verse_text: Eph 5:15 Look carefully then how you walk , not as unwise but as wise ,

current.analysis_note: "not as unwise but as wise" — asofos as structural opposite; the unwise person is the one not walking in wisdom

verses.verse_text: Mat 15:16 And he said , “ Are you also still without understanding ?

current.analysis_note: "are you also still without understanding?" — Jesus to the disciples; asunetos as the inner condition of those who have not yet grasped

meaning.text: asunetos names the absence of the inner perceptive faculty in the disciples — they have not grasped the point of what Jesus has just said.

verses.verse_text: Mar 7:18 And he said to them , “ Then are you also without understanding ? Do you not see that whatever goes into a person from outside cannot defile him ,

current.analysis_note: "then are you also without understanding?" — same address; the understanding faculty not yet operative in the disciples

meaning.text: asunetos names the absence of the inner perceptive faculty in the disciples — they have not grasped the meaning of the teaching on defilement.

verses.verse_text: Rom 1:21 For although they knew God , they did not honor him as God or give thanks to him, but they became futile in their thinking , and their foolish hearts were darkened .

current.analysis_note: "foolish hearts were darkened" — asunetos as the darkened inner condition of those who suppressed knowledge of God

meaning.text: asunetos names the foolish inner condition of those who refused to honour God — the heart without understanding is darkened.

new_vcg.title: Universal failure of human inner comprehension: suniēmi / asunetos
new_vcg.description: The total absence of inner understanding in fallen humanity — not a specific judicial act but a description of the general human condition before God.

verses.verse_text: Rom 1:31 foolish , faithless , heartless , ruthless .

current.analysis_note: "foolish, faithless, heartless, ruthless" — asunetos as one vice among the cluster marking those who have rejected God

meaning.text: asunetos names foolishness as one item in a list of moral failures — the absence of the inner perceptive faculty alongside the absence of faithfulness and compassion.

verses.verse_text: Rom 10:19 But I ask , did Israel not understand ? First Moses says , “ I will make you jealous of those who are not a nation ; with a foolish nation I will make you angry .”

current.analysis_note: "with a foolish nation I will make you angry" — the Gentiles are a foolish (asunetos) nation in God's purpose; understanding absent

meaning.text: asunetos names the Gentile nation as foolish — lacking the inner perceptive faculty, yet used by God to provoke Israel.

verses.verse_text: Luk 14:31 Or what king , going out to encounter another king in war , will not sit down first and deliberate whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand ?

current.analysis_note: "sit down first and deliberate whether he is able" — bouleuō as strategic deliberation

meaning.text: bouleuō names the inner deliberative act of weighing options — the parable uses military planning as the figure for the inner consideration that discipleship requires.

new_vcg.title: Deliberate inner decision-making: bouleuō as the mind resolving upon a course of action
new_vcg.description: The inner weighing of options leading to a resolved decision — the mind deliberating and settling upon a course of action.

db.description: Term names the inner act of deliberate decision — the mind resolving upon a course of action through weighing and choosing

new.title: Deliberate inner decision-making: bouleuō as the mind resolving upon a course of action
new.description: The inner weighing of options leading to a resolved decision — the mind deliberating and settling upon a course of action.

verses.verse_text: Joh 11:53 So from that day on they made plans to put him to death .

current.analysis_note: "they made plans to put him to death" — collective lethal planning

meaning.text: bouleuō names the deliberate inner resolve formed against Jesus — the council's purposive decision to kill him, made and held from that moment.

new_vcg.title: Deliberate resolve to harm: bouleuō naming purposive decision directed against another
new_vcg.description: The inner will resolving upon a course of action specifically directed toward harming or killing another person.

db.description: Term names deliberate planning directed toward harm — the inner will forming an intention against another

new.title: Deliberate resolve to harm: bouleuō naming purposive decision directed against another
new.description: The inner will resolving upon a course of action specifically directed toward harming or killing another person.

verses.verse_text: Joh 12:10 So the chief priests made plans to put Lazarus to death as well ,

current.analysis_note: "the chief priests made plans to put Lazarus to death as well" — the killing-plan extended to the witness

meaning.text: bouleuō names the extended deliberate resolve — the same purposive intent extended to include Lazarus because his existence was evidence for Jesus.

new_vcg.title: Deliberate resolve to harm: bouleuō naming purposive decision directed against another
new_vcg.description: The inner will resolving upon a course of action specifically directed toward harming or killing another person.

meaning.text: bouleuō names the inner resolve formed in anger — the council's purposive intention to kill the apostles.

meaning.text: bouleuō names the deliberate inner resolve formed in a crisis — navigating by deliberate decision toward the best available option.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:17 Was I vacillating when I wanted to do this ? Do I make my plans according to the flesh , ready to say “ Yes , yes ” and “ No , no ” at the same time?

current.analysis_note: "do I make my plans according to the flesh?" — bouleuō as plan-making; Paul denying fleshly planning

meaning.text: bouleuō names Paul's purposive intent about travel — the charge of vacillation prompts the defence that his deliberate plans are not fleshly.

verses.verse_text: Mat 1:19 And her husband Joseph , being a just man and unwilling to put her to shame , resolved to divorce her quietly .

current.analysis_note: "resolved to divorce her quietly" — boulomai as deliberate inner resolution

meaning.text: boulomai names Joseph's inner volitional resolution — he forms a deliberate purpose to act in a way that protects Mary while complying with his understanding of the law.

new_vcg.title: Human volitional intent: boulomai naming purposive inner will in motion
new_vcg.description: The human inner will/desire forming an intent and moving toward action. Covers a wide range from trivial preferences to morally significant choices. The inner volitional act is what is named.

verses.verse_text: Mat 11:27 All things have been handed over to me by my Father , and no one knows the Son except the Father , and no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.

current.analysis_note: "no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him" — the Son's sovereign boulomai as the gateway to knowledge of the Father

meaning.text: boulomai names Christ's sovereign inner will in disclosure — the choosing is an act of divine purposive will.

verses.verse_text: Mar 15:15 So Pilate , wishing to satisfy the crowd , released for them Barabbas , and having scourged Jesus , he delivered him to be crucified .

current.analysis_note: "Pilate, wishing to satisfy the crowd" — boulomai as the inner desire shaping a public decision

meaning.text: boulomai names Pilate's inner volitional intent — he wills to satisfy rather than to do justice, and acts accordingly.

verses.verse_text: Luk 10:22 All things have been handed over to me by my Father , and no one knows who the Son is except the Father , or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.”

current.analysis_note: Same as Mat 11:27 parallel

meaning.text: boulomai names Christ's sovereign willing of disclosure — same as Mat 11:27 parallel.

verses.verse_text: Luk 22:42 saying , “ Father , if you are willing , remove this cup from me . Nevertheless , not my will , but yours , be done .”

current.analysis_note: "Father, if you are willing, remove this cup; not my will but yours" — the Father's boulomai as the absolute; the Son's deferred to it

meaning.text: boulomai names the human willing of Christ in Gethsemane — the prayer expresses both his inner desire (remove the cup) and his submission to the Father's sovereign will. The tension between human and divine willing is at the centre.

new_vcg.title: God's sovereign purposive will: boulomai naming divine willing in disclosure, creation, and redemption
new_vcg.description: The inner purposive will of God or Christ — sovereign, self-originating, directing revelation, creation, gifts, and redemption. The will is not contingent on human response.
new_vcg.verse_annotation: human willing of Christ in Gethsemane submitted to divine will

current.analysis_note: "do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?" — Pilate's crowd-boulomai in a moment of divine irony

meaning.text: boulomai names the inner willing of the crowd as Pilate presents the choice — he appeals to their want/desire to shape the outcome.

meaning.text: boulomai names the imputed intent of the apostles as perceived by the authorities — their teaching is read as purposive intention.

meaning.text: diabebaioō names the outward insistence arising from inner conviction — they assert with confidence without the inner understanding that should ground such conviction. The form is present without the inner substance.

meaning.text: diaginōskō NR status — legal determination, not inner-being content.

verses.verse_text: Mat 16:3 And in the morning , ‘It will be stormy today , for the sky is red and threatening .’ You know how to interpret the appearance of the sky , but you cannot interpret the signs of the times .

verses.verse_text: Mar 9:34 But they kept silent , for on the way they had argued with one another about who was the greatest .

new_vcg.title: Reasoned discourse as engagement of the inner life: dialegō
new_vcg.description: Paul's method of reasoning in synagogues and public venues — persuasive discourse aimed at reaching and convincing the inner person. The goal is inner persuasion; the form is reasoned argument. The effect can be conviction (Felix alarmed), communal growth, or stubborn resistance.
new_vcg.subsection: Productive reasoning aimed at the inner person

db.description: Term names the act of reasoning and disputing as an engagement of inner faculties — where discourse aims to persuade, convince, or address the inner life of hearers, or expresses inner orientation

verses.verse_text: Act 19:9 But when some became stubborn and continued in unbelief , speaking evil of the Way before the congregation , he withdrew from them and took the disciples with him, reasoning daily in the hall of Tyrannus .

meaning.text: dialegō names the daily reasoning in a public venue — Paul's systematic engagement of the inner life of all who came through reasoned discourse.

verses.verse_text: Act 20:9 And a young man named Eutychus , sitting at the window , sank into a deep sleep as Paul talked still longer . And being overcome by sleep , he fell down from the third story and was taken up dead .

meaning.text: dialegō names the extended talking that caused Eutychus to fall — the length of Paul's reasoning discourse.

meaning.text: dialegō names Paul's denial of disruptive disputing — the term here covers the kind of public reasoned debate that he did not engage in inflammatory form.

verses.verse_text: Jude 9 But when the archangel Michael , contending with the devil , was disputing about the body of Moses , he did not presume to pronounce a blasphemous judgment , but said , “The Lord rebuke you .”

meaning.text: dialegō names the dispute/disputation as a form of inner-faculty engagement — even angelic conflict over a contested matter takes the form of reasoned disputation.

verses.verse_text: Mat 16:7 And they began discussing it among themselves , saying , “We brought no bread .”

current.analysis_note: 'they began discussing it among themselves' — communal dialogizomai about a practical problem

meaning.text: dialogizomai names the communal inner reasoning about a practical matter — discussion among themselves as a form of shared deliberation.

new_vcg.title: Communal shared deliberation: dialogizomai as reasoning together
new_vcg.description: The disciples or others reasoning together as a group — discussing a shared situation, usually producing an inadequate response that Jesus addresses.

current.analysis_note: 'why are you discussing among yourselves?' — Jesus naming the communal dialogizomai

meaning.text: dialogizomai names the disciples' anxious shared reasoning — Jesus perceives their collective deliberation and challenges it.

verses.verse_text: Mat 21:25 The baptism of John , from where did it come ? From heaven or from man ?” And they discussed it among themselves , saying , “ If we say , ‘ From heaven ,’ he will say to us , ‘ Why then did you not believe him ?’

current.analysis_note: 'they discussed it among themselves' — communal reasoning about John's baptism

meaning.text: dialogizomai names the calculating shared inner reasoning of the leaders — they reason through the political consequences of each answer.

new_vcg.title: Disputed opinions, doubts, and inner questioning: dialogismos as contested inner deliberation
new_vcg.description: The inner reasoning that is in dispute — either doubts arising in the heart that disturb faith, or contested opinions that produce quarrelling within the community.

db.description: Term names inner doubts, arguments, or disputed opinions — the mind's questioning and deliberating about contested matters

new.title: Disputed opinions, doubts, and inner questioning: dialogismos as contested inner deliberation
new.description: The inner reasoning that is in dispute — either doubts arising in the heart that disturb faith, or contested opinions that produce quarrelling within the community.

verses.verse_text: 1Ti 2:8 I desire then that in every place the men should pray , lifting holy hands without anger or quarreling ;

current.analysis_note: 'without anger or quarreling' — dialogismos paired with anger; contentious inner reasoning

meaning.text: dialogismos names the inner disputing/quarreling from which prayer must be free — what the hands do in prayer must not be accompanied by inner deliberative controversy.

new_vcg.title: Disputed opinions, doubts, and inner questioning: dialogismos as contested inner deliberation

verses.verse_text: Jam 2:4 have you not then made distinctions among yourselves and become judges with evil thoughts ?

current.analysis_note: 'become judges with evil thoughts' — dialogismos as inner moral reasoning producing unjust discrimination

meaning.text: dialogismos names the evil inner reasoning that produces unjust discrimination — the deliberative inner evaluation of persons by appearance is named as evil.

new_vcg.title: Evil thoughts arising from the heart: dialogismos as corrupt inner reasoning
new_vcg.description: The evil reasoning or thoughts that originate in the heart — not incidental but structural, preceding and generating sinful action. They are the inner source of moral failure.

db.description: Term names the evil thoughts that originate in the heart — the corrupt inner reasoning that produces sin and estrangement from God

new.title: Evil thoughts arising from the heart: dialogismos as corrupt inner reasoning

verses.verse_text: Luk 11:17 But he , knowing their thoughts , said to them , “ Every kingdom divided against itself is laid waste , and a divided household falls .

current.analysis_note: 'knowing their thoughts, he said to them' — dianoema as the inner formed thoughts of the crowd; Jesus perceives them immediately and responds to their content before they are voiced

meaning.text: dianoēma names the inner thoughts of the crowd that Jesus knows — the formed inner thought-content of persons that divine perception grasps directly.

new_vcg.title: Hidden inner thoughts known directly to Jesus: dianoēma
new_vcg.description: A single verse. The hidden inner thought-content of persons that is directly accessible to Jesus's perception — he knows what they are thinking before they speak.

new.title: Hidden inner thoughts known directly to Jesus: dianoēma

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:28 But if there is no one to interpret , let each of them keep silent in church and speak to himself and to God .

meaning.text: diermēneutēs names the interpreter whose absence requires the speaker to redirect tongues toward private inner communion with God — without interpretation, the only inner-life engagement possible is personal, not communal.

new_vcg.title: Spirit-given interpretation as a community inner-life building gift: hermēneia and diermēneutēs
new_vcg.description: The Spirit-given capacity to interpret tongues — enabling what is spoken in tongues to become intelligible inner content for the community. Without interpretation, tongues cannot build up the inner life of others; only private inner communion with God remains.

verses.verse_text: Jam 1:6 But let him ask in faith , with no doubting , for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea that is driven and tossed by the wind .

meaning.text: eikō names the simile that characterises inner instability — the doubter's inner condition is characterised through comparison with a wave. The simile reveals the nature of the inner state: directionless, tossed, unstable.

new_vcg.title: Simile characterising an inner state: eikō
new_vcg.description: The simile reveals what an inner condition is like — the comparison illuminates the nature of inner instability or unreceptive hearing.

db.description: Term frames a simile that characterises an inner state — self-deception or inner instability

new.description: The simile reveals what an inner condition is like — the comparison illuminates the nature of inner instability or unreceptive hearing.

meaning.text: eikō names the simile that characterises the hearer-not-doer — the comparison with the mirror-looker who immediately forgets reveals the nature of the inner response: seeing without retaining or acting.

new_vcg.title: Simile characterising an inner state: eikō

current.analysis_note: 'as he considered these things' — Joseph turning the problem over in his mind before the angel intervenes

current.analysis_note: 'pray to the Lord that, if possible, the intent of your heart may be forgiven you' — epinoia as the heart-located formed intention; explicitly requiring divine forgiveness; the inner design stands as a moral object before God

meaning.text: epinoia names the purposeful inner intent of Simon's heart — the formed inner thought-disposition that animated his offer of money for the Spirit. This intent requires repentance and forgiveness.

meaning.text: logos P-status — quotation of Psalm, no distinctive inner-being content in span.

verses.verse_text: Rom 9:6 But it is not as though the word of God has failed . For not all who are descended from Israel belong to Israel ,
Rom 9:9 For this is what the promise said : “ About this time next year I will return , and Sarah shall have a son .”
Rom 9:28 for the Lord will carry out his sentence upon the earth fully and without delay .”

Rom 13:9 For the commandments, “You shall not commit adultery , You shall not murder , You shall not steal , You shall not covet ,” and any other commandment , are summed up in this word : “You shall love your neighbor as yourself .”

meaning.text: logos names the summarising word that holds all commandments — the word as inner-dwelling moral principle. All the law's moral content is gathered into the single neighbour-love word, which becomes the internal moral standard.

1Cor 12:8 For to one is given through the Spirit the utterance of wisdom , and to another the utterance of knowledge according to the same Spirit ,

new_vcg.title: sofia: wisdom hidden in Christ and given from above
new_vcg.description: Divine wisdom as the content of what God has hidden in Christ and gives as a gift — the alternative to human wisdom, with named qualities, Spirit-given, belonging to God and the Lamb.

new.title: sofia: wisdom hidden in Christ and given from above
new.description: Divine wisdom as the content of what God has hidden in Christ and gives as a gift — the alternative to human wisdom, with named qualities, Spirit-given, belonging to God and the Lamb.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:9 So with yourselves , if with your tongue you utter speech that is not intelligible , how will anyone know what is said ? For you will be speaking into the air .
1Cor 14:19 Nevertheless , in church I would rather speak five words with my mind in order to instruct others , than ten thousand words in a tongue .

meaning.text: logos names the words spoken with the mind (nous) — the inner-directed, intelligible word that reaches the inner person of the hearer and instructs. Quantity is irrelevant; the inner engagement of the mind is what counts.

meaning.text: logos names the single word that fulfils the entire law — the moral totality of the law compressed into neighbour-love. As in Rom 13:9, logos here is the inner moral principle.

meaning.text: logos names the word of Christ as the inner-dwelling presence — it takes up residence within the person richly, shaping inner wisdom and producing community formation. The word is a dwelling inner content.

Col 4:3 At the same time , pray also for us , that God may open to us a door for the word , to declare the mystery of Christ , on account of which I am in prison —
Col 4:6 Let your speech always be gracious , seasoned with salt , so that you may know how you ought to answer each person .

meaning.text: methermēneuō names the translation that reveals the inner theological meaning of the name — the act of translating Immanuel discloses that the name carries "God with us" as its inner content. Translation as theological revelation.

2Cor 2:11 so that we would not be outwitted by Satan ; for we are not ignorant of his designs .

current.analysis_note: 'we are not ignorant of his designs' — noema as Satan's formed inner thought-designs; the enemy has a noema that Paul is aware of

meaning.text: noēma names Satan's designs or thought-schemes — the formed inner strategy of the adversary that the apostolic community knows and guards against.

new_vcg.title: The inner cognitive faculty as site of spiritual contest: noēma
new_vcg.description: The mind as inner cognitive faculty is not neutral — it can be hardened, blinded, led astray, or guarded. Satan works through it; God guards it; the apostolic mission takes its thoughts captive. All six uses locate noēma as a contested inner terrain.

current.analysis_note: "by your foresight, most excellent Felix, reforms are being made for this nation"

meaning.text: pronoia names the capacity for purposeful governance foresight — the inner capacity to anticipate and plan for the good of those under one's care. Tertullus applies it flattery-wise to Felix.

new_vcg.title: Purposeful foresight and provision: pronoia as governance foresight or deliberate inner provision
new_vcg.description: The capacity for purposeful foresight — either in governance (anticipating and providing for those under care) or in deliberate provision for desire (making plans to gratify inner want).

verses.verse_text: Rom 13:14 But put on the Lord Jesus Christ , and make no provision for the flesh , to gratify its desires .

current.analysis_note: "make no provision for the flesh, to gratify its desires" — pronoia as forward-provision planning; here refused toward the flesh

meaning.text: pronoia names deliberate inner provision — the act of planning ahead to satisfy desire. The command is not to make purposive plans for the flesh's gratification.

current.analysis_note: "I have often intended to come to you" — Paul's repeated inner intention; deliberate personal intent

meaning.text: protithēmi names Paul's purposive inner intention — a goal set before the will that has been repeatedly frustrated.

new_vcg.description: The act of setting a goal or purpose before the will — either human (a travel intention repeatedly frustrated) or divine (God setting forth Christ as propitiation from an eternal purpose).

current.analysis_note: P: "God put forward as a propitiation by his blood" — divine setting-forth of Christ → Group 165

meaning.text: protithēmi names God's purposive inner intention publicly displayed — Christ as propitiation is what God set forth as his intended means of atonement.

verses.verse_text: Mat 11:19 The Son of Man came eating and drinking , and they say , ‘ Look at him ! A glutton and a drunkard , a friend of tax collectors and sinners !’ Yet wisdom is justified by her deeds .”

current.analysis_note: "wisdom is justified by her deeds" — personified wisdom vindicated

verses.verse_text: Mat 12:42 The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it , for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon , and behold , something greater than Solomon is here .

current.analysis_note: "hear the wisdom of Solomon" — wisdom as what is sought in a great person

verses.verse_text: Mat 13:54 and coming to his hometown he taught them in their synagogue , so that they were astonished , and said , “ Where did this man get this wisdom and these mighty works ?

current.analysis_note: "where did this man get this wisdom?" — wisdom as the observable quality that astonishes; people recognise it without being able to account for it

verses.verse_text: Mar 6:2 And on the Sabbath he began to teach in the synagogue , and many who heard him were astonished , saying , “ Where did this man get these things ? What is the wisdom given to him ? How are such mighty works done by his hands ?

current.analysis_note: "what is the wisdom given to him?" — the wisdom is observed; its origin questioned; it belongs to the person as a given quality

verses.verse_text: Luk 2:40 And the child grew and became strong , filled with wisdom . And the favor of God was upon him .

current.analysis_note: "filled with wisdom and the favour of God was upon him" — wisdom as the inner quality of the growing child

verses.verse_text: Luk 2:52 And Jesus increased in wisdom and in stature and in favor with God and man .

current.analysis_note: "increased in wisdom" — wisdom as a growing inner characteristic; quantifiable, progressive

verses.verse_text: Luk 7:35 Yet wisdom is justified by all her children .”

current.analysis_note: "wisdom justified by all her children" — personified divine wisdom vindicated by those who receive her

verses.verse_text: Luk 11:31 The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the men of this generation and condemn them , for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon , and behold , something greater than Solomon is here .

current.analysis_note: "to hear the wisdom of Solomon" — wisdom as what can be sought and heard in a person

verses.verse_text: Luk 11:49 Therefore also the Wisdom of God said , ‘I will send them prophets and apostles , some of whom they will kill and persecute ,’

current.analysis_note: "the Wisdom of God said" — divine wisdom as speaker; personified

verses.verse_text: Luk 21:15 for I will give you a mouth and wisdom , which none of your adversaries will be able to withstand or contradict .

current.analysis_note: "I will give you a mouth and wisdom which none can withstand" — wisdom as a given quality for testimony; opponents cannot overcome it

verses.verse_text: Act 6:3 Therefore , brothers , pick out from among you seven men of good repute , full of the Spirit and of wisdom , whom we will appoint to this duty .

current.analysis_note: "full of the Spirit and of wisdom" — wisdom as the inner quality qualifying leaders

verses.verse_text: Act 6:10 But they could not withstand the wisdom and the Spirit with which he was speaking .

current.analysis_note: "could not withstand the wisdom and the Spirit with which he was speaking" — wisdom visible in speech; irresistible

verses.verse_text: Act 7:10 and rescued him out of all his afflictions and gave him favor and wisdom before Pharaoh , king of Egypt , who made him ruler over Egypt and over all his household .

current.analysis_note: "gave him favour and wisdom before Pharaoh" — wisdom as a quality given to Joseph and recognisable by Pharaoh

verses.verse_text: Act 7:22 And Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians , and he was mighty in his words and deeds .

current.analysis_note: "instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians" — wisdom as cultural-intellectual competence received through instruction

verses.verse_text: Rom 11:33 Oh , the depth of the riches and wisdom and knowledge of God ! How unsearchable are his judgments and how inscrutable his ways !

current.analysis_note: "depth of riches and wisdom and knowledge of God" — wisdom as divine attribute beyond human comprehension

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:17 For Christ did not send me to baptize but to preach the gospel , and not with words of eloquent wisdom , lest the cross of Christ be emptied of its power.

current.analysis_note: "not with words of eloquent wisdom, lest cross be emptied of power" — sophia as what would drain the cross of its power

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:19 For it is written , “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise , and the discernment of the discerning I will thwart .”

current.analysis_note: "destroy the wisdom of the wise, thwart discernment" — sofia as the category being abolished

meaning.text: sofia names wisdom as the human category that God will destroy — already noted above under G4680 sofos for the same verse.

new_vcg.title: sofos / sofia: human wisdom overturned by the cross
new_vcg.description: Human wisdom as a category that the cross renders inadequate, foolish, and subject to divine judgment. God hides revelation from the wise and reveals it to children. The wisdom of the world is folly before God.

db.description: sofia and sofos in 1 Corinthians 1–3, and in parallel passages, name human wisdom as a category that the cross overturns. God hides things from the wise; the wisdom of the world is folly with God. This is not wisdom inverted by the person claiming it (A-4) but wisdom as a category that stands in structural opposition to the wisdom of God.

new.title: sofos / sofia: human wisdom overturned by the cross
new.description: Human wisdom as a category that the cross renders inadequate, foolish, and subject to divine judgment. God hides revelation from the wise and reveals it to children. The wisdom of the world is folly before God.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:20 Where is the one who is wise ? Where is the scribe ? Where is the debater of this age ? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of the world ?

current.analysis_note: **ANCHOR (sofia)** — "Has God not made foolish the wisdom of the world?"

new_vcg.title: sofos / sofia: human wisdom overturned by the cross
new_vcg.description: Human wisdom as a category that the cross renders inadequate, foolish, and subject to divine judgment. God hides revelation from the wise and reveals it to children. The wisdom of the world is folly before God.

db.description: sofia and sofos in 1 Corinthians 1–3, and in parallel passages, name human wisdom as a category that the cross overturns. God hides things from the wise; the wisdom of the world is folly with God. This is not wisdom inverted by the person claiming it (A-4) but wisdom as a category that stands in structural opposition to the wisdom of God.

new.title: sofos / sofia: human wisdom overturned by the cross
new.description: Human wisdom as a category that the cross renders inadequate, foolish, and subject to divine judgment. God hides revelation from the wise and reveals it to children. The wisdom of the world is folly before God.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:21 For since , in the wisdom of God , the world did not know God through wisdom , it pleased God through the folly of what we preach to save those who believe .

current.analysis_note: "world did not know God through wisdom" — sophia as the instrument that failed to reach God

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:22 For Jews demand signs and Greeks seek wisdom ,

current.analysis_note: "Greeks seek wisdom" — sofia as cultural aspiration; seeking wisdom as an alternative to the cross

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:24 but to those who are called , both Jews and Greeks , Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God .

current.analysis_note: "Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God" — Christ as the embodiment of divine wisdom

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:30 And because of him you are in Christ Jesus , who became to us wisdom from God , righteousness and sanctification and redemption ,

current.analysis_note: "became to us wisdom from God" — Christ as the locus of received wisdom; it comes from outside

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:1 And I , when I came to you , brothers , did not come proclaiming to you the testimony of God with lofty speech or wisdom .

current.analysis_note: "did not come proclaiming with lofty speech or wisdom" — sophia as what Paul deliberately avoided

meaning.text: sofia names human wisdom as a mode of presentation Paul deliberately avoided — the cross does not commend itself through the category of wisdom.

meaning.text: sofia names human wisdom as the rhetorical category Paul's preaching did not employ — Spirit and power replaced it.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:6 Yet among the mature we do impart wisdom , although it is not a wisdom of this age or of the rulers of this age , who are doomed to pass away .

current.analysis_note: "we do impart wisdom... not wisdom of this age" — wisdom given to the mature; distinguished from worldly wisdom

new_vcg.title: sofia: wisdom hidden in Christ and given from above
new_vcg.description: Divine wisdom as the content of what God has hidden in Christ and gives as a gift — the alternative to human wisdom, with named qualities, Spirit-given, belonging to God and the Lamb.

db.description: Wisdom as a gift — God gives it, persons receive it, it enables what the recipient could not produce from themselves. The giving and receiving is the characteristic dynamic of this group.

meaning.text: sofia names the divine wisdom — hidden, decreed before the ages, for our glory. This is not human wisdom but God's own.

new_vcg.title: sofia: wisdom hidden in Christ and given from above
new_vcg.description: Divine wisdom as the content of what God has hidden in Christ and gives as a gift — the alternative to human wisdom, with named qualities, Spirit-given, belonging to God and the Lamb.

db.description: Wisdom as a gift — God gives it, persons receive it, it enables what the recipient could not produce from themselves. The giving and receiving is the characteristic dynamic of this group.

new.title: sofia: wisdom hidden in Christ and given from above
new.description: Divine wisdom as the content of what God has hidden in Christ and gives as a gift — the alternative to human wisdom, with named qualities, Spirit-given, belonging to God and the Lamb.

verses.verse_text: 2Ti 3:15 and how from childhood you have been acquainted with the sacred writings , which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus .

current.analysis_note: "make you wise for salvation" — sofizo as the transformative act; Scripture makes wise

meaning.text: sofizo names the act of making wise — Scripture's transformative work on the inner person. The writings impart wisdom that orients the person toward salvation.

new_vcg.title: sofizo: making wise (positive) and devising myths (negative)
new_vcg.description: Two opposite uses of the same term — one naming Scripture's transformative work of making the person wise for salvation; the other naming what the apostles explicitly did not do (devise clever myths).
new_vcg.verse_annotation: positive — Scripture producing inner wisdom oriented toward salvation

verses.verse_text: Mat 11:25 At that time Jesus declared , “I thank you , Father , Lord of heaven and earth , that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and revealed them to little children ;

current.analysis_note: same — "hidden from wise and understanding"

verses.verse_text: Act 5:2 and with his wife’s knowledge he kept back for himself some of the proceeds and brought only a part of it and laid it at the apostles ’ feet .

meaning.text: suneidō names the wife's inner conscious awareness — she was a co-knower of the deception. Moral complicity as shared inner awareness.

new_vcg.title: Conscious inner awareness: suneidō as moral complicity and cognitive recognition
new_vcg.description: The same inner faculty of conscious awareness operates in two registers: (a) moral co-knowing — the inner awareness of shared participation in a morally charged act; and (b) cognitive recognition — becoming aware of a situation and acting on it.
new_vcg.verse_annotation: suneidō — shared inner awareness of the deception

db.description: Term names a conscious inner awareness — moral complicity or cognitive recognition — that informs volitional response. The moral-complicity pole (shared inner co-knowing of one's participation in a morally charged act) is the primary conscience characteristic; the cognitive-recognition pole (becoming aware of a situation) represents the term's broader semantic range within the same faculty.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:20 Brothers , do not be children in your thinking . Be infants in evil , but in your thinking be mature .

current.analysis_note: 'do not be children in your thinking. Be infants in evil, but in your thinking be mature' — fren as the inner thinking-capacity with a maturity spectrum; believers are called to its full development

meaning.text: frēn names the faculty of mature thinking — the capacity for rational and moral discernment that marks adult inner life. To be children in thinking is to be undeveloped in the faculty; to be mature is to have it trained and functioning at full capacity.

new_vcg.title: The faculty of mature thinking: frēn
new_vcg.description: A single verse. The capacity for moral and rational discernment — named as a faculty that can be childlike or mature. The command is to be mature in this faculty while being infants in evil.

db.description: Term names the faculty of mature thinking — the capacity for moral and rational discernment that marks adult inner life

new.title: The faculty of mature thinking: frēn
new.description: A single verse. The capacity for moral and rational discernment — named as a faculty that can be childlike or mature. The command is to be mature in this faculty while being infants in evil.

verses.verse_text: Mat 16:23 But he turned and said to Peter , “ Get behind me , Satan ! You are a hindrance to me . For you are not setting your mind on the things of God , but on the things of man .”

current.analysis_note: "not setting your mind on things of God but things of man" — froneō as directional orientation; Peter's is wrong-directed

meaning.text: froneō names the inner orientation — what the mind is set on. Peter's mind is oriented toward human concerns, not divine ones. The rebuke names the direction of the inner person's habitual attention.

verses.verse_text: Mar 8:33 But turning and seeing his disciples , he rebuked Peter and said , “ Get behind me , Satan ! For you are not setting your mind on the things of God , but on the things of man .”

current.analysis_note: "not setting your mind on things of God" — same correction; the inner orientation is missetting

meaning.text: froneō names the same inner orientation as Mat 16:23 — the parallel passage. Same meaning.

verses.verse_text: Act 28:22 But we desire to hear from you what your views are, for with regard to this sect we know that everywhere it is spoken against .”

current.analysis_note: "your views" — froneō as settled inner perspective/position

verses.verse_text: Rom 8:5 For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh , but those who live according to the Spirit set their minds on the things of the Spirit .

current.analysis_note: "set their minds on things of flesh / Spirit" — froneō as the determinative inner orientation; flesh-minded or Spirit-minded describes the whole person

verses.verse_text: Rom 12:3 For by the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think , but to think with sober judgment , each according to the measure of faith that God has assigned .

current.analysis_note: "think with sober judgment, each according to measure of faith" — froneō directed toward self-assessment; wisdom about oneself

verses.verse_text: Rom 12:16 Live in harmony with one another . Do not be haughty , but associate with the lowly . Never be wise in your own sight.

current.analysis_note: "live in harmony... never be wise in your own sight" — shared froneō as community basis; self-directed froneō refused

meaning.text: froneō names self-attributed inner wisdom — the same pattern as Rom 11:25. The mind set on one's own superiority is warned against.

verses.verse_text: Rom 14:6 The one who observes the day , observes it in honor of the Lord . The one who eats , eats in honor of the Lord , since he gives thanks to God , while the one who abstains , abstains in honor of the Lord and gives thanks to God .

current.analysis_note: "the one who observes the day, observes it in honor of the Lord" — froneō as the inner orientation governing practice

verses.verse_text: Rom 15:5 May the God of endurance and encouragement grant you to live in such harmony with one another , in accord with Christ Jesus ,

current.analysis_note: "live in harmony, in accord with Christ Jesus" — shared froneō in the community

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 13:11 When I was a child , I spoke like a child , I thought like a child , I reasoned like a child . When I became a man , I gave up childish ways .

current.analysis_note: "thought like a child... gave up childish ways" — froneō as developmental inner orientation that matures

meaning.text: froneō names the inner mode of thinking characteristic of a stage of life — childish thinking gives way to adult thinking. Wisdom here is associated with maturity of inner orientation.

new_vcg.title: froneō: relational concern and communal alignment
new_vcg.description: The inner orientation directed toward another person or the community — attention, care, shared mind, alignment in conflict resolution.

db.description: froneō does not name a single faculty but the orientation of the whole inner person — what one is set on, caring about, thinking about as a habitual posture. The Philippians verses are central: "have this mind which is in Christ Jesus" is the paradigmatic use. Reading the verses, 1825 (mind on divine vs earthly) and 1826 (relational/communal mindedness) are two expressions of the same characteristic: froneō as the inner orientation of the whole person, directed toward an object. They belong in one group.

new.title: froneō: relational concern and communal alignment
new.description: The inner orientation directed toward another person or the community — attention, care, shared mind, alignment in conflict resolution.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 13:11 Finally , brothers , rejoice . Aim for restoration , comfort one another, agree with one another , live in peace ; and the God of love and peace will be with you .

current.analysis_note: "agree with one another, live in peace" — shared froneō as community orientation

meaning.text: froneō names the inner orientation of mutual alignment — being of the same mind with one another as an expression of wisdom in community.

new_vcg.title: froneō: relational concern and communal alignment

db.description: froneō does not name a single faculty but the orientation of the whole inner person — what one is set on, caring about, thinking about as a habitual posture. They belong in one group.

verses.verse_text: Luk 1:17 and he will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah , to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children , and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just , to make ready for the Lord a people prepared .”

current.analysis_note: "to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children, and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just" — fronesis as the moral-practical understanding that follows conversion; what John's ministry will produce in hearts

meaning.text: fronesis names the inner wisdom/understanding that characterises the just — the quality the disobedient are to be turned toward. Understanding as moral orientation.

new_vcg.title: fronesis: practical wisdom / moral orientation as divine gift
new_vcg.description: Practical insight as a divine endowment — enabling recognition of God's will, naming the moral orientation of the just, shaping the inner person toward wisdom in all things.
new_vcg.verse_annotation: fronesis as the moral orientation of the righteous

db.description: Term names understanding as an inner-being capacity — the comprehension of God's will that enables transformed living

new.title: fronesis: practical wisdom / moral orientation as divine gift
new.description: Practical insight as a divine endowment — enabling recognition of God's will, naming the moral orientation of the just, shaping the inner person toward wisdom in all things.

verses.verse_text: Eph 1:8 which he lavished upon us , in all wisdom and insight

current.analysis_note: "lavished upon us in all wisdom and insight" — fronesis as divinely given practical understanding

meaning.text: fronesis names insight as a divine gift lavished on believers — an inner capacity for understanding God's will given alongside wisdom.

new_vcg.title: suniēmi / sunesis: inner comprehension of mystery as divine gift
new_vcg.description: The inner comprehension of what was hidden — the mystery of Christ, the mystery of God's will — given as a divine endowment, not acquired by human intelligence.

db.description: Term names understanding as an inner-being capacity — the comprehension of God's will that enables transformed living

verses.verse_text: Mat 7:24 “ Everyone then who hears these words of mine and does them will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock .

current.analysis_note: "wise man who built his house on the rock" — fronimos evidenced in obedient construction

meaning.text: fronimos names the wise person as one whose inner quality shows in hearing and doing — wisdom is the orientation of the person who acts on what Christ says.

new_vcg.title: fronimos: practical shrewd wisdom expressed in action and stewardship
new_vcg.description: The inner quality of practical wisdom that shows in foresighted action — preparation for what is needed, trustworthy stewardship in the master's absence, shrewd navigation of a hostile world.

db.description: Shrewdness as an inner quality of practical wisdom — the capacity for perceptive, pragmatic discernment in worldly dealings, commended even when applied by those without spiritual orientation (Luk 16:8).

new.title: fronimos: practical shrewd wisdom expressed in action and stewardship
new.description: The inner quality of practical wisdom that shows in foresighted action — preparation for what is needed, trustworthy stewardship in the master's absence, shrewd navigation of a hostile world.

verses.verse_text: Mat 10:16 “ Behold , I am sending you out as sheep in the midst of wolves , so be wise as serpents and innocent as doves .

current.analysis_note: "be wise as serpents and innocent as doves" — fronimos as the directive inner quality for mission

meaning.text: fronimos names practical shrewd wariness as the inner quality needed for navigating a hostile world — wisdom as perceptive caution.

new_vcg.title: fronimos: practical shrewd wisdom expressed in action and stewardship
new_vcg.description: The inner quality of practical wisdom that shows in foresighted action — preparation for what is needed, trustworthy stewardship in the master's absence, shrewd navigation of a hostile world.

verses.verse_text: Mat 24:45 “ Who then is the faithful and wise servant , whom his master has set over his household , to give them their food at the proper time ?

current.analysis_note: "faithful and wise servant" — fronimos paired with faithfulness; the quality produces trustworthy stewardship

meaning.text: fronimos names wisdom as the inner quality of the trustworthy servant — paired with faithfulness as the characteristic of the one who acts reliably in the master's absence.

verses.verse_text: Mat 25:2 Five of them were foolish , and five were wise .

current.analysis_note: "five were wise" — fronimos as the distinguishing quality of the prepared virgins

meaning.text: fronimos names the five virgins as wise — their inner quality is their defining characteristic, set against the foolish five.

new_vcg.title: fronimos: practical shrewd wisdom expressed in action and stewardship
new_vcg.description: The inner quality of practical wisdom that shows in foresighted action — preparation for what is needed, trustworthy stewardship in the master's absence, shrewd navigation of a hostile world.

db.description: Shrewdness as an inner quality of practical wisdom — the capacity for perceptive, pragmatic discernment in worldly dealings, commended even when applied by those without spiritual orientation (Luk 16:8).

new.title: fronimos: practical shrewd wisdom expressed in action and stewardship
new.description: The inner quality of practical wisdom that shows in foresighted action — preparation for what is needed, trustworthy stewardship in the master's absence, shrewd navigation of a hostile world.

verses.verse_text: Mat 25:4 but the wise took flasks of oil with their lamps .

current.analysis_note: "wise took flasks of oil" — fronimos expressed in practical foresight and provision

meaning.text: fronimos names the inner quality that produces foresighted action — the wise prepared in advance for what would be needed.

verses.verse_text: Mat 25:8 And the foolish said to the wise , ‘ Give us some of your oil , for our lamps are going out .’

current.analysis_note: "foolish said to wise, give us your oil" — fronimos as the quality that provides for others only after providing for oneself

meaning.text: fronimos names the wise as those who have prepared — their inner quality is visible in what they hold and the foolish lack.

verses.verse_text: Mat 25:9 But the wise answered , saying , ‘ Since there will not be enough for us and for you , go rather to the dealers and buy for yourselves .’

current.analysis_note: "wise answered: go buy for yourselves" — fronimos maintains appropriate limits

meaning.text: fronimos names the wise whose inner quality produces clear-eyed assessment of what is possible — they do not give what they cannot spare.

verses.verse_text: Luk 12:42 And the Lord said , “ Who then is the faithful and wise manager , whom his master will set over his household , to give them their portion of food at the proper time ?

current.analysis_note: "faithful and wise manager" — fronimos in Luke; stewardship wisdom

meaning.text: fronimos names the wise manager — same pattern as Mat 24:45. Wisdom and faithfulness as the paired inner qualities of trustworthy stewardship.

verses.verse_text: Luk 16:8 The master commended the dishonest manager for his shrewdness . For the sons of this world are more shrewd in dealing with their own generation than the sons of light .

current.analysis_note: "sons of this world more shrewd" — fronimos as worldly prudence; ironic commendation

meaning.text: fronimos names worldly shrewdness as an inner quality that the sons of this world exercise more effectively than the sons of light — a challenge to exercise equivalent wisdom in spiritual matters.

new_vcg.title: fronimos: practical shrewd wisdom expressed in action and stewardship
new_vcg.description: The inner quality of practical wisdom that shows in foresighted action — preparation for what is needed, trustworthy stewardship in the master's absence, shrewd navigation of a hostile world.

verses.verse_text: Rom 11:25 Lest you be wise in your own sight, I do not want you to be unaware of this mystery , brothers : a partial hardening has come upon Israel , until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in .

current.analysis_note: "lest you be wise in your own sight" — fronimos in negative use; self-directed wisdom refused

verses.verse_text: Rom 12:16 Live in harmony with one another . Do not be haughty , but associate with the lowly . Never be wise in your own sight.

current.analysis_note: "never be wise in your own sight" — same refusal

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 4:10 We are fools for Christ’s sake , but you are wise in Christ . We are weak , but you are strong . You are held in honor , but we in disrepute .

current.analysis_note: "you are wise in Christ" — ironic; fronimos claimed by those who are actually foolish in Christ

meaning.text: fronimos names the Corinthians' self-assessed wisdom — Paul uses the term ironically, contrasting their self-regard with the apostles' apparent foolishness.

new_vcg.title: sofos / sofia: human wisdom overturned by the cross
new_vcg.description: Human wisdom as a category that the cross renders inadequate, foolish, and subject to divine judgment. God hides revelation from the wise and reveals it to children. The wisdom of the world is folly before God.

db.description: Shrewdness as an inner quality of practical wisdom — the capacity for perceptive, pragmatic discernment in worldly dealings, commended even when applied by those without spiritual orientation (Luk 16:8).

meaning.text: fronimos names the capacity for sound rational judgment that Paul appeals to — the inner faculty of discernment that enables the Corinthians to evaluate his argument.

meaning.text: fronimos names the Corinthians' self-assessed wisdom ironically — their supposed wisdom makes them tolerant of foolishness, which Paul exposes as its own form of foolishness.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:33 Now therefore let Pharaoh select a discerning and wise man , and set him over the land of Egypt .

current.analysis_note: "discerning and wise man" — bin paired with cha.kham as the inner quality qualifying for governance; understanding is the perceptive capacity that enables right decision

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty as a qualification for appointment — the discerning person perceives situations rightly and can govern.

new_vcg.title: Understanding as governance qualification: bin / bi.nah as what leadership requires
new_vcg.description: Understanding paired with wisdom as the inner quality that qualifies persons for governance, judgment, and appointments. The capacity to discern rightly between situations, persons, and cases.

db.description: bin as the inner perceptive faculty in its constituted form — the quality of the wise, discerning person. The faculty is seated in the heart (1Ki 3:9, Psa 49:20), enables right judgment (1Ki 3:9,11), receives instruction (Pro 9:8), perceives situations rightly (2Sa 12:19, Job 6:30), and is the inner capacity from which wise conduct flows. It is morally conditioned — it enables covenant-keeping (Deu 4:6) and is blocked by the absence of the fear of the Lord.

new.title: Understanding as governance qualification: bin / bi.nah as what leadership requires
new.description: Understanding paired with wisdom as the inner quality that qualifies persons for governance, judgment, and appointments. The capacity to discern rightly between situations, persons, and cases.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:39 Then Pharaoh said to Joseph , “ Since God has shown you all this , there is none so discerning and wise as you are.

current.analysis_note: "none so discerning and wise as you" — Joseph praised for superlative bin; the faculty recognised by Pharaoh

meaning.text: bin names the perceptive faculty recognised in Joseph — God's disclosure is what has demonstrated the quality.

verses.verse_text: Deu 1:13 Choose for your tribes wise , understanding , and experienced men , and I will appoint them as your heads .’

current.analysis_note: "wise, understanding, and experienced men" — bin as qualification for tribal leadership; the inner perceptive capacity named alongside wisdom

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty as one of three qualities required for leadership — the capacity to grasp situations and discern rightly.

meaning.text: bin names understanding as what covenant obedience will be seen to be — understanding demonstrated through faithfulness. Assigned to 816-002.

new_vcg.title: Understanding gained through seeking and engagement with the word and covenant
new_vcg.description: Understanding as the fruit of deliberate orientation toward God — seeking, keeping the precepts, holding to the covenant. The faculty grows and deepens through the person's active engagement.

db.description: Term names understanding as the inner reception and grasp of revealed truth — the capacity to perceive insight into mystery, to receive what God gives, and to resist what God thwarts

new.description: Understanding as the fruit of deliberate orientation toward God — seeking, keeping the precepts, holding to the covenant. The faculty grows and deepens through the person's active engagement.

verses.verse_text: Deu 32:7 Remember the days of old ; consider the years of many generations ; ask your father , and he will show you, your elders , and they will tell you .

current.analysis_note: "consider the years of many generations" — bin directed toward historical reflection as the path to understanding God's ways

meaning.text: bin names the reflective perceptive act — considering the past in order to understand the present. The faculty is directed toward historical memory.

new_vcg.title: Directional attending: bin as perceptive attention directed toward an object
new_vcg.description: Bin used in the sense of directing perceptive attention toward something — looking closely, examining carefully, paying attention. Distinct from understanding as comprehension. The faculty is directed attention, not cognitive grasping.

db.description: Term names the prayerful seeking and receiving of understanding from God — the inner posture of humility and petition before God for discernment

new.title: Directional attending: bin as perceptive attention directed toward an object
new.description: Bin used in the sense of directing perceptive attention toward something — looking closely, examining carefully, paying attention. Distinct from understanding as comprehension. The faculty is directed attention, not cognitive grasping.

verses.verse_text: Deu 32:10 “He found him in a desert land , and in the howling waste of the wilderness ; he encircled him, he cared for him, he kept him as the apple of his eye .

current.analysis_note: "he cared for him" — God's attentive understanding expressed as care; the inner faculty of divine perception directed toward Israel in the wilderness

meaning.text: bin names God's attentive care — his perceptive attending to Israel in the wilderness. The faculty here is divine attention directed toward the person.

verses.verse_text: Deu 32:29 If they were wise , they would understand this ; they would discern their latter end !

current.analysis_note: "they would understand this; they would discern their latter end" — bin as the capacity to perceive moral and eschatological consequences

meaning.text: bin names the perceptive faculty needed to grasp the consequences of one's choices — discerning the end from the present.

new_vcg.title: sa.khal as perceptive prudent discernment: seeing clearly what is before you
new_vcg.description: The inner capacity for perceptive attending — seeing God's acts, situations, or conditions with clarity and wisdom. The prudent person considers, ponders, observes rightly where others miss or fail to attend.

db.description: bin as the inner perceptive faculty in its constituted form — the quality of the wise, discerning person. The faculty is seated in the heart (1Ki 3:9, Psa 49:20), enables right judgment (1Ki 3:9,11), receives instruction (Pro 9:8), perceives situations rightly (2Sa 12:19, Job 6:30), and is the inner capacity from which wise conduct flows. It is morally conditioned — it enables covenant-keeping (Deu 4:6) and is blocked by the absence of the fear of the Lord.

new.title: sa.khal as perceptive prudent discernment: seeing clearly what is before you
new.description: The inner capacity for perceptive attending — seeing God's acts, situations, or conditions with clarity and wisdom. The prudent person considers, ponders, observes rightly where others miss or fail to attend.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 3:8 And the Lord called Samuel again the third time . And he arose and went to Eli and said , “ Here I am, for you called me.” Then Eli perceived that the Lord was calling the boy .

current.analysis_note: "Eli perceived that the Lord was calling the boy" — this is the positive use of bin (Eli finally understands). But it is in group 1363. **Reading: this verse shows understanding achieved after repeated failure to understand.** The understanding eventually grasps what was repeatedly missed. The verse records the moment of perception. This belongs in B-1, not B-4. **Flag: vr=173355 may be misassigned to 1363.**

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive act — Eli reads the situation correctly on the third occurrence and grasps what is happening.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 16:18 One of the young men answered , “ Behold , I have seen a son of Jesse the Bethlehemite , who is skillful in playing , a man of valor , a man of war , prudent in speech , and a man of good presence , and the Lord is with him .”

current.analysis_note: "prudent in speech" — bin expressed in speech; the inner faculty governing articulate communication

meaning.text: bin names prudence in speech as an expression of the inner perceptive faculty — David is described as one whose understanding governs his words.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 12:19 But when David saw that his servants were whispering together, David understood that the child was dead . And David said to his servants , “Is the child dead ?” They said , “He is dead .”

current.analysis_note: "David understood that the child was dead" — bin as perceptual comprehension in a lived situation; understanding reading indirect signs
current.notes: Re-evaluation v3_10: revised from 932-003 to 932-001 — David is the subject perceiving the child's death; bin names human inner cognitive act, not God's comprehension.

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive act — reading the servants' behaviour and grasping what it means without being told.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 3:9 Give your servant therefore an understanding mind to govern your people , that I may discern between good and evil , for who is able to govern this your great people ?”

current.analysis_note: "an understanding mind to govern... that I may discern between good and evil" — Solomon's request; bin as the faculty for moral-judicial discernment located in the mind

meaning.text: bin names the discerning faculty as what Solomon requests — an inner capacity to perceive and distinguish, seated in the heart/mind, necessary for governance.

new_vcg.title: Understanding as governance qualification: bin / bi.nah as what leadership requires
new_vcg.description: Understanding paired with wisdom as the inner quality that qualifies persons for governance, judgment, and appointments. The capacity to discern rightly between situations, persons, and cases.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 3:11 And God said to him, “ Because you have asked this , and have not asked for yourself long life or riches or the life of your enemies , but have asked for yourself understanding to discern what is right ,

current.analysis_note: "asked for understanding to discern what is right" — bin as the faculty for moral discernment; what Solomon asked and God approved

meaning.text: bin names the understanding Solomon asked for — the inner faculty that enables right discernment, valued by God as the right thing to ask for.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 3:12 behold , I now do according to your word . Behold , I give you a wise and discerning mind , so that none like you has been before you and none like you shall arise after you .

current.analysis_note: "a wise and discerning mind" — bin as the inner quality given by God; located in the mind

meaning.text: bin names the discerning faculty God gives Solomon — seated in the mind, unparalleled, enabling right perception and judgment.

new_vcg.description: Understanding paired with wisdom as the inner quality that qualifies persons for governance, judgment, and appointments. The capacity to discern rightly between situations, persons, and cases.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 3:21 When I rose in the morning to nurse my child , behold , he was dead . But when I looked at him closely in the morning , behold , he was not the child that I had borne .”

current.analysis_note: "looked at him closely" — bin in its observational use; perceptive examination
current.notes: Re-evaluation v3_10: revised from 932-003 to 932-001 — subject is the human woman (her scrutiny of the infant), not God; bin names human perceptual discernment, not divine comprehension of humans.

meaning.text: bin names the perceptive looking — the mother's careful examination that reveals the truth. The faculty is directed toward physical observation with understanding.

new_vcg.description: Bin used in the sense of directing perceptive attention toward something — looking closely, examining carefully, paying attention. Distinct from understanding as comprehension. The faculty is directed attention, not cognitive grasping.
