verses.verse_text: 1Ch 15:22 Chenaniah , leader of the Levites in music , should direct the music , for he understood it.

current.analysis_note: "he understood it" — Chenaniah understood music; bin as domain-specific inner comprehension
current.notes: Musical understanding as a domain of the human cognitive discernment capacity; borderline-retain per §3.3

meaning.text: bin names the perceptive mastery of a craft — understanding music as an inner competence that qualifies for leadership of it.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 25:7 The number of them along with their brothers , who were trained in singing to the Lord , all who were skillful , was 288 .

current.analysis_note: "all who were skillful" — bin as trained competence in a skill; understanding as enabling skilled performance
current.notes: Musical skill as domain of human understanding capacity; borderline-retain

meaning.text: bin names skillfulness in music as an inner competence — understanding of the craft qualifying the person for temple service.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 25:8 And they cast lots for their duties , small and great , teacher and pupil alike .

current.analysis_note: "teacher and pupil alike" — bin as the inner capacity that makes one a teacher (as distinct from a pupil)

meaning.text: bin names the teacher as one whose inner perceptive faculty qualifies them to instruct — the one who understands enough to teach.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 27:32 Jonathan , David’s uncle , was a counselor , being a man of understanding and a scribe . He and Jehiel the son of Hachmoni attended the king’s sons .

current.analysis_note: "a man of understanding and a scribe" — bin as the inner quality identifying Jonathan as a counsellor

meaning.text: bin names understanding as a defining inner quality of Jonathan — it qualifies him as counselor. Understanding and scribal knowledge are paired.

new_vcg.title: bin / bi.nah as constituted inner perceptive faculty: understanding governing speech, relationships, and perception
new_vcg.description: Understanding as a substantive inner quality of the person — what they are characterised by. The faculty is active, receptive, and productive: it governs what the wise person perceives, how they speak, and how they relate. The person of understanding is defined by this quality.
new_vcg.subsection: Understanding as a receptive and productive quality

db.description: bin as the inner perceptive faculty in its constituted form — the quality of the wise, discerning person. The faculty is seated in the heart (1Ki 3:9, Psa 49:20), enables right judgment (1Ki 3:9,11), receives instruction (Pro 9:8), perceives situations rightly (2Sa 12:19, Job 6:30), and is the inner capacity from which wise conduct flows. It is morally conditioned — it enables covenant-keeping (Deu 4:6) and is blocked by the absence of the fear of the Lord.

new.title: bin / bi.nah as constituted inner perceptive faculty: understanding governing speech, relationships, and perception
new.description: Understanding as a substantive inner quality of the person — what they are characterised by. The faculty is active, receptive, and productive: it governs what the wise person perceives, how they speak, and how they relate. The person of understanding is defined by this quality.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 28:9 “And you , Solomon my son , know the God of your father and serve him with a whole heart and with a willing mind , for the Lord searches all hearts and understands every plan and thought . If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will cast you off forever .

current.analysis_note: "the Lord searches all hearts and understands every plan and thought"

meaning.text: bin names God's comprehensive inner perception of the human person — he understands every plan and thought. The faculty here is divine.

new_vcg.title: God's perceptive comprehension of the human person: bin / bi.nah as divine knowing
new_vcg.description: God's inner perceptive faculty directed toward the human person — his understanding of thoughts, deeds, plans, and the heart. Divine understanding of the human is comprehensive and intimate.

db.description: Term names God's inner comprehension of the human person — God's perception, understanding, and attending to the heart, thought, and deed

new.title: God's perceptive comprehension of the human person: bin / bi.nah as divine knowing
new.description: God's inner perceptive faculty directed toward the human person — his understanding of thoughts, deeds, plans, and the heart. Divine understanding of the human is comprehensive and intimate.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 11:23 And he dealt wisely and distributed some of his sons through all the districts of Judah and Benjamin , in all the fortified cities , and he gave them abundant provisions and procured wives for them.

current.analysis_note: "dealt wisely" — bin expressed through wise practical action

meaning.text: bin names the wise perceptive action — the inner faculty expressed in prudent strategic distribution of his sons.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 26:5 He set himself to seek God in the days of Zechariah , who instructed him in the fear of God , and as long as he sought the Lord , God made him prosper .

current.analysis_note: "Zechariah who instructed him in the fear of God" — bin transferred through instruction in fear of God; the teacher enables the student's understanding faculty

meaning.text: bin names the instruction in understanding — Zechariah's inner comprehension of the fear of God that he imparts.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 34:12 And the men did the work faithfully . Over them were set Jahath and Obadiah the Levites , of the sons of Merari , and Zechariah and Meshullam , of the sons of the Kohathites , to have oversight . The Levites , all who were skillful with instruments of music ,

current.analysis_note: "skillful with instruments of music" — bin as skilled competence; same use as 1Ch 25:7
current.notes: Musical skill as domain of cognitive discernment; borderline-retain

meaning.text: bin names skillfulness in music as an inner competence qualifying for temple service.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 35:3 And he said to the Levites who taught all Israel and who were holy to the Lord , “ Put the holy ark in the house that Solomon the son of David , king of Israel , built . You need not carry it on your shoulders . Now serve the Lord your God and his people Israel .

current.analysis_note: "Levites who taught all Israel" — bin as what the Levites enabled in the people through Torah-teaching

meaning.text: bin names the teaching role — those whose inner understanding of the law enables them to teach it.

verses.verse_text: Ezr 8:15 I gathered them to the river that runs to Ahava , and there we camped three days . As I reviewed the people and the priests , I found there none of the sons of Levi .

meaning.text: bin names the act of careful examination — reviewing with discerning attention. Set aside as no_inner_being — the span here is administrative review rather than inner perceptive faculty.

verses.verse_text: Ezr 8:16 Then I sent for Eliezer , Ariel , Shemaiah , Elnathan , Jarib , Elnathan , Nathan , Zechariah , and Meshullam , leading men , and for Joiarib and Elnathan , who were men of insight ,

current.analysis_note: "men of insight" — bin as the inner quality identifying certain leaders; used as a person-description

meaning.text: bin names insight as the defining inner quality of those Ezra sends — they are chosen for their perceptive understanding.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:2 So Ezra the priest brought the Law before the assembly , both men and women and all who could understand what they heard , on the first day of the seventh month .

current.analysis_note: "all who could understand what they heard" — bin as the capacity qualifying people to receive the Law reading

meaning.text: bin names the inner capacity to understand the Law as heard — those who have the faculty to receive and grasp what is read.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:3 And he read from it facing the square before the Water Gate from early morning until midday , in the presence of the men and the women and those who could understand . And the ears of all the people were attentive to the Book of the Law .

current.analysis_note: "those who could understand" — same; understanding as the qualifying inner capacity for Torah reception

meaning.text: bin names the same inner capacity — those able to receive and grasp the reading of the Law.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:7 Also Jeshua , Bani , Sherebiah , Jamin , Akkub , Shabbethai , Hodiah , Maaseiah , Kelita , Azariah , Jozabad , Hanan , Pelaiah , the Levites , helped the people to understand the Law , while the people remained in their places .

current.analysis_note: "helped the people to understand the Law" — bin enabled by the Levites' explanatory ministry

meaning.text: bin names the enabling of understanding — the Levites' inner comprehension being used to open the Law to others.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:8 They read from the book , from the Law of God , clearly , and they gave the sense , so that the people understood the reading .

current.analysis_note: "they gave the sense so that the people understood the reading" — bin activated by clear exposition; the inner faculty grasps meaning when the word is made clear

meaning.text: bin names the inner act of grasping — the people receiving and comprehending what was read and explained.

new_vcg.title: se.khel: discernment as personal inner quality producing good outcomes
new_vcg.description: A distinctive inner quality of practical wisdom — discernment, good sense, shrewd perception — that produces favourable outcomes and is recognisable in persons.

db.description: Term names the prayerful seeking and receiving of understanding from God — the inner posture of humility and petition before God for discernment

new.title: se.khel: discernment as personal inner quality producing good outcomes
new.description: A distinctive inner quality of practical wisdom — discernment, good sense, shrewd perception — that produces favourable outcomes and is recognisable in persons.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:9 And Nehemiah , who was the governor , and Ezra the priest and scribe , and the Levites who taught the people said to all the people , “This day is holy to the Lord your God ; do not mourn or weep .” For all the people wept as they heard the words of the Law .

current.analysis_note: "the Levites who taught the people" — bin as what teaching produces in the people

meaning.text: bin names teaching as the expression of inner understanding — those who comprehend the Law enabling others to receive it.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:12 And all the people went their way to eat and drink and to send portions and to make great rejoicing , because they had understood the words that were declared to them .

current.analysis_note: "they had understood the words that were declared to them" — bin achieved; the people's inner grasp of the word is the outcome; produces rejoicing

meaning.text: bin names the inner act of comprehending — the people's understanding of the words producing joy.

verses.verse_text: Neh 10:28 “The rest of the people , the priests , the Levites , the gatekeepers , the singers , the temple servants , and all who have separated themselves from the peoples of the lands to the Law of God , their wives , their sons , their daughters , all who have knowledge and understanding ,

current.analysis_note: "all who have knowledge and understanding" — bin as the qualifying inner capacity for covenant membership

meaning.text: bin names understanding as one of the defining inner capacities of those who separate themselves to covenant faithfulness.

verses.verse_text: Neh 13:7 and came to Jerusalem , and I then discovered the evil that Eliashib had done for Tobiah , preparing for him a chamber in the courts of the house of God .

current.analysis_note: "I discovered the evil that Eliashib had done" — bin as the act of perceiving and understanding a situation on arrival
current.notes: Re-evaluation v3_10: revised from 932-003 to 932-001 — Nehemiah is the subject discovering/perceiving the evil; bin names human discernment, not divine comprehension.

meaning.text: bin names the perceptive discovery — Nehemiah perceiving and grasping the wrongdoing on his arrival.

verses.verse_text: Job 6:24 “ Teach me , and I will be silent ; make me understand how I have gone astray .

current.analysis_note: "make me understand how I have gone astray" — petition for understanding directed toward self-comprehension before God

meaning.text: bin names the inner faculty Job asks to have activated — he wants the perceptive understanding of where he has erred. The posture is humble petition.

verses.verse_text: Job 6:30 Is there any injustice on my tongue ? Cannot my palate discern the cause of calamity ?

current.analysis_note: "cannot my palate discern the cause of calamity?" — bin as inner discernment applied to one's own situation

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty — Job claims his inner understanding can distinguish real injustice from supposed fault.

verses.verse_text: Job 9:11 Behold, he passes by me, and I see him not; he moves on , but I do not perceive him .

current.analysis_note: "he moves on but I do not perceive him" — Job's inability to perceive God; understanding failing before divine hiddenness

meaning.text: bin names the failed perceptive act — Job cannot discern God's passing. The faculty is present but the object is beyond it.

new_vcg.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new_vcg.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

db.description: Term names the failure, hardening, or absence of inner understanding — spiritual blindness, incapacity to perceive God or moral reality, and the rhetorical limits of human comprehension before God

new.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Job 11:11 For he knows worthless men ; when he sees iniquity , will he not consider it?

current.analysis_note: "he knows worthless men; when he sees iniquity, will he not consider it?" — God's understanding as moral perception of iniquity

meaning.text: bin names God's perceptive attending — he sees and considers iniquity. Divine understanding is thorough and attentive.

new_vcg.title: God's perceptive comprehension of the human person: bin / bi.nah as divine knowing
new_vcg.description: God's inner perceptive faculty directed toward the human person — his understanding of thoughts, deeds, plans, and the heart. Divine understanding of the human is comprehensive and intimate.

new.title: God's perceptive comprehension of the human person: bin / bi.nah as divine knowing
new.description: God's inner perceptive faculty directed toward the human person — his understanding of thoughts, deeds, plans, and the heart. Divine understanding of the human is comprehensive and intimate.

verses.verse_text: Job 13:1 “Behold, my eye has seen all this, my ear has heard and understood it .

current.analysis_note: "my eye has seen all this, my ear has heard and understood it" — this is bin achieved, not failed. **Reading: this is a positive use — Job claims to have understood what the friends said.** May be misassigned to group 1363. **Flag: vr=27737 may belong in B-1.**

meaning.text: bin names the inner act of grasping what is heard — understanding as the reception and comprehension of what the ear receives.

verses.verse_text: Job 14:21 His sons come to honor , and he does not know it; they are brought low , and he perceives it not .

current.analysis_note: "he perceives it not" — the dead man cannot perceive honour or shame; bin absent in death

meaning.text: bin names the failed perceptive faculty of the dead — the person beyond death cannot perceive what happens to those they loved.

new_vcg.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new_vcg.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

db.description: Term names the failure, hardening, or absence of inner understanding — spiritual blindness, incapacity to perceive God or moral reality, and the rhetorical limits of human comprehension before God

new.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:9 What do you know that we do not know ? What do you understand that is not clear to us ?

current.analysis_note: "what do you understand that is not clear to us?" — bin as the inner capacity that grasps meaning; Eliphaz challenging Job

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive capacity that Eliphaz claims equals or surpasses Job's — a challenge about the reach of Job's understanding.

verses.verse_text: Job 18:2 “How long will you hunt for words ? Consider , and then we will speak .

current.analysis_note: "consider, and then we will speak" — directive to Bildad to exercise bin before speaking; not failure but a call for understanding

meaning.text: bin names the reflective perceptive act — Bildad calls Job to think carefully before speaking.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:5 I would know what he would answer me and understand what he would say to me .

current.analysis_note: "I would understand what he would say to me" — understanding directed toward grasping God's word

meaning.text: bin names the inner faculty Job wants to exercise in response to God — the desire to comprehend what God would answer.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:8 “ Behold , I go forward , but he is not there, and backward , but I do not perceive him ;

current.analysis_note: "backward, but I do not perceive him" — Job unable to perceive God in any direction; understanding fails before divine presence

meaning.text: bin names the failed perceptive act — Job cannot locate God by going forward or backward. The faculty cannot reach its object.

new_vcg.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new_vcg.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

new.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:15 Therefore I am terrified at his presence ; when I consider , I am in dread of him .

current.analysis_note: "when I consider, I am in dread of him" — bin achieved (consideration) but producing terror; the faculty functions but the object overwhelms

meaning.text: bin names the reflective perceptive act — contemplating God produces dread. Understanding and terror are linked.

verses.verse_text: Job 26:14 Behold , these are but the outskirts of his ways , and how small a whisper do we hear of him! But the thunder of his power who can understand ?”

current.analysis_note: "the thunder of his power, who can understand?" — the limits of understanding before divine power; rhetorical question about the reach of the faculty

meaning.text: bin names the inner faculty confronted with its limits — divine power exceeds human understanding.

new_vcg.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new_vcg.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

new.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Job 28:23 “ God understands the way to it, and he knows its place .

current.analysis_note: "God understands the way to it, and he knows its place" — God's bin grasps where wisdom is located; divine comprehension of wisdom's location

meaning.text: bin names God's inner comprehension of the location of wisdom and understanding — he alone knows where it is found.

new_vcg.title: Understanding gained through seeking and engagement with the word and covenant
new_vcg.description: Understanding as the fruit of deliberate orientation toward God — seeking, keeping the precepts, holding to the covenant. The faculty grows and deepens through the person's active engagement.

new.description: Understanding as the fruit of deliberate orientation toward God — seeking, keeping the precepts, holding to the covenant. The faculty grows and deepens through the person's active engagement.

verses.verse_text: Job 30:20 I cry to you for help and you do not answer me; I stand , and you only look at me .

current.analysis_note: "I stand, and you only look at me" — Job's address to God; the perception is God's but the affect is non-response; God sees but appears not to act

meaning.text: bin names God's attending — he looks at Job without answering. The faculty is present but not responsive. A difficult use — closer to "look at" than to comprehend.

new_vcg.title: Directional attending: bin as perceptive attention directed toward an object
new_vcg.description: Bin used in the sense of directing perceptive attention toward something — looking closely, examining carefully, paying attention. Distinct from understanding as comprehension. The faculty is directed attention, not cognitive grasping.
new_vcg.verse_annotation: God's attending without responding — difficult use

db.description: Term names God's inner comprehension of the human person — God's perception, understanding, and attending to the heart, thought, and deed

new.title: Directional attending: bin as perceptive attention directed toward an object

verses.verse_text: Job 31:1 “I have made a covenant with my eyes ; how then could I gaze at a virgin ?

current.analysis_note: "how then could I gaze at a virgin?" — bin as the inner perception Job exercises by looking; he made a covenant with his eyes because bin enables perception

meaning.text: bin names the directed perceptive act — the gaze that would violate the covenant of the eyes. The inner faculty of attention directed toward a forbidden object.

new_vcg.title: Directional attending: bin as perceptive attention directed toward an object
new_vcg.description: Bin used in the sense of directing perceptive attention toward something — looking closely, examining carefully, paying attention. Distinct from understanding as comprehension. The faculty is directed attention, not cognitive grasping.
new_vcg.verse_annotation: directed perceptive attention that would violate the covenant of the eyes

db.description: bin as the inner perceptive faculty in its constituted form — the quality of the wise, discerning person. The faculty is seated in the heart (1Ki 3:9, Psa 49:20), enables right judgment (1Ki 3:9,11), receives instruction (Pro 9:8), perceives situations rightly (2Sa 12:19, Job 6:30), and is the inner capacity from which wise conduct flows. It is morally conditioned — it enables covenant-keeping (Deu 4:6) and is blocked by the absence of the fear of the Lord.

new.title: Directional attending: bin as perceptive attention directed toward an object

verses.verse_text: Job 32:8 But it is the spirit in man , the breath of the Almighty , that makes him understand.

current.analysis_note: "the breath of the Almighty makes him understand" — bin activated by the divine breath; the inner faculty is Spirit-enabled

meaning.text: bin names understanding as what the breath of God produces in the person — the Spirit as the source of inner comprehension.

new_vcg.title: Spirit-given understanding: bin / bi.nah / te.vu.nah as a divine endowment
new_vcg.description: Understanding as explicitly God-given — the inner capacity placed within the person by God or the Spirit, not acquired through human effort. Includes craft intelligence for sacred work and the Messiah's Spirit-given understanding.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:9 It is not the old who are wise , nor the aged who understand what is right .

current.analysis_note: "the aged do not understand what is right" — bin as the inner faculty that perceives moral truth; age alone does not guarantee it

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty for moral discernment — understanding what is right is not a function of age but of something else (implied: the Spirit).

verses.verse_text: Job 32:12 I gave you my attention , and, behold , there was none among you who refuted Job or who answered his words .

current.analysis_note: "none among you who refuted Job" — bin as the attention given; here none among the friends succeeded in rebutting Job

meaning.text: bin names the act of attentive perceptive listening — the inner faculty directed toward what others say.

verses.verse_text: Job 36:29 Can anyone understand the spreading of the clouds , the thunderings of his pavilion ?

current.analysis_note: "can anyone understand the spreading of the clouds?" — rhetorical; understanding cannot reach divine natural phenomena

meaning.text: bin names the inner faculty confronted with natural phenomena beyond human comprehension — the limits of understanding before divine works.

new_vcg.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new_vcg.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

db.description: Term names the failure, hardening, or absence of inner understanding — spiritual blindness, incapacity to perceive God or moral reality, and the rhetorical limits of human comprehension before God

new.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:14 “ Hear this, O Job ; stop and consider the wondrous works of God .

current.analysis_note: "consider the wondrous works of God" — directive to bin; the wondrous works exceed understanding

meaning.text: bin names the reflective perceptive act directed toward divine activity — the call to use the inner faculty of understanding to attend to God's works.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:18 Have you comprehended the expanse of the earth ? Declare , if you know all this.

current.analysis_note: "have you comprehended the expanse of the earth?" — God challenging Job; bin insufficient to grasp creation's extent

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty confronted with cosmic scale — comprehending the earth's expanse is beyond human reach.

new_vcg.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new_vcg.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:20 that you may take it to its territory and that you may discern the paths to its home ?

current.analysis_note: "that you may discern the paths to its home?" — God's ironic challenge; Job cannot discern what God alone knows

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty God challenges Job to exercise — discerning the paths of light and darkness is beyond human capacity.

verses.verse_text: Job 42:3 ‘ Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge ?’ Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand , things too wonderful for me, which I did not know .

current.analysis_note: "I have uttered what I did not understand, things too wonderful for me" — Job's confession; understanding had been exceeded by what he spoke

meaning.text: bin names Job's recognition that his inner faculty of understanding was insufficient for what he was speaking about — self-acknowledged failure of comprehension.

verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster : for the flutes . A Psalm of David . Psa 5:1 Give ear to my words , O Lord ; consider my groaning .

current.analysis_note: "consider my groaning" — petition for God to exercise bin toward the psalmist's prayer; God is the subject of the requested understanding

meaning.text: bin names God's attentive perceptive attending — the psalmist asks God to consider, to perceive and attend to, his inner groaning.

new_vcg.title: God's perceptive comprehension of the human person: bin / bi.nah as divine knowing
new_vcg.description: God's inner perceptive faculty directed toward the human person — his understanding of thoughts, deeds, plans, and the heart. Divine understanding of the human is comprehensive and intimate.
new_vcg.verse_annotation: the psalmist petitions for divine perceptive attending

db.description: Term names God's inner comprehension of the human person — God's perception, understanding, and attending to the heart, thought, and deed

new.title: God's perceptive comprehension of the human person: bin / bi.nah as divine knowing
new.description: God's inner perceptive faculty directed toward the human person — his understanding of thoughts, deeds, plans, and the heart. Divine understanding of the human is comprehensive and intimate.

verses.verse_text: Psa 19:12 Who can discern his errors ? Declare me innocent from hidden faults.

current.analysis_note: "who can discern his errors?" — bin insufficient for full self-knowledge; hidden faults beyond inner perception

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty directed toward self — the question expresses the limits of self-understanding regarding hidden faults.

verses.verse_text: Psa 28:5 Because they do not regard the works of the Lord or the work of his hands , he will tear them down and build them up no more.

current.analysis_note: "they do not regard the works of the Lord" — bin withheld from those who ignore God's works

meaning.text: bin names the failure to perceive and attend to — not regarding is the absence of the inner faculty applied to God's works.

verses.verse_text: Psa 32:9 Be not like a horse or a mule , without understanding , which must be curbed with bit and bridle , or it will not stay near you .

current.analysis_note: "be not like a mule without understanding" — bin as the inner quality distinguishing rational persons from animals

meaning.text: bin names understanding as the faculty that distinguishes the human person from an animal — it enables voluntary, responsive following rather than coercion.

new_vcg.title: bin / bi.nah as constituted inner perceptive faculty: understanding governing speech, relationships, and perception
new_vcg.description: Understanding as a substantive inner quality of the person — what they are characterised by. The faculty is active, receptive, and productive: it governs what the wise person perceives, how they speak, and how they relate. The person of understanding is defined by this quality.

meaning.text: bin names God's perceptive observing — his inner comprehension of human deeds as well as hearts.

current.analysis_note: "though you look carefully at his place, he will not be there" — bin exerted but the wicked is not found

meaning.text: bin names the careful perceptive looking — the faculty of attentive discernment applied to searching for the wicked.

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty whose absence equates the human person to animals — without understanding, status and pomp count for nothing.

verses.verse_text: Psa 50:22 “ Mark this , then , you who forget God , lest I tear you apart , and there be none to deliver !

current.analysis_note: "mark this, then, you who forget God" — directive to understanding; the forgetting-God is the failure being addressed

meaning.text: bin names the perceptive attending being commanded — to mark is to perceive and take heed.

verses.verse_text: Psa 58:9 Sooner than your pots can feel the heat of thorns , whether green or ablaze , may he sweep them away !
Psa 73:17 until I went into the sanctuary of God ; then I discerned their end .

current.analysis_note: "until I went into the sanctuary of God; then I discerned their end" — understanding achieved only in the sanctuary; prior to that, bin had failed

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive act enabled by the sanctuary encounter — the psalmist could not understand the prosperity of the wicked until God gave him perception of their end.

verses.verse_text: Psa 82:5 They have neither knowledge nor understanding , they walk about in darkness ; all the foundations of the earth are shaken .

current.analysis_note: "neither knowledge nor understanding, they walk in darkness" — bin absent = walking in darkness

meaning.text: bin names the absence of the inner perceptive faculty — without understanding, the result is moral and directional darkness.

new_vcg.title: Failed or absent understanding: bin as the person who cannot perceive
new_vcg.description: Understanding absent in persons or groups — whether through their own failure, the consequence of wickedness, the absence of the divine gift, or Israel's characteristic blindness.

current.analysis_note: "the fool cannot understand this" — bin blocked in the fool

meaning.text: bin names the perceptive faculty the fool lacks — the capacity to perceive the significance of God's acts.

verses.verse_text: Psa 94:7 and they say , “The Lord does not see ; the God of Jacob does not perceive .”

current.analysis_note: "the God of Jacob does not perceive" — the wicked's false claim; bin denied of God as a statement of unbelief

meaning.text: bin names the perceptive faculty being denied to God by the wicked — their claim that God does not perceive their actions.

verses.verse_text: Psa 94:8 Understand , O dullest of the people ! Fools , when will you be wise ?

current.analysis_note: "Understand, O dullest of the people! Fools, when will you be wise?" — directive to the fool to exercise bin; their current failure named

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty being commanded — the dull are called to exercise the understanding they refuse.

new_vcg.title: sa.khal as perceptive prudent discernment: seeing clearly what is before you
new_vcg.description: The inner capacity for perceptive attending — seeing God's acts, situations, or conditions with clarity and wisdom. The prudent person considers, ponders, observes rightly where others miss or fail to attend.

current.analysis_note: "let them consider the steadfast love of the Lord" — bin directed toward reflecting on God's deeds

meaning.text: bin names the perceptive reflective act directed toward God's love — the wise person uses their inner faculty to perceive and attend to what God has done.

current.analysis_note: "make me understand the way of your precepts" — petition for understanding of Torah; bin as the faculty that grasps God's law

meaning.text: bin names the inner faculty the psalmist asks God to open — petitionary seeking of understanding for the precepts.

new_vcg.title: Meditative dwelling on God's law, precepts, and word: si.ach as law-meditation
new_vcg.description: The sustained inward dwelling on God's law, precepts, statutes, and promises — the defining meditative practice of the devoted person. The object is always divine instruction. All uses from Psalm 119.

current.analysis_note: "give me understanding that I may keep your law" — bin as the precondition for covenant obedience; understanding enables keeping

meaning.text: bin names understanding as what enables covenant keeping — sought from God, enabling the whole-heart observance.

current.analysis_note: "give me understanding that I may learn your commandments" — same pattern; bin as petition, law as object

meaning.text: bin names understanding as what enables learning — petitioned from God, making instruction receivable.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:95 The wicked lie in wait to destroy me, but I consider your testimonies .

current.analysis_note: "I consider your testimonies" — bin as the active contemplation of God's word

meaning.text: bin names the reflective perceptive act directed toward God's testimonies — while the wicked wait to destroy, the psalmist's inner faculty is on the testimonies.

new_vcg.title: Understanding gained through seeking and engagement with the word and covenant

current.analysis_note: "I understand more than the aged, for I keep your precepts" — bin produced by covenant obedience; understanding exceeds age-wisdom when grounded in Torah

meaning.text: bin names the inner understanding produced by keeping the precepts — covenant faithfulness produces perceptive understanding.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:104 Through your precepts I get understanding ; therefore I hate every false way .

current.analysis_note: "through your precepts I get understanding" — the precepts are the source of bin; understanding received through divine word

meaning.text: bin names understanding as the fruit of the precepts — received through engagement with God's word.

current.analysis_note: "give me understanding that I may know your testimonies" — petition pattern; bin directed toward knowing

meaning.text: bin names the inner faculty sought from God — without it the testimonies cannot be truly known.

current.analysis_note: "the unfolding of your words gives light; it imparts understanding to the simple" — divine word as the source of bin; understanding as what the simple receive through the unfolding of the word

meaning.text: bin names understanding as what God's word imparts — the inner perceptive faculty opened by the word, given even to the simple.

new.description: God's inner perceptive faculty directed toward the human person — his understanding of thoughts, deeds, plans, and the heart. Divine understanding of the human is comprehensive and intimate.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:2 To know wisdom and instruction , to understand words of insight ,

current.analysis_note: "to understand words of insight" — bin as the goal of Proverbs' teaching; the faculty that receives wisdom-speech

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive act that the book of Proverbs aims to produce — grasping insight as the goal of the collection.

new_vcg.title: Understanding gained through seeking and engagement with the word and covenant
new_vcg.description: Understanding as the fruit of deliberate orientation toward God — seeking, keeping the precepts, holding to the covenant. The faculty grows and deepens through the person's active engagement.

db.description: bin as the inner perceptive faculty in its constituted form — the quality of the wise, discerning person. The faculty is seated in the heart (1Ki 3:9, Psa 49:20), enables right judgment (1Ki 3:9,11), receives instruction (Pro 9:8), perceives situations rightly (2Sa 12:19, Job 6:30), and is the inner capacity from which wise conduct flows. It is morally conditioned — it enables covenant-keeping (Deu 4:6) and is blocked by the absence of the fear of the Lord.

new.title: Understanding gained through seeking and engagement with the word and covenant
new.description: Understanding as the fruit of deliberate orientation toward God — seeking, keeping the precepts, holding to the covenant. The faculty grows and deepens through the person's active engagement.

current.analysis_note: "the one who understands obtains guidance" — bin as what enables the reception of guidance

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty that makes the person receptive to guidance — understanding is the condition for receiving.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:13 On the lips of him who has understanding , wisdom is found , but a rod is for the back of him who lacks sense .

new_vcg.title: bin / bi.nah as constituted inner perceptive faculty: understanding governing speech, relationships, and perception
new_vcg.description: Understanding as a substantive inner quality of the person — what they are characterised by. The faculty is active, receptive, and productive: it governs what the wise person perceives, how they speak, and how they relate. The person of understanding is defined by this quality.

verses.verse_text: Pro 20:24 A man’s steps are from the Lord ; how then can man understand his way ?

current.analysis_note: "how can man understand his way?" — bin insufficient for ultimate self-comprehension

meaning.text: bin names the inner faculty confronted with its own limits — human steps are from the Lord; the person cannot fully understand their own path.

new_vcg.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new_vcg.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

meaning.text: bin names the reflective perceptive attending to one's own conduct — the inner faculty used for self-examination and direction.

verses.verse_text: Pro 23:1 When you sit down to eat with a ruler , observe carefully what is before you,

current.analysis_note: "observe carefully what is before you" — bin as perceptive attention in a social situation

meaning.text: bin names the careful perceptive attending to one's social situation — wisdom in a powerful person's presence.

new_vcg.title: Directional attending: bin as perceptive attention directed toward an object
new_vcg.description: Bin used in the sense of directing perceptive attention toward something — looking closely, examining carefully, paying attention. Distinct from understanding as comprehension. The faculty is directed attention, not cognitive grasping.

new.title: Directional attending: bin as perceptive attention directed toward an object

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:12 If you say , “ Behold , we did not know this ,” does not he who weighs the heart perceive it? Does not he who keeps watch over your soul know it, and will he not repay man according to his work ?

current.analysis_note: B-3 | God's bin (B-3) and the question directed to God (B-1 context)

meaning.text: bin names God's perceptive comprehension of the heart — he perceives what the person claims not to know.

new_vcg.title: God's perceptive comprehension of the human person: bin / bi.nah as divine knowing
new_vcg.description: God's inner perceptive faculty directed toward the human person — his understanding of thoughts, deeds, plans, and the heart. Divine understanding of the human is comprehensive and intimate.

db.description: Term names understanding as the inner reception and grasp of revealed truth — the capacity to perceive insight into mystery, to receive what God gives, and to resist what God thwarts

new.title: God's perceptive comprehension of the human person: bin / bi.nah as divine knowing
new.description: God's inner perceptive faculty directed toward the human person — his understanding of thoughts, deeds, plans, and the heart. Divine understanding of the human is comprehensive and intimate.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:2 When a land transgresses , it has many rulers , but with a man of understanding and knowledge , its stability will long continue .

current.analysis_note: "with a man of understanding and knowledge, its stability will long continue" — bin as the quality of the ruler that produces national stability

meaning.text: bin names understanding as the inner quality that produces stable governance — the person of understanding sustains what others cannot.

new_vcg.title: bin / bi.nah as constituted inner perceptive faculty: understanding governing speech, relationships, and perception
new_vcg.description: Understanding as a substantive inner quality of the person — what they are characterised by. The faculty is active, receptive, and productive: it governs what the wise person perceives, how they speak, and how they relate. The person of understanding is defined by this quality.

db.description: bin as the inner perceptive faculty in its constituted form — the quality of the wise, discerning person. The faculty is seated in the heart (1Ki 3:9, Psa 49:20), enables right judgment (1Ki 3:9,11), receives instruction (Pro 9:8), perceives situations rightly (2Sa 12:19, Job 6:30), and is the inner capacity from which wise conduct flows. It is morally conditioned — it enables covenant-keeping (Deu 4:6) and is blocked by the absence of the fear of the Lord.

new.title: bin / bi.nah as constituted inner perceptive faculty: understanding governing speech, relationships, and perception
new.description: Understanding as a substantive inner quality of the person — what they are characterised by. The faculty is active, receptive, and productive: it governs what the wise person perceives, how they speak, and how they relate. The person of understanding is defined by this quality.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:5 Evil men do not understand justice , but those who seek the Lord understand it completely .

current.analysis_note: "those who seek the Lord understand it completely" — bin as the faculty that grasps justice when oriented to God

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty opened by seeking God — those who seek the Lord understand justice in full.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as moral knowledge guiding right action
new_vcg.description: The inner moral knowing that directs the righteous person — knowing what is acceptable, what is just, what is right. Its absence names moral blindness; its presence names moral clarity.

new.title: ya.da as moral knowledge guiding right action
new.description: The inner moral knowing that directs the righteous person — knowing what is acceptable, what is just, what is right. Its absence names moral blindness; its presence names moral clarity.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:7 The one who keeps the law is a son with understanding , but a companion of gluttons shames his father .

current.analysis_note: "the one who keeps the law is a son with understanding" — bin produced by law-keeping

meaning.text: bin names understanding as what covenant-keeping constitutes — the law-keeper is a son of understanding.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:11 A rich man is wise in his own eyes , but a poor man who has understanding will find him out .

current.analysis_note: "a poor man who has understanding will find him out" — bin as the inner faculty that perceives what wealth conceals

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty of the poor person that enables them to see through the rich man's pretension.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:7 A righteous man knows the rights of the poor ; a wicked man does not understand such knowledge .

current.analysis_note: "a righteous man knows the rights of the poor; a wicked man does not understand" — bin enabling moral perception of the vulnerable

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty blocked by wickedness — the wicked person cannot perceive the rights of the poor.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:19 By mere words a servant is not disciplined , for though he understands , he will not respond .

current.analysis_note: "though he understands, he will not respond" — bin present but not acted on; gap between understanding and response

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty present but unproductive — the servant comprehends but will not comply. Understanding without response.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:11 Again I saw that under the sun the race is not to the swift , nor the battle to the strong , nor bread to the wise , nor riches to the intelligent , nor favor to those with knowledge , but time and chance happen to them all .

current.analysis_note: "neither riches to the intelligent" — bin as personal quality that does not guarantee outcomes

meaning.text: bin names intelligence as an inner quality that does not guarantee material outcome — time and chance equalise.

verses.verse_text: Isa 1:3 The ox knows its owner , and the donkey its master’s crib , but Israel does not know , my people do not understand .”

current.analysis_note: "my people do not understand" — Israel's covenantal failure expressed as absence of bin

meaning.text: bin names the absent inner perceptive faculty of Israel — the ox and donkey recognise their master, but Israel does not perceive God.

new_vcg.title: Failed or absent understanding: bin as the person who cannot perceive
new_vcg.description: Understanding absent in persons or groups — whether through their own failure, the consequence of wickedness, the absence of the divine gift, or Israel's characteristic blindness.

db.description: Term names the failure, hardening, or absence of inner understanding — spiritual blindness, incapacity to perceive God or moral reality, and the rhetorical limits of human comprehension before God

new.title: Failed or absent understanding: bin as the person who cannot perceive
new.description: Understanding absent in persons or groups — whether through their own failure, the consequence of wickedness, the absence of the divine gift, or Israel's characteristic blindness.

verses.verse_text: Isa 3:3 the captain of fifty and the man of rank , the counselor and the skillful magician and the expert in charms .

current.analysis_note: "expert in charms" — bin as technical expertise/competence; a specialist inner capacity
current.notes: Expert in charms — technical expertise as a domain of cognitive discernment capacity; borderline-retain per §3.3

meaning.text: bin names expertise — the specialised inner knowledge and perceptive skill qualifying the person for a particular role.

new_vcg.title: Human counsel: wisdom given between persons shaping deliberation
new_vcg.description: One person gives advice or counsel to another — the inner wisdom of the advisor expressed and offered to shape the recipient's purposes. The outcome depends on whether counsel is received or rejected.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:21 Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes , and shrewd in their own sight !

current.analysis_note: "shrewd in their own sight" — self-attributed understanding as false; bin claimed but inverted

meaning.text: bin names self-assessed shrewdness — the inner perceptive faculty claimed as self-sourced rather than received.

verses.verse_text: Isa 6:9 And he said , “ Go , and say to this people : “‘Keep on hearing , but do not understand ; keep on seeing , but do not perceive .’

current.analysis_note: "keep on hearing but do not understand" — the judicial instruction; understanding withheld

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty that will not be activated — a judicial withholding of understanding from those who will not receive.

new_vcg.title: Judicial withholding of understanding: suniēmi / bin as blocked comprehension
new_vcg.description: Understanding deliberately withheld by God — the inner faculty is present but its activation is prevented by divine judgment. Hearing without receiving; perception without comprehension.

db.description: Term names the failure, hardening, or absence of inner understanding — spiritual blindness, incapacity to perceive God or moral reality, and the rhetorical limits of human comprehension before God

new.title: Judicial withholding of understanding: suniēmi / bin as blocked comprehension
new.description: Understanding deliberately withheld by God — the inner faculty is present but its activation is prevented by divine judgment. Hearing without receiving; perception without comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Isa 6:10 Make the heart of this people dull , and their ears heavy , and blind their eyes ; lest they see with their eyes , and hear with their ears , and understand with their hearts , and turn and be healed .”

current.analysis_note: "make the heart dull... lest they understand with their hearts and turn"

meaning.text: bin names the perceptive reception of divine truth in the heart — the faculty whose activation would lead to turning and healing. God's judgment holds it closed.

verses.verse_text: Isa 10:13 For he says : “ By the strength of my hand I have done it, and by my wisdom , for I have understanding ; I remove the boundaries of peoples , and plunder their treasures; like a bull I bring down those who sit on thrones.

current.analysis_note: "by my wisdom, for I have understanding" — Assyria's arrogant self-claim; bin as the boast of the oppressor

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty that the Assyrian king claims as his own — self-attributed understanding as the ground of his arrogance.

verses.verse_text: Isa 14:16 Those who see you will stare at you and ponder over you: ‘Is this the man who made the earth tremble , who shook kingdoms ,

current.analysis_note: "ponder over you" — bin as the act of reflective comprehension

meaning.text: bin names the reflective perceptive act of those who see the fallen tyrant — they stare and try to grasp how this could have happened.

verses.verse_text: Isa 28:9 “To whom will he teach knowledge , and to whom will he explain the message ? Those who are weaned from the milk , those taken from the breast ?

current.analysis_note: "to whom will he explain the message?" — bin as what the audience cannot be assumed to have; rhetorical

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty needed to receive explanation — the question implies the audience lacks the capacity to receive.

new_vcg.title: de.ah as the knowledge of the Lord filling the earth: eschatological covenantal knowing
new_vcg.description: Knowledge of the Lord as the content of the eschatological promise — universal, filling, covering as the waters cover the sea.

db.description: Term names the failure, hardening, or absence of inner understanding — spiritual blindness, incapacity to perceive God or moral reality, and the rhetorical limits of human comprehension before God

new.title: de.ah as the knowledge of the Lord filling the earth: eschatological covenantal knowing
new.description: Knowledge of the Lord as the content of the eschatological promise — universal, filling, covering as the waters cover the sea.

verses.verse_text: Isa 28:19 As often as it passes through it will take you; for morning by morning it will pass through , by day and by night ; and it will be sheer terror to understand the message .

current.analysis_note: "it will be sheer terror to understand the message" — bin achieved but the content is devastating

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive act of grasping divine judgment — understanding the message is not comfort but terror. The faculty here receives something overwhelming.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:14 therefore , behold , I will again do wonderful things with this people , with wonder upon wonder ; and the wisdom of their wise men shall perish , and the discernment of their discerning men shall be hidden .”

current.analysis_note: "the discernment of their discerning men shall be hidden" — divine removal of understanding from the wise class

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty of the discerning — what God will hide is not the faculty but its operation.

new.description: Understanding deliberately withheld by God — the inner faculty is present but its activation is prevented by divine judgment. Hearing without receiving; perception without comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:16 You turn things upside down ! Shall the potter be regarded as the clay , that the thing made should say of its maker , “He did not make me”; or the thing formed say of him who formed it, “He has no understanding ”?

current.analysis_note: "the thing formed say... He has no understanding" — creation's absurd denial of the Creator's understanding

meaning.text: bin names understanding attributed or denied to the Creator — the absurdity of the created thing judging its maker's inner comprehension.

new_vcg.title: Evaluative regard of persons and things: assigning worth or category by reckoning
new_vcg.description: One person (human or divine) evaluates another person, group, or thing by reckoning — assigning them a worth, category, or status. The reckoning is relational and evaluative, not judicial-moral as in VCG-D-01. Includes both positive and negative assessments, and both legitimate and contested evaluations.

db.description: Term names the failure, hardening, or absence of inner understanding — spiritual blindness, incapacity to perceive God or moral reality, and the rhetorical limits of human comprehension before God

verses.verse_text: Isa 32:4 The heart of the hasty will understand and know , and the tongue of the stammerers will hasten to speak distinctly .

current.analysis_note: "the heart of the hasty will understand" — future restoration of bin to those who currently lack it

meaning.text: bin names understanding as what the heart of the hasty will attain in the coming renewal — the inner perceptive faculty opened in a future restored state.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:14 Whom did he consult , and who made him understand ? Who taught him the path of justice , and taught him knowledge , and showed him the way of understanding ?

current.analysis_note: — | Dual: rhetorical (who taught God? = B-3); but also about understanding in creation (B-2)

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty no one gave God — the rhetorical question asserts God's self-sufficient understanding.

new_vcg.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new_vcg.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.
new_vcg.verse_annotation: God's understanding is self-originating, beyond human teaching

db.description: Term names understanding as the inner reception and grasp of revealed truth — the capacity to perceive insight into mystery, to receive what God gives, and to resist what God thwarts

new.title: Limits of human understanding before divine mystery: bin / bi.nah facing what cannot be grasped
new.description: The person exercises the inner perceptive faculty but reaches its boundary — divine works, cosmic scale, the mystery of God's ways are simply beyond human comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:21 Do you not know ? Do you not hear ? Has it not been told you from the beginning ? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth ?

current.analysis_note: "have you not understood from the foundations of the earth?" — bin as what should have been grasped from creation's witness

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive reception of what has been available from the beginning — the question challenges those who act as if they do not know.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as limits of human knowing before divine mystery
new_vcg.description: Human ya.da encountering its own limits before God's work, purposes, and nature — cannot comprehend, cannot find, cannot know the end or the route.

db.description: Term names the prayerful seeking and receiving of understanding from God — the inner posture of humility and petition before God for discernment

new.title: ya.da as limits of human knowing before divine mystery
new.description: Human ya.da encountering its own limits before God's work, purposes, and nature — cannot comprehend, cannot find, cannot know the end or the route.

verses.verse_text: Isa 43:10 “ You are my witnesses ,” declares the Lord , “and my servant whom I have chosen , that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed , nor shall there be any after me.

current.analysis_note: "understand that I am he" — bin directed toward divine identity; grasping the self-revelation of God

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive reception of God's identity — understanding that God is who he is, as the goal of Israel's witness role.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as the "then you shall know" pattern: divine self-disclosure through event producing recognition
new_vcg.description: A repeated pattern across the OT: God acts, and the purpose of the action is that persons will know — know that he is the Lord, that he is present, that he sent his messenger. The knowing is produced by the event, not by the seeker.

db.description: Term names the prayerful seeking and receiving of understanding from God — the inner posture of humility and petition before God for discernment

new.title: ya.da as the "then you shall know" pattern: divine self-disclosure through event producing recognition
new.description: A repeated pattern across the OT: God acts, and the purpose of the action is that persons will know — know that he is the Lord, that he is present, that he sent his messenger. The knowing is produced by the event, not by the seeker.

verses.verse_text: Isa 43:18 “ Remember not the former things , nor consider the things of old .

current.analysis_note: "remember not the former things, nor consider the things of old" — bin as considering; here redirected from old to new acts of God

meaning.text: bin names the reflective perceptive attending that God here redirects — do not fix your inner attention on the former things.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:18 They know not , nor do they discern , for he has shut their eyes , so that they cannot see , and their hearts , so that they cannot understand .

current.analysis_note: "their hearts, so that they cannot understand" — bin closed at the heart; idolaters cannot perceive the absurdity of their worship

meaning.text: bin names the failed inner perceptive faculty of idol-worshippers — God has shut their eyes and hearts.

new_vcg.title: sa.khal as perceptive prudent discernment: seeing clearly what is before you
new_vcg.description: The inner capacity for perceptive attending — seeing God's acts, situations, or conditions with clarity and wisdom. The prudent person considers, ponders, observes rightly where others miss or fail to attend.

db.description: Term names the failure, hardening, or absence of inner understanding — spiritual blindness, incapacity to perceive God or moral reality, and the rhetorical limits of human comprehension before God

new.title: sa.khal as perceptive prudent discernment: seeing clearly what is before you
new.description: The inner capacity for perceptive attending — seeing God's acts, situations, or conditions with clarity and wisdom. The prudent person considers, ponders, observes rightly where others miss or fail to attend.

verses.verse_text: Isa 52:15 so shall he sprinkle many nations ; kings shall shut their mouths because of him, for that which has not been told them they see , and that which they have not heard they understand .

current.analysis_note: "that which they have not heard they understand" — bin grasping what has not yet been verbally announced; understanding the Servant's work

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive reception of new revelation — nations who have never heard will understand when the Servant is revealed.

verses.verse_text: Isa 56:11 The dogs have a mighty appetite ; they never have enough . But they are shepherds who have no understanding ; they have all turned to their own way , each to his own gain , one and all .

current.analysis_note: "shepherds who have no understanding" — bin absent in those who should lead

meaning.text: bin names the absent inner perceptive faculty of the false shepherds — they cannot perceive beyond their own appetite.

new_vcg.description: Understanding absent in persons or groups — whether through their own failure, the consequence of wickedness, the absence of the divine gift, or Israel's characteristic blindness.

verses.verse_text: Isa 57:1 The righteous man perishes , and no one lays it to heart ; devout men are taken away , while no one understands . For the righteous man is taken away from calamity ;

current.analysis_note: "while no one understands" — bin collectively absent; no one grasps the significance of the righteous man's death

meaning.text: bin names the failed perceptive faculty of the community — no one perceives that the righteous person's death is a mercy.

verses.verse_text: Jer 2:10 For cross to the coasts of Cyprus and see , or send to Kedar and examine with care ; see if there has been such a thing .

current.analysis_note: "examine with care; see if there has been such a thing" — directive to exercise bin; the verse is a call to comparative perception

meaning.text: bin names the careful perceptive examination being commanded — attentive discernment applied to historical comparison.

new_vcg.title: Directional attending: bin as perceptive attention directed toward an object
new_vcg.description: Bin used in the sense of directing perceptive attention toward something — looking closely, examining carefully, paying attention. Distinct from understanding as comprehension. The faculty is directed attention, not cognitive grasping.

verses.verse_text: Jer 4:22 “ For my people are foolish ; they know me not ; they are stupid children ; they have no understanding . They are ‘ wise ’—in doing evil ! But how to do good they know not .”

current.analysis_note: "they have no understanding... wise in doing evil" — absence of understanding for good; wisdom-faculty serving evil

meaning.text: bin names the absent inner perceptive faculty of Israel — they are skilled in evil but cannot perceive how to do good.

new_vcg.description: Understanding absent in persons or groups — whether through their own failure, the consequence of wickedness, the absence of the divine gift, or Israel's characteristic blindness.

verses.verse_text: Jer 9:12 Who is the man so wise that he can understand this ? To whom has the mouth of the Lord spoken , that he may declare it? Why is the land ruined and laid waste like a wilderness , so that no one passes through ?

current.analysis_note: "who is the man so wise that he can understand this?" — bin as what is required to grasp the judgment; no one has it

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive faculty needed to grasp divine judgment — the question implies it is absent.

current.analysis_note: "Consider, and call for the mourning women" — bin as careful perception in a complex situation

meaning.text: bin names the reflective perceptive act being commanded — attend to the situation and act accordingly.

new_vcg.title: Craft skill applied to secular or non-sacred purposes: cha.kham and cha.shav in craft outside the sanctuary
new_vcg.description: The same inner capacity for craft skill and design applied outside the sacred context — to ships, idols, military machines, and instruments of music.

db.description: bin as the inner perceptive faculty in its constituted form — the quality of the wise, discerning person. The faculty is seated in the heart (1Ki 3:9, Psa 49:20), enables right judgment (1Ki 3:9,11), receives instruction (Pro 9:8), perceives situations rightly (2Sa 12:19, Job 6:30), and is the inner capacity from which wise conduct flows. It is morally conditioned — it enables covenant-keeping (Deu 4:6) and is blocked by the absence of the fear of the Lord.

current.analysis_note: "in the latter days you will understand it clearly" — understanding deferred; current absence of bin will be resolved eschatologically

meaning.text: bin names the future inner perceptive comprehension of God's purposes — what cannot be grasped now will be understood then.

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive quality of the prudent as the source of counsel — prudence and counsel are linked. Edom is famous for this wisdom; now it will be gone.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:5 As I was considering , behold , a male goat came from the west across the face of the whole earth , without touching the ground . And the goat had a conspicuous horn between his eyes .

current.analysis_note: "as I was considering, behold, a male goat came" — bin as Daniel's act of contemplating the vision

meaning.text: bin names the reflective perceptive attending — Daniel considering the vision when the next element of it appears.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:16 And I heard a man’s voice between the banks of the Ulai , and it called , “ Gabriel , make this man understand the vision .”

current.analysis_note: "Gabriel, make this man understand the vision" — bin enabled by angelic explanation; understanding given through divine intermediary

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive comprehension Gabriel is sent to produce in Daniel — the vision must be made intelligible to him.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:17 So he came near where I stood . And when he came , I was frightened and fell on my face . But he said to me, “ Understand , O son of man , that the vision is for the time of the end .”

current.analysis_note: "Understand, O son of man, that the vision is for the time of the end" — bin as the faculty directed toward prophetic vision

meaning.text: bin names the inner comprehension Gabriel calls Daniel to — the command to receive and grasp the vision's significance.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:23 And at the latter end of their kingdom , when the transgressors have reached their limit , a king of bold face , one who understands riddles , shall arise .

current.analysis_note: "a king who understands riddles" — bin as the inner capacity for grasping hidden meanings

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive capacity of the coming king — understanding riddles as an attribute of the dangerous figure who arises.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:27 And I , Daniel , was overcome and lay sick for some days . Then I rose and went about the king’s business , but I was appalled by the vision and did not understand it.

meaning.text: bin names the failed inner comprehension — Daniel could not grasp the meaning of what he had seen.

verses.verse_text: Dan 9:2 in the first year of his reign , I , Daniel , perceived in the books the number of years that , according to the word of the Lord to Jeremiah the prophet , must pass before the end of the desolations of Jerusalem , namely, seventy years .

current.analysis_note: "I, Daniel, perceived in the books the number of years" — bin as the act of grasping prophetic meaning from written prophecy

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive act of reading — Daniel grasps the significance of Jeremiah's prophecy from the text.

verses.verse_text: Dan 9:22 He made me understand , speaking with me and saying , “O Daniel , I have now come out to give you insight and understanding .

current.analysis_note: — | Also vr=7097 (bi.nah) — same verse, two terms

meaning.text: bin names the understanding Gabriel brings to Daniel — a gift of inner comprehension of the vision delivered by the messenger.

current.analysis_note: "consider the word and understand the vision" — directive to exercise bin toward the prophetic word

meaning.text: bin names the reflective perceptive attending Daniel is commanded to give — the inner faculty directed toward the word and the vision.

verses.verse_text: Dan 10:1 In the third year of Cyrus king of Persia a word was revealed to Daniel , who was named Belteshazzar . And the word was true , and it was a great conflict . And he understood the word and had understanding of the vision .

current.analysis_note: "he understood the word and had understanding of the vision" — bin achieved; Daniel's understanding of divine communication

meaning.text: bin names the inner comprehension Daniel reaches — the perceptive grasp of both the word and its visionary content.

current.analysis_note: "understand the words that I speak to you" — angelic directive to exercise bin

meaning.text: bin names the inner perceptive reception being commanded — the messenger calls Daniel to grasp what he is about to say.

current.analysis_note: "from the first day you set your heart to understand and humbled yourself"

meaning.text: bin names the deliberate orientation of the inner faculty — Daniel set his heart toward understanding as a posture of seeking.

new_vcg.description: Understanding as the fruit of deliberate orientation toward God — seeking, keeping the precepts, holding to the covenant. The faculty grows and deepens through the person's active engagement.

verses.verse_text: Job 20:3 I hear censure that insults me, and out of my understanding a spirit answers me .

current.analysis_note: "out of my understanding a spirit answers me" — bi.nah as the inner source from which response arises; understanding as the seat of the reasoning self

meaning.text: bi.nah names the inner perceptive faculty from which Zophar's response arises — understanding as the source of his answer.

verses.verse_text: Job 28:12 “But where shall wisdom be found ? And where is the place of understanding ?

current.analysis_note: "where is the place of understanding?" — rhetorical; bi.nah's inaccessibility to human searching

meaning.text: bi.nah names understanding as something sought but not locatable by human means — the rhetorical question frames understanding as beyond ordinary reach.

new_vcg.title: Understanding gained through seeking and engagement with the word and covenant
new_vcg.description: Understanding as the fruit of deliberate orientation toward God — seeking, keeping the precepts, holding to the covenant. The faculty grows and deepens through the person's active engagement.

current.analysis_note: "from where does wisdom come, where is understanding?" — same; the unreachability of bi.nah apart from God

meaning.text: bi.nah names understanding as the object of the same question — its source and location are beyond human searching.

current.analysis_note: "to turn away from evil is understanding" — bi.nah defined as moral-practical: its content is the turning from evil

meaning.text: bi.nah names understanding as what moral turning away constitutes — understanding is not abstract knowledge but enacted orientation away from evil.

current.analysis_note: "if you have understanding, hear this" — bi.nah as the prerequisite for reception

meaning.text: bi.nah names understanding as the precondition for receiving what is said — the hearer must have the inner faculty to receive the argument.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:4 “ Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth ? Tell me, if you have understanding .

current.analysis_note: "tell me, if you have understanding" — God's ironic challenge to Job; bi.nah insufficient for creation-knowledge

meaning.text: bi.nah names understanding as what the one who could answer God's challenge would need — a condition for grasping the foundations of creation.

current.analysis_note: "who has given understanding to the mind?" — God as the giver of bi.nah; the faculty is divine gift

meaning.text: bi.nah names understanding as a divine gift placed within the person — God is the one who gives the inner faculty of understanding.

meaning.text: bi.nah names discernment as the inner perceptive quality that enables restraint — seeing clearly enough to know when to stop.

current.analysis_note: "I have not the understanding of a man" — Agur's self-abnegating confession; bi.nah as what human beings normally have but he confesses absent

meaning.text: bi.nah names the inner perceptive faculty that Agur disclaims — a confession of his own inadequacy before the mysteries.

verses.verse_text: Isa 11:2 And the Spirit of the Lord shall rest upon him, the Spirit of wisdom and understanding , the Spirit of counsel and might , the Spirit of knowledge and the fear of the Lord .

current.analysis_note: "the Spirit of wisdom and understanding" — bi.nah as one of the Spirit's gifts resting on the Messiah

meaning.text: bi.nah names understanding as one of the Spirit's endowments on the Messiah — a Spirit-given inner capacity alongside wisdom, counsel, might, and knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Isa 27:11 When its boughs are dry , they are broken ; women come and make a fire of them. For this is a people without discernment ; therefore he who made them will not have compassion on them; he who formed them will show them no favor .

current.analysis_note: "a people without discernment" — bi.nah absent; produces withdrawal of divine compassion

meaning.text: bi.nah names discernment as the inner perceptive faculty whose absence results in divine withdrawal of compassion — the link between inner capacity and covenant relationship.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:14 therefore , behold , I will again do wonderful things with this people , with wonder upon wonder ; and the wisdom of their wise men shall perish , and the discernment of their discerning men shall be hidden .”

current.analysis_note: "the discernment of their discerning men shall be hidden" — bi.nah removed by divine judgment

meaning.text: bi.nah names discernment as what God hides — the inner capacity is present but its access to truth is blocked by God's action.

new_vcg.title: Judicial withholding of understanding: suniēmi / bin as blocked comprehension
new_vcg.description: Understanding deliberately withheld by God — the inner faculty is present but its activation is prevented by divine judgment. Hearing without receiving; perception without comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:24 And those who go astray in spirit will come to understanding , and those who murmur will accept instruction .”

current.analysis_note: "those who go astray in spirit will come to understanding" — bi.nah as the restoration awaiting the straying

meaning.text: bi.nah names understanding as the destination of restoration — those who erred will arrive at the inner perceptive faculty. Understanding is the fruit of turning back.

verses.verse_text: Isa 33:19 You will see no more the insolent people , the people of an obscure speech that you cannot comprehend , stammering in a tongue that you cannot understand .

current.analysis_note: "a tongue you cannot understand" — bi.nah as comprehension of speech; foreign tongue exceeds the faculty

meaning.text: bi.nah names the perceptive faculty confronted with unintelligible speech — the inner capacity to grasp meaning, here applied to language comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Jer 23:20 The anger of the Lord will not turn back until he has executed and accomplished the intents of his heart . In the latter days you will understand it clearly .

current.analysis_note: "in the latter days you will understand it clearly" — bi.nah as eschatological clarity

meaning.text: bi.nah names the clarity of future understanding — the quality of the perceptive grasp that will come.
