current.analysis_note: "in every matter of wisdom and understanding... ten times better" — bi.nah as the quality evaluated by the king; the inner faculty assessed

meaning.text: bi.nah names understanding as one of the two inner qualities in which the four youths surpassed the court experts — a tested and verified inner capacity.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:15 When I , Daniel , had seen the vision , I sought to understand it . And behold , there stood before me one having the appearance of a man .

current.analysis_note: "I sought to understand it" — Daniel's active pursuit of bi.nah regarding the vision

meaning.text: bi.nah names the object of Daniel's seeking — he wants to grasp the meaning of what he has seen.

verses.verse_text: Dan 9:22 He made me understand , speaking with me and saying , “O Daniel , I have now come out to give you insight and understanding .

current.analysis_note: B-2 | Same verse dual entry

meaning.text: bi.nah names the understanding conveyed — the inner capacity to grasp the revelation given by Gabriel.

verses.verse_text: Dan 10:1 In the third year of Cyrus king of Persia a word was revealed to Daniel , who was named Belteshazzar . And the word was true , and it was a great conflict . And he understood the word and had understanding of the vision .

current.analysis_note: "he understood the word and had understanding of the vision" — bi.nah achieved; Daniel's comprehension of prophetic revelation

meaning.text: bi.nah names understanding of the vision as what Daniel possesses — same verse, bi.nah as the noun form of the inner capacity attained.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:21 He changes times and seasons ; he removes kings and sets up kings ; he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding ;

current.analysis_note: "he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding" — bi.nah as the inner capacity that qualifies persons to receive divine knowledge; the faculty as the pre-condition for receiving

meaning.text: bi.nah names understanding as the inner capacity that makes the person a recipient of God's knowledge — the faculty that qualifies reception.

new_vcg.title: Aramaic wisdom terms: chokh.mah / chak.kim as divine wisdom given to Daniel
new_vcg.description: Wisdom as God's exclusive gift — given to Daniel, belonging to God, not a product of professional training. The Aramaic terms operate consistently in the Daniel narratives as marking the contrast between institutional wisdom (which fails) and Spirit-given wisdom (which succeeds).

db.description: Understanding as the inner receptive capacity that God recognises and enriches — those who have understanding receive wisdom and knowledge from God (Dan 2:21); the cognitive-spiritual capacity for discernment.

verses.verse_text: Gen 2:9 And out of the ground the Lord God made to spring up every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food . The tree of life was in the midst of the garden , and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil .

current.analysis_note: "the tree of the knowledge of good and evil" — da.at as the defining content of what was prohibited; the tree names a constitutive moral capacity

meaning.text: da.at names the knowledge that the tree embodies — moral knowledge of good and evil as a defining inner capacity. The tree is named by what it contains; knowledge of good and evil is not mere information but a transformative inner orientation.

verses.verse_text: Gen 2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat , for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die .”

current.analysis_note: "of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat" — da.at as what was placed off-limits; moral-constitutional boundary

meaning.text: da.at names the knowledge God withholds from the human — moral self-determination, the capacity to define good and evil for oneself. The prohibition frames this knowledge as dangerous.

verses.verse_text: Exo 31:3 and I have filled him with the Spirit of God , with ability and intelligence , with knowledge and all craftsmanship ,

current.analysis_note: — | Same verse as M15-B (te.vu.nah); cross-sub-group dual entry

meaning.text: da.at names knowledge as one of the Spirit-given inner endowments for sacred craft — an inner capacity given by God alongside ability, intelligence, and craftsmanship. Note: assigned to 1838-001 rather than 955-003 — this appears to be a misassignment; the verse is the same as Exo 35:31 which is correctly in 955-003.

new_vcg.description: Understanding as explicitly God-given — the inner capacity placed within the person by God or the Spirit, not acquired through human effort. Includes craft intelligence for sacred work and the Messiah's Spirit-given understanding.

verses.verse_text: Num 24:16 the oracle of him who hears the words of God , and knows the knowledge of the Most High , who sees the vision of the Almighty , falling down with his eyes uncovered :

current.analysis_note: 1509 | Same verse dual entry

meaning.text: da.at names the knowledge of the Most High as an inner capacity of the seer — divine knowledge held within the person as a gift. verses.verse_text: Deu 4:42 that the manslayer might flee there , anyone who kills his neighbor unintentionally , without being at enmity with him in time past ; he may flee to one of these cities and save his life :

current.analysis_note: "kills his neighbour unintentionally" — da.at as the inner state of intent/intent-absence

meaning.text: da.at names the inner state of intent — killing without da.at is killing without the moral knowledge/intent of enmity. The presence or absence of da.at determines culpability.

new_vcg.title: da.at as inner intent determining moral status of an act
new_vcg.description: Da.at (or its absence) as what determines whether an act is intentional — presence of da.at/knowing means wilful; absence means unintentional. The inner moral condition determines culpability.

meaning.text: da.at names knowledge as something that brings inner pain — the more one knows, the more the limits and griefs of existence become apparent.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:21 because sometimes a person who has toiled with wisdom and knowledge and skill must leave everything to be enjoyed by someone who did not toil for it. This also is vanity and a great evil .

Ecc 2:26 For to the one who pleases him God has given wisdom and knowledge and joy , but to the sinner he has given the business of gathering and collecting , only to give to one who pleases God . This also is vanity and a striving after wind .
Ecc 7:12 For the protection of wisdom is like the protection of money , and the advantage of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of him who has it.
Ecc 9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do , do it with your might , for there is no work or thought or knowledge or wisdom in Sheol , to which you are going .

current.analysis_note: "no work or thought or knowledge or wisdom in Sheol" — da.at as a living-person capacity that death erases

meaning.text: da.at names knowledge as belonging to the living — it ceases at death. Inner knowing is a capacity of the living person.

meaning.text: da.at names knowledge of God as what Israel lacks — the absence produces exile. The knowledge of God and national stability are directly linked.

meaning.text: da.at names knowledge as a Spirit-given endowment on the Messiah — one of the sevenfold gifts resting on him.

meaning.text: da.at names knowledge as part of God's gift of stability — Spirit-given inner knowing as part of the coming abundance.

current.analysis_note: "no knowledge or discernment to say..." — da.at absent in the idolater; cannot perceive the absurdity

meaning.text: da.at names the knowledge absent in the idol-worshipper — they lack the inner capacity to perceive the absurdity of their practice.

meaning.text: da.at names the knowledge God frustrates — the supposed inner knowing of diviners and wise men rendered empty.

current.analysis_note: "by his knowledge shall the righteous one... make many to be accounted righteous" — the Servant's inner knowledge as the instrument of justification

meaning.text: da.at names the knowledge by which the Servant justifies — his inner knowing is the instrument of the many's righteousness. A remarkable and distinctive use.

meaning.text: da.at names the inner capacity absent in idol-makers — without it, their craft produces only falsehood.

current.analysis_note: "Is not this to know me? declares the Lord" — da.at defined as the practice of justice toward the poor

meaning.text: da.at names the knowledge of God as expressed in justice for the vulnerable — knowing God and doing justice are equated. A profound definitional statement.

meaning.text: da.at names the defining absence in Israel — without the knowledge of God, the land has no faithfulness or steadfast love. All three are interlinked.

current.analysis_note: "my people destroyed for lack of knowledge"

meaning.text: da.at names knowledge of God as the inner orientation whose rejection produces destruction — the most explicit statement of the consequences of da.at's absence.

verses.verse_text: Hos 6:6 For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice , the knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings .

current.analysis_note: — | Also appears in the covenantal-knowing cluster; da.at and ya.da verses from same passage cross-reference

meaning.text: da.at names the knowledge of God as what God desires above sacrifice — relational inner knowing as the substance of true worship. Note: assigned to 1838-001, but this is a positive statement of what God desires, not culpable ignorance. The assignment appears to be a misrouting.

meaning.text: da.at names knowledge as the priestly possession that must be guarded and dispensed — the inner capacity held by the priest for the benefit of the community.

verses.verse_text: Psa 49:3 My mouth shall speak wisdom ; the meditation of my heart shall be understanding .

current.analysis_note: 'the meditation of my heart shall be understanding' — ha.gut as heart-located generative inner activity whose product is bi.nah

meaning.text: ha.gut names heart-meditation as the activity that produces understanding — the reflective inner engagement of the mind before God is what understanding is made of.

new_vcg.title: Heart-meditation producing understanding: ha.gut as the contemplative inner engagement
new_vcg.description: A single verse. The meditation of the heart is named as what produces understanding — the reflective inner engagement before God is the generative activity from which wisdom flows.

db.description: Term names the meditation of the heart as the inner activity that produces understanding — reflective inner engagement of the mind before God

new.title: Heart-meditation producing understanding: ha.gut as the contemplative inner engagement
new.description: A single verse. The meditation of the heart is named as what produces understanding — the reflective inner engagement before God is the generative activity from which wisdom flows.

verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster : for the flutes . A Psalm of David . Psa 5:1 Give ear to my words , O Lord ; consider my groaning .

current.analysis_note: 'consider my groaning' — ha.gig as wordless inner groaning offered to God before speech

meaning.text: ha.gig names inner musing that has become groaning — deep reflective engagement with distress that the psalmist presents to God as the substance of prayer.

new_vcg.title: Inner musing that burns to groaning and speech: ha.gig as prayer-substance
new_vcg.description: Deep inner reflective engagement that intensifies inwardly — becoming fire in the heart — and is then offered to God as the substance of prayer.

db.description: Term names inner musing and groaning brought before God — the deep inner reflection that burns within the person and that is offered to God as the substance of prayer

new.title: Inner musing that burns to groaning and speech: ha.gig as prayer-substance
new.description: Deep inner reflective engagement that intensifies inwardly — becoming fire in the heart — and is then offered to God as the substance of prayer.

verses.verse_text: Psa 39:3 My heart became hot within me. As I mused , the fire burned ; then I spoke with my tongue :

current.analysis_note: 'as I mused, the fire burned; then I spoke' — inner musing producing cardiac heat producing compelled utterance

meaning.text: ha.gig names the deep inner reflective engagement that generates heat within — musing that intensifies to burning and finally breaks out as speech.

new_vcg.title: Inner musing that burns to groaning and speech: ha.gig as prayer-substance
new_vcg.description: Deep inner reflective engagement that intensifies inwardly — becoming fire in the heart — and is then offered to God as the substance of prayer.

db.description: Term names inner musing and groaning brought before God — the deep inner reflection that burns within the person and that is offered to God as the substance of prayer

new.title: Inner musing that burns to groaning and speech: ha.gig as prayer-substance

verses.verse_text: Psa 19:14 Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in your sight , O Lord , my rock and my redeemer .

current.analysis_note: 'the meditation of my heart be acceptable in your sight' — hig.ga.von as heart-meditation offered to God

meaning.text: hig.ga.von names the heart's meditation as what is offered to God alongside the words of the mouth — the inner reflective activity is the inward counterpart to outward speech. Both are offered as worship.

new_vcg.title: Heart-meditation offered to God alongside spoken words: hig.ga.von as worship substance
new_vcg.description: The heart's meditative activity offered to God as the inward counterpart of spoken prayer — what the mouth says and what the heart meditates are both presented.

verses.verse_text: Psa 92:3 to the music of the lute and the harp , to the melody of the lyre .

meaning.text: hig.ga.von set aside as no inner-being content — musical instrument sound, not meditative activity.

verses.verse_text: Lam 3:62 The lips and thoughts of my assailants are against me all the day long.

current.analysis_note: 'the lips and thoughts of my assailants are against me' — hig.ga.von as hostile inner rumination; directional neutrality confirmed

meaning.text: hig.ga.von names the hostile meditations/thoughts of the assailants — what they dwell on inwardly and utter with their lips is turned against the suffering person.

verses.verse_text: Gen 11:6 And the Lord said , “Behold, they are one people , and they have all one language , and this is only the beginning of what they will do . And nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them.

current.analysis_note: "nothing they propose will be impossible" — za.mam as unified deliberate human purposing

meaning.text: za.mam names the human capacity for purposeful resolve — what they form as intention and set out to accomplish. The divine concern is about the power of unified human purposing when unchecked.

new_vcg.title: Human purposeful resolve: za.mam as intent formed and set out to achieve
new_vcg.description: A human subject forms a purposeful intent and commits to carrying it out — the resolve of unified human purposing.

db.description: Term names the formation of purposeful intention in a human subject — what the mind resolves to do

new.title: Human purposeful resolve: za.mam as intent formed and set out to achieve
new.description: A human subject forms a purposeful intent and commits to carrying it out — the resolve of unified human purposing.

verses.verse_text: Jer 4:28 “ For this the earth shall mourn , and the heavens above be dark ; for I have spoken ; I have purposed ; I have not relented , nor will I turn back .”

current.analysis_note: "I have spoken; I have purposed; I have not relented, nor will I turn back" — divine za.mam as irrevocable

meaning.text: za.mam names God's resolute divine purposing — what he has formed as intention will not be withdrawn. The triple affirmation (spoken, purposed, not relented) frames divine counsel as irrevocable.

new_vcg.title: Divine counsel: God's sovereign purposive intent directed toward nations and history
new_vcg.description: God forms and executes sovereign intention that cannot be annulled or redirected. His counsel is self-originating, irresistible, and encompasses the whole of history. Multiple human terms (ya.ats, cha.shav, za.mam) converge in expressing this.

current.analysis_note: "the Lord has both planned and done what he spoke" — za.mam executed; divine purpose becoming historical fact

meaning.text: za.mam names God's resolute purposing carried through — intent formed and executed, not relented from.

current.analysis_note: "the Lord has done what he purposed" — divine za.mam fulfilled in Jerusalem's fall

meaning.text: za.mam names God's resolute ancient purposing fulfilled — what was formed as intention long ago has now been enacted.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:12 Because of this the king was angry and very furious , and commanded that all the wise men of Babylon be destroyed .

current.analysis_note: "commanded all wise men of Babylon to be destroyed" — the class is in danger because they cannot perform what Spirit-given wisdom can

meaning.text: chak.kim names the professional class of court wise men — their failure to interpret the dream has made them a target. Their title and their incapacity stand together.

new_vcg.title: Professional court wise men as institutional class: cha.kham / chak.kim as class title
new_vcg.description: The court class of professional wise men — summoned for interpretation, giving counsel, named as the foil for what Spirit-given wisdom can do. Their title and incapacity stand together in the Daniel narratives.

db.description: The chak.kim P-status verses all appear in Daniel 2–5. They name "the wise men of Babylon" — the institutional class of court wise men who cannot read divine mysteries. They form the consistent foil: what human wisdom (even at its most professional and prestigious) cannot do, the Spirit-given wisdom in Daniel can. The group's function is structural contrast — it makes visible what Spirit-given wisdom is by showing what it is not.

new.title: Professional court wise men as institutional class: cha.kham / chak.kim as class title
new.description: The court class of professional wise men — summoned for interpretation, giving counsel, named as the foil for what Spirit-given wisdom can do. Their title and incapacity stand together in the Daniel narratives.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:13 So the decree went out , and the wise men were about to be killed ; and they sought Daniel and his companions , to kill them.

current.analysis_note: "decree went out, wise men were about to be killed" — their failure has lethal consequences

meaning.text: chak.kim names the class facing destruction — their wisdom could not meet the king's demand.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:14 Then Daniel replied with prudence and discretion to Arioch , the captain of the king’s guard , who had gone out to kill the wise men of Babylon .

current.analysis_note: "gone out to kill the wise men of Babylon" — Daniel is included in this class but is about to demonstrate a different wisdom

meaning.text: chak.kim names the class Daniel is being grouped with — he intercedes for them though they could not do what he can.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:18 and told them to seek mercy from the God of heaven concerning this mystery , so that Daniel and his companions might not be destroyed with the rest of the wise men of Babylon .

current.analysis_note: "seek mercy that Daniel not be destroyed with rest of wise men" — Daniel is classified with the chak.kim but possesses what they lack

meaning.text: chak.kim names the class with whom Daniel is at risk — solidarity in danger despite difference in capacity.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:21 He changes times and seasons ; he removes kings and sets up kings ; he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding ;

current.analysis_note: "gives wisdom to the wise" — chak.kim receive what they cannot generate; their wisdom is derivative

meaning.text: chak.kim names the wise as recipients of what God gives — even the capacity they have is given by him. The wise men of Babylon could not access this.

new_vcg.title: Aramaic wisdom terms: chokh.mah / chak.kim as divine wisdom given to Daniel
new_vcg.description: Wisdom as God's exclusive gift — given to Daniel, belonging to God, not a product of professional training. The Aramaic terms operate consistently in the Daniel narratives as marking the contrast between institutional wisdom (which fails) and Spirit-given wisdom (which succeeds).

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:24 Therefore Daniel went in to Arioch , whom the king had appointed to destroy the wise men of Babylon . He went and said thus to him: “Do not destroy the wise men of Babylon ; bring me in before the king , and I will show the king the interpretation .”

current.analysis_note: "do not destroy the wise men of Babylon" — Daniel intercedes for the class despite their failure

meaning.text: chak.kim names the class Daniel is interceding for — he will do what they could not, but he does not abandon them.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:27 Daniel answered the king and said , “ No wise men , enchanters , magicians , or astrologers can show to the king the mystery that the king has asked ,

current.analysis_note: "no wise men can show the king the mystery" — the class's fundamental limitation stated

meaning.text: chak.kim names the limitation of the professional wisdom class — the mystery is beyond their reach by definition.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:48 Then the king gave Daniel high honors and many great gifts , and made him ruler over the whole province of Babylon and chief prefect over all the wise men of Babylon .

current.analysis_note: "chief prefect over all wise men of Babylon" — Daniel elevated above the class; divine wisdom surpasses human

meaning.text: chak.kim names the professional class over which Daniel is appointed — he is set above the institutional wisdom of Babylon by the king's recognition of what God has given him.

verses.verse_text: Dan 4:6 So I made a decree that all the wise men of Babylon should be brought before me, that they might make known to me the interpretation of the dream .

current.analysis_note: "decree that all wise men be brought before me" — Nebuchadnezzar still tries human wisdom first

meaning.text: chak.kim names the professional class summoned to interpret — again they are the foil for what Spirit-given wisdom can do.

current.analysis_note: "all wise men not able; but you are able, for spirit of holy gods is in you" — the contrast is explicit: chak.kim fail, Daniel succeeds through the Spirit

meaning.text: chak.kim names the class whose inability frames Daniel's ability — the distinction is the Spirit, not professional qualification.

current.analysis_note: "none so discerning and wise as you" — the wisest person qualified to govern; superlative wisdom = greatest fitness

meaning.text: cha.kham names wisdom as a quality recognised in Joseph by Pharaoh — it has been demonstrated through the revelation and interpretation. God's disclosure is the ground of the recognition.

meaning.text: cha.kham names wisdom as the constituted inner quality of those selected for governance — the men are defined by it.

verses.verse_text: Deu 4:6 Keep them and do them, for that will be your wisdom and your understanding in the sight of the peoples , who , when they hear all these statutes , will say , ‘ Surely this great nation is a wise and understanding people .’

current.analysis_note: "wisdom and understanding in the sight of the peoples" — covenant obedience produces visible wisdom; other nations will perceive it as a national quality

meaning.text: cha.kham names wisdom as what covenant obedience will be seen to be — obedience is the outward form that wisdom takes. Israel's inner quality becomes visible through its conduct.

db.description: cha.kham in Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and historical narrative names wisdom as a constituted inner quality of the person — not a role or a skill but who they are. current.analysis_note: "a bribe blinds the eyes of the wise" — wisdom is the inner capacity for justice; bribes corrupt it

meaning.text: cha.kham names the wise person as one whose inner perception can be corrupted by external pressure. The wise are not immune — their wisdom is vulnerable to what blinds.

new_vcg.title: Wisdom as constituted inner quality of character: cha.kham as holistic inner endowment

new_vcg.title: Self-attributed wisdom: cha.kham as false inner claim

db.description: cha.kham here names wisdom as the qualification for a role — the wise man sought for appointment, the wise men of the court, those who have the inner capacity for governance, law, and counsel. The focus is on wisdom as a capacity that qualifies persons for institutional responsibility.

new.title: Professional court wise men as institutional class: cha.kham / chak.kim as class title

verses.verse_text: Job 34:34 Men of understanding will say to me, and the wise man who hears me will say:

current.analysis_note: "wise man who hears me will say" — the wise as those qualified to assess

meaning.text: cha.kham names the wise person as one whose inner quality enables right hearing and agreement — their response confirms the truth of the argument.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:24 Therefore men fear him; he does not regard any who are wise in their own conceit .”

current.analysis_note: "does not regard those wise in their own conceit" — self-wisdom as the object of God's disregard

meaning.text: cha.kham names wisdom claimed as self-attribution — conceit is the opposite of the fear that produces genuine wisdom. God does not regard those who hold wisdom as their own.

new_vcg.title: Self-attributed wisdom: cha.kham as false inner claim
new_vcg.description: Persons who attribute wisdom to themselves without having it — the inner claim is exposed as false, hollow, or dangerous.

db.description: cha.kham here names wisdom turned back on itself — the person who claims wisdom without the fear of the Lord, whose wisdom serves evil, or whose self-assessment of wisdom is the very sign of its absence.

new.title: Self-attributed wisdom: cha.kham as false inner claim
new.description: Persons who attribute wisdom to themselves without having it — the inner claim is exposed as false, hollow, or dangerous.

verses.verse_text: Psa 49:10 For he sees that even the wise die ; the fool and the stupid alike must perish and leave their wealth to others .

current.analysis_note: "even the wise die" — wisdom does not exempt from mortality

meaning.text: cha.kham names the wise person whose inner quality does not exempt them from death — wisdom and folly are equalised by mortality. The observation is about the limits of what wisdom can secure.

new_vcg.title: Wisdom as constituted inner quality of character: cha.kham as holistic inner endowment
new_vcg.description: Wisdom as a defining inner quality of the person — not a skill or technique but a whole-person orientation that shows in perception, speech, conduct, and relationships. The quality is positive and characterises those who are genuinely wise.

db.description: cha.kham in Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and historical narrative names wisdom as a constituted inner quality of the person — not a role or a skill but who they are. The wise person is characterised by this quality: they receive instruction, they are cautious, they hold back their spirit, their heart is in the right place. The quality is relational — it shows in how they relate to others, to rebuke, to advice.

new.description: Wisdom as a defining inner quality of the person — not a skill or technique but a whole-person orientation that shows in perception, speech, conduct, and relationships. The quality is positive and characterises those who are genuinely wise.

meaning.text: cha.kham names wisdom as the inner quality that enables attention and consideration of God's acts — the wise person is the one who sees and reflects on what God has done.

meaning.text: cha.kham names self-attributed wisdom — being wise in one's own eyes is the opposite of fearing the Lord. The admonition contrasts self-sourced wisdom with the wisdom rooted in reverence.

current.analysis_note: "wise will inherit honor, fools get disgrace" — the contrast; here wisdom's proper fruit stated

meaning.text: cha.kham names the wise person as the one whose inner quality leads to an outcome — honour. The verse states the trajectory of wisdom, not its content.

current.analysis_note: "four things small but exceedingly wise" — wisdom as an inner quality even tiny creatures possess

meaning.text: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality found in small creatures — wisdom is not proportional to size or power but is a distinct inner capacity.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:14 The wise person has his eyes in his head , but the fool walks in darkness . And yet I perceived that the same event happens to all of them.

current.analysis_note: "the wise person has his eyes in his head" — wisdom as clear perception of reality

meaning.text: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality that produces perceptual clarity — the wise see where they are going.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:16 For of the wise as of the fool there is no enduring remembrance , seeing that in the days to come all will have been long forgotten . How the wise dies just like the fool !

current.analysis_note: "the wise dies just like the fool" — Qoheleth's irony: wisdom doesn't exempt from death

meaning.text: cha.kham names the wise person alongside the fool under the equalising effect of death and forgetfulness — wisdom does not guarantee lasting legacy.

db.description: cha.kham in Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and historical narrative names wisdom as a constituted inner quality of the person — not a role or a skill but who they are. The wise person is characterised by this quality: they receive instruction, they are cautious, they hold back their spirit, their heart is in the right place. The quality is relational — it shows in how they relate to others, to rebuke, to advice.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:19 and who knows whether he will be wise or a fool ? Yet he will be master of all for which I toiled and used my wisdom under the sun . This also is vanity .

current.analysis_note: "who knows whether he will be wise or a fool?" — wisdom as an inner quality that cannot be guaranteed by inheritance

meaning.text: cha.kham names wisdom as an uncertain quality in a future heir — the fruit of one's labour may pass to someone without the inner quality to steward it.

meaning.text: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality whose advantage is called into question by Qoheleth — under the sun, wisdom's edge is limited.

meaning.text: cha.kham names the wise person confronted with the limits of their inner capacity — wisdom cannot penetrate God's work under the sun.

meaning.text: cha.kham names the wise as those in God's hand — their inner quality does not exempt them from divine sovereignty over their lives and deeds.

verses.verse_text: Isa 3:3 the captain of fifty and the man of rank , the counselor and the skillful magician and the expert in charms .

current.analysis_note: "the skillful magician and expert in charms" — wisdom as institutional specialisation

new_vcg.title: Human counsel: wisdom given between persons shaping deliberation
new_vcg.description: One person gives advice or counsel to another — the inner wisdom of the advisor expressed and offered to shape the recipient's purposes. The outcome depends on whether counsel is received or rejected.

db.description: cha.kham here names wisdom as the qualification for a role — the wise man sought for appointment, the wise men of the court, those who have the inner capacity for governance, law, and counsel. The focus is on wisdom as a capacity that qualifies persons for institutional responsibility.

new.title: Human counsel: wisdom given between persons shaping deliberation
new.description: One person gives advice or counsel to another — the inner wisdom of the advisor expressed and offered to shape the recipient's purposes. The outcome depends on whether counsel is received or rejected.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:21 Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes , and shrewd in their own sight !

current.analysis_note: "woe to those wise in their own eyes" — prophetic judgment on self-attributed wisdom

verses.verse_text: Isa 19:11 The princes of Zoan are utterly foolish ; the wisest counselors of Pharaoh give stupid counsel . How can you say to Pharaoh , “I am a son of the wise , a son of ancient kings ”?

current.analysis_note: "wisest counselors of Pharaoh give stupid counsel" — wisdom as the inner capacity of the court that has failed

verses.verse_text: Isa 19:12 Where then are your wise men? Let them tell you that they might know what the Lord of hosts has purposed against Egypt .

current.analysis_note: "where then are your wise men?" — wisdom as what the nation's counsellors should have

new_vcg.title: Divine counsel: God's sovereign purposive intent directed toward nations and history
new_vcg.description: God forms and executes sovereign intention that cannot be annulled or redirected. His counsel is self-originating, irresistible, and encompasses the whole of history. Multiple human terms (ya.ats, cha.shav, za.mam) converge in expressing this.

db.description: cha.kham here names wisdom as the qualification for a role — the wise man sought for appointment, the wise men of the court, those who have the inner capacity for governance, law, and counsel. The focus is on wisdom as a capacity that qualifies persons for institutional responsibility.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:14 therefore , behold , I will again do wonderful things with this people , with wonder upon wonder ; and the wisdom of their wise men shall perish , and the discernment of their discerning men shall be hidden .”

current.analysis_note: "wisdom of their wise men shall perish" — judgment on the wisdom-capacity of the governing class

new_vcg.title: Judicial withholding of understanding: suniēmi / bin as blocked comprehension
new_vcg.description: Understanding deliberately withheld by God — the inner faculty is present but its activation is prevented by divine judgment. Hearing without receiving; perception without comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Isa 31:2 And yet he is wise and brings disaster ; he does not call back his words , but will arise against the house of the evildoers and against the helpers of those who work iniquity .

current.analysis_note: "he is wise and brings disaster" — God's own wisdom in governance; applied to his judgment

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:20 He who is too impoverished for an offering chooses wood that will not rot ; he seeks out a skillful craftsman to set up an idol that will not move .

current.analysis_note: "seeks out a skillful craftsman to set up an idol" — craft-skill directed toward idolatry; the inner capacity serves a false object

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:25 who frustrates the signs of liars and makes fools of diviners , who turns wise men back and makes their knowledge foolish ,

current.analysis_note: "turns wise men back and makes their knowledge foolish" — wisdom-capacity of the wise overturned by God

new_vcg.title: Wisdom destroyed or absent in judgment: cha.kham as what God removes
new_vcg.description: God destroys, removes, or withholds wisdom from those who rely on it against him — the wise men of nations are rendered helpless, made fools, or destroyed.

db.description: cha.kham here names wisdom as the qualification for a role — the wise man sought for appointment, the wise men of the court, those who have the inner capacity for governance, law, and counsel. verses.verse_text: Jer 4:22 “ For my people are foolish ; they know me not ; they are stupid children ; they have no understanding . They are ‘ wise ’—in doing evil ! But how to do good they know not .”

current.analysis_note: "'wise' in doing evil" — wisdom faculty serving evil; wisdom-as-label used ironically

meaning.text: cha.kham names the people's wisdom as misdirected — their inner capacity functions in the domain of evil, not good. Wisdom without moral alignment is corrupted.

new_vcg.title: Self-attributed wisdom: cha.kham as false inner claim
new_vcg.description: Persons who attribute wisdom to themselves without having it — the inner claim is exposed as false, hollow, or dangerous.

db.description: cha.kham here names wisdom turned back on itself — the person who claims wisdom without the fear of the Lord, whose wisdom serves evil, or whose self-assessment of wisdom is the very sign of its absence.

new.title: Self-attributed wisdom: cha.kham as false inner claim
new.description: Persons who attribute wisdom to themselves without having it — the inner claim is exposed as false, hollow, or dangerous.

verses.verse_text: Jer 8:8 “ How can you say , ‘We are wise , and the law of the Lord is with us’? But behold , the lying pen of the scribes has made it into a lie .

current.analysis_note: "'we are wise, and the law of the Lord is with us'" — false self-claim of wisdom exposed

meaning.text: cha.kham names a self-claim to wisdom that is exposed as false — the lying pen of the scribes has emptied the law of its truth, making the claim hollow.

current.analysis_note: "wise men shall be put to shame; rejected the word of the Lord" — when wisdom rejects revelation, it destroys itself

meaning.text: cha.kham names the wise whose rejection of God's word has emptied their wisdom — what wisdom is in them without the word?

verses.verse_text: Jer 9:12 Who is the man so wise that he can understand this ? To whom has the mouth of the Lord spoken , that he may declare it? Why is the land ruined and laid waste like a wilderness , so that no one passes through ?

current.analysis_note: "who is so wise that he can understand this?" — the wise are challenged; wisdom insufficient to comprehend judgment

meaning.text: cha.kham names the inner capacity being called for — understanding God's judgment on the land requires a wisdom that no one apparently possesses. The question implies wisdom of this order is absent.

meaning.text: cha.kham names the skilled mourning women — their skill is a craft of lament, a professional inner and performative capacity. This verse is P-status (pending).

new_vcg.description: The same inner capacity for craft skill and design applied outside the sacred context — to ships, idols, military machines, and instruments of music.

current.analysis_note: "let not the wise man boast in his wisdom" — wisdom as a potential object of boasting to be refused

meaning.text: cha.kham names wisdom as something the wise person is tempted to claim as their own glory — the admonition cuts against the pride of inner capacity.

current.analysis_note: "among all the wise ones of the nations there is none like you" — the nations' wisdom contrasted with God's incomparable nature

meaning.text: cha.kham names the wise of the nations as those with the greatest inner capacity — yet none compares to the Lord. The wise ones are the measure, and God surpasses them all.

verses.verse_text: Jer 10:9 Beaten silver is brought from Tarshish , and gold from Uphaz . They are the work of the craftsman and of the hands of the goldsmith ; their clothing is violet and purple ; they are all the work of skilled men .

current.analysis_note: "skilled men" — craftsmen making idols; same craft-wisdom faculty serving a false object

meaning.text: cha.kham names the skilled craftsmen whose work produces the idols — craft skill in service of false worship.

new_vcg.title: Craft skill applied to secular or non-sacred purposes: cha.kham and cha.shav in craft outside the sanctuary
new_vcg.description: The same inner capacity for craft skill and design applied outside the sacred context — to ships, idols, military machines, and instruments of music.

db.description: cha.kham and cha.shav H2803G name an inner capacity for skilled craft that is explicitly Spirit-given. The skill resides in the heart/mind and is directed toward sacred construction. This is not general competence — it is an endowment for holy work.

new.title: Craft skill applied to secular or non-sacred purposes: cha.kham and cha.shav in craft outside the sanctuary
new.description: The same inner capacity for craft skill and design applied outside the sacred context — to ships, idols, military machines, and instruments of music.

verses.verse_text: Jer 18:18 Then they said , “ Come , let us make plots against Jeremiah , for the law shall not perish from the priest , nor counsel from the wise , nor the word from the prophet . Come , let us strike him with the tongue , and let us not pay attention to any of his words .”

current.analysis_note: "counsel shall not perish from the wise" — wisdom as the social function of a class

meaning.text: cha.kham names the wise as the institutional source of counsel — their inner quality produces guidance for the community. The conspirators assume this will continue even as they plot against the prophet.

new_vcg.title: Wisdom as governance qualification: cha.kham as inner capacity required for leadership
new_vcg.description: Wisdom as the specific qualification needed for the exercise of leadership and governance — it is the inner quality that enables just rule, right appointments, and effective governance.

db.description: cha.kham here names wisdom as the qualification for a role — the wise man sought for appointment, the wise men of the court, those who have the inner capacity for governance, law, and counsel. The focus is on wisdom as a capacity that qualifies persons for institutional responsibility.

new.title: Wisdom as governance qualification: cha.kham as inner capacity required for leadership

verses.verse_text: Jer 50:35 “A sword against the Chaldeans , declares the Lord , and against the inhabitants of Babylon , and against her officials and her wise men !

current.analysis_note: "sword against her wise men" — wisdom as a national capacity under judgment

meaning.text: cha.kham names Babylon's wise men as a class to be destroyed — their wisdom is named as part of what gives Babylon its power and identity.

new_vcg.title: Professional court wise men as institutional class: cha.kham / chak.kim as class title
new_vcg.description: The court class of professional wise men — summoned for interpretation, giving counsel, named as the foil for what Spirit-given wisdom can do. Their title and incapacity stand together in the Daniel narratives.

verses.verse_text: Jer 51:57 I will make drunk her officials and her wise men , her governors , her commanders , and her warriors ; they shall sleep a perpetual sleep and not wake , declares the King , whose name is the Lord of hosts .

current.analysis_note: "make drunk her wise men" — wisdom-capacity of Babylon's leaders destroyed

meaning.text: cha.kham names Babylon's wise men among those whom God will render helpless — wisdom offers no protection against divine judgment.

verses.verse_text: Eze 27:8 The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers ; your skilled men, O Tyre , were in you; they were your pilots .

current.analysis_note: "skilled men, O Tyre, were your pilots" — maritime skill as inner craft wisdom

meaning.text: cha.kham names Tyre's skilled men whose inner capacity for navigation makes them the pilots of the city's commerce. Wisdom as craft excellence in service of a great commercial enterprise.

new_vcg.title: Craft skill applied to secular or non-sacred purposes: cha.kham and cha.shav in craft outside the sanctuary
new_vcg.description: The same inner capacity for craft skill and design applied outside the sacred context — to ships, idols, military machines, and instruments of music.

db.description: cha.kham and cha.shav H2803G name an inner capacity for skilled craft that is explicitly Spirit-given. The skill resides in the heart/mind and is directed toward sacred construction. This is not general competence — it is an endowment for holy work.

new.title: Craft skill applied to secular or non-sacred purposes: cha.kham and cha.shav in craft outside the sanctuary
new.description: The same inner capacity for craft skill and design applied outside the sacred context — to ships, idols, military machines, and instruments of music.

verses.verse_text: Eze 27:9 The elders of Gebal and her skilled men were in you, caulking your seams ; all the ships of the sea with their mariners were in you to barter for your wares .

current.analysis_note: "skilled men were in you, caulking your seams" — specialist technical skill

meaning.text: cha.kham names the skilled craftsmen of Gebal whose inner capacity for craft work maintains Tyre's ships.

verses.verse_text: Eze 28:3 you are indeed wiser than Daniel ; no secret is hidden from you ;

current.analysis_note: "you are wiser than Daniel; no secret is hidden from you" — wisdom as the claimed inner capacity of the prince of Tyre

meaning.text: cha.kham names the prince of Tyre's self-assessed wisdom as surpassing even Daniel — the claim is ironic, setting up the judgment against pride.

new_vcg.title: Self-attributed wisdom: cha.kham as false inner claim
new_vcg.description: Persons who attribute wisdom to themselves without having it — the inner claim is exposed as false, hollow, or dangerous.
new_vcg.verse_annotation: ironic — sets up judgment against pride

verses.verse_text: Hos 13:13 The pangs of childbirth come for him, but he is an unwise son , for at the right time he does not present himself at the opening of the womb .

current.analysis_note: "he is an unwise son" — lo-cha.kham (not-wise); structural opposite; the unwise person who does not read the moment

meaning.text: cha.kham used negatively — the son is unwise because he fails to act at the moment of opportunity. Wisdom here is the capacity to recognise and respond to the right moment.

new_vcg.title: Wisdom destroyed or absent in judgment: cha.kham as what God removes
new_vcg.description: God destroys, removes, or withholds wisdom from those who rely on it against him — the wise men of nations are rendered helpless, made fools, or destroyed.

db.description: cha.kham in Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and historical narrative names wisdom as a constituted inner quality of the person — not a role or a skill but who they are. The wise person is characterised by this quality: they receive instruction, they are cautious, they hold back their spirit, their heart is in the right place. The quality is relational — it shows in how they relate to others, to rebuke, to advice.

new.title: Wisdom destroyed or absent in judgment: cha.kham as what God removes
new.description: God destroys, removes, or withholds wisdom from those who rely on it against him — the wise men of nations are rendered helpless, made fools, or destroyed.

verses.verse_text: Hos 14:9 Whoever is wise , let him understand these things; whoever is discerning , let him know them; for the ways of the Lord are right , and the upright walk in them, but transgressors stumble in them .

current.analysis_note: "whoever is wise, let him understand these things" — wisdom as the prerequisite for understanding

meaning.text: cha.kham names wisdom as the inner quality that enables understanding of God's ways — wisdom and discernment are the capacities that perceive the rightness of God's paths.

new_vcg.title: Wisdom as constituted inner quality of character: cha.kham as holistic inner endowment
new_vcg.description: Wisdom as a defining inner quality of the person — not a skill or technique but a whole-person orientation that shows in perception, speech, conduct, and relationships. The quality is positive and characterises those who are genuinely wise.

db.description: cha.kham in Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and historical narrative names wisdom as a constituted inner quality of the person — not a role or a skill but who they are. verses.verse_text: Obd 8 Will I not on that day , declares the Lord , destroy the wise men out of Edom , and understanding out of Mount Esau ?

meaning.text: cha.kham names Edom's wise men as what God will remove — their inner wisdom is a source of national strength and identity. Its removal is judgment. P-status verse.

verses.verse_text: Ezr 7:25 “And you , Ezra , according to the wisdom of your God that is in your hand , appoint magistrates and judges who may judge all the people in the province Beyond the River , all such as know the laws of your God . And those who do not know them, you shall teach .

current.analysis_note: "wisdom of your God that is in your hand" — wisdom given to Ezra for governance; it is God's but held by Ezra

meaning.text: chokh.mah names the wisdom of God as something given to Ezra — held in his hand, operative in him. It qualifies him to appoint those who will judge by God's laws.

new_vcg.title: Aramaic wisdom terms: chokh.mah / chak.kim as divine wisdom given to Daniel
new_vcg.description: Wisdom as God's exclusive gift — given to Daniel, belonging to God, not a product of professional training. The Aramaic terms operate consistently in the Daniel narratives as marking the contrast between institutional wisdom (which fails) and Spirit-given wisdom (which succeeds).

db.description: Wisdom as a gift — God gives it, persons receive it, it enables what the recipient could not produce from themselves. The giving and receiving is the characteristic dynamic of this group.

meaning.text: chokh.mah names wisdom as a divine attribute — belonging to God. Daniel's prayer attributes wisdom to God as his possession, not a human achievement.

meaning.text: cha.shav names God's purposive devising — forming a way where none existed. The span points to intentional inner purposing directed toward a merciful outcome. This is divine planning, not craftsmanship.

verses.verse_text: Gen 15:6 And he believed the Lord , and he counted it to him as righteousness .

current.analysis_note: "counted it to him as righteousness" — God's inner act of moral reckoning; faith assessed and credited

meaning.text: cha.shav names the divine act of moral crediting — God reckons Abram's faith as righteousness. The inner act of counting/imputing is here divine, not human.

new_vcg.title: Divine moral imputation: crediting or not-crediting righteousness/guilt before God
new_vcg.description: The inner divine act of reckoning a moral status to a person — positive (faith counted as righteousness; act counted as righteousness) or negative (bloodguilt imputed; iniquity not counted). The reckoning is judicial and moral, not merely classificatory. The subject is always God or divine authority.

new_vcg.title: Evaluative regard of persons and things: assigning worth or category by reckoning

verses.verse_text: Gen 50:20 As for you , you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good , to bring it about that many people should be kept alive , as they are today .

current.analysis_note: "you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good" — DUAL (also E-11 per Flag 3): same term, brothers' evil intent and God's good intent in one verse; the definitive directional-neutrality verse

meaning.text: cha.shav names the deliberate purposive intent of both the brothers (evil) and God (good) — the same word for both, showing divine and human purposing working through the same events toward opposite ends.

new_vcg.title: Purposive divine and human intent working through the same event toward opposite ends

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 18:25 Then Saul said , “ Thus shall you say to David , ‘The king desires no bride-price except a hundred foreskins of the Philistines , that he may be avenged of the king’s enemies .’” Now Saul thought to make David fall by the hand of the Philistines .

current.analysis_note: "Saul thought to make David fall by the hand of the Philistines" — deliberate personal plotting; purposive intent with a specific goal

meaning.text: cha.shav names Saul's deliberate inner scheming — a purposive plan formed in the mind to use the bride-price as a trap.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 14:13 And the woman said , “ Why then have you planned such a thing against the people of God ? For in giving this decision the king convicts himself, inasmuch as the king does not bring his banished one home again.

current.analysis_note: "why then have you planned such a thing against the people of God?" — cha.shav H2803I as deliberate royal ruling-intent

meaning.text: cha.shav names David's royal purposing that has had an unintended consequence — the wise woman uses it to press the case for Absalom's return.

new_vcg.title: Evaluative regard of persons and things: assigning worth or category by reckoning
new_vcg.description: One person (human or divine) evaluates another person, group, or thing by reckoning — assigning them a worth, category, or status. current.analysis_note: "seek wisdom and the scheme of things" — chesh.bon as the deliberative reckoning-quest

meaning.text: chesh.bon names the rational faculty seeking to grasp the underlying structure of reality — Qoheleth's quest for the scheme of things as an inner explanatory capacity.

new_vcg.title: The rational explanatory faculty: chesh.bon seeking the scheme of things
new_vcg.description: The inner capacity for rational explanation and calculation — the mind seeking to understand the underlying structure of reality. Belongs to the living; ceases at death.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 14:24 And the men of Israel had been hard pressed that day , so Saul had laid an oath on the people , saying , “ Cursed be the man who eats food until it is evening and I am avenged on my enemies .” So none of the people had tasted food .
1Sa 14:29 Then Jonathan said , “My father has troubled the land . See how my eyes have become bright because I tasted a little of this honey .
1Sa 14:43 Then Saul said to Jonathan , “ Tell me what you have done .” And Jonathan told him, “I tasted a little honey with the tip of the staff that was in my hand . Here I am; I will die .”

2Sa 3:35 Then all the people came to persuade David to eat bread while it was yet day . But David swore , saying , “ God do so to me and more also , if I taste bread or anything else till the sun goes down !”

current.analysis_note: "if I taste bread" — ta.am as the marker of restored normal inner engagement; the vow makes tasting a sacred refusal

meaning.text: ta.am names the deliberate abstaining from tasting as the outward form of inner mourning for Abner. The refusal to taste marks the inner state of grief expressed through the body.

new_vcg.title: ta.am: taste as the faculty of inner discernment
new_vcg.description: The palate's capacity to test and evaluate food serves as the image for the inner faculty of discerning and testing. Used directly of food-tasting and figuratively of testing words, situations, and the goodness of God.

db.description: Term names the abstaining from taste as an expression of inner grief or penitence — the voluntary refusal to eat as the outward form of an inward state of mourning or contrition

new.title: ta.am: taste as the faculty of inner discernment
new.description: The palate's capacity to test and evaluate food serves as the image for the inner faculty of discerning and testing. Used directly of food-tasting and figuratively of testing words, situations, and the goodness of God.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 19:35 I am this day eighty years old . Can I discern what is pleasant and what is not ? Can your servant taste what he eats or what he drinks ? Can I still listen to the voice of singing men and singing women ? Why then should your servant be an added burden to my lord the king ?

current.analysis_note: "can I discern what is pleasant and what is not?" — ta.am as the perceptual capacity for pleasurable discrimination; the elderly Barzillai has lost it

meaning.text: ta.am names the inner faculty of discrimination applied to food — tasting as the capacity to discern what is pleasant. The aged Barzillai uses taste as the figure for diminished inner discernment.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:11 Does not the ear test words as the palate tastes food ?

current.analysis_note: "does not the ear test words as the palate tastes food?" — ta.am as the image for inner evaluative perception

meaning.text: ta.am names taste as the figure for inner discernment — the ear's testing of words is like the palate's tasting of food. The inner faculty of discrimination applied to speech.

current.analysis_note: "the ear tests words as the palate tastes food" — same analogy; Elihu reiterates it; ta.am as the inner evaluative capacity

meaning.text: ta.am names the same figure as Job 12:11 — inner discrimination by ear is like the palate's tasting. The faculty of discernment is the connecting image.

current.analysis_note: "taste and see that the Lord is good" — ta.am as experiential inner apprehension; the invitation to direct inner experience of God's character

meaning.text: ta.am names inner discernment as the figure for experiencing God — tasting God's goodness is the inner act of personally testing and finding him good. The figurative use is central to the VCG.

current.analysis_note: "she perceives that her merchandise is profitable" — ta.am as commercial discernment; the virtuous woman's inner evaluation of her trade

meaning.text: ta.am names the inner discernment applied to commercial life — the capable wife's inner faculty for testing and evaluating applied to the profitability of her work.

current.analysis_note: "let neither man nor beast taste anything" — ta.am as what the fast refuses; inner engagement with normal sustenance withheld as an act of corporate inner posture before God

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:5 For God knows that when you eat of it your eyes will be opened , and you will be like God , knowing good and evil .”

meaning.text: ya.da names what the serpent attributes to God — divine self-knowledge of what the fruit does. The temptation is access to what God knows.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as moral knowledge guiding right action
new_vcg.description: The inner moral knowing that directs the righteous person — knowing what is acceptable, what is just, what is right. Its absence names moral blindness; its presence names moral clarity.
new_vcg.verse_annotation: the moral knowledge acquired through the fall

db.description: Self-knowledge of inner moral condition, guilt, capacity before God

new.title: ya.da as moral knowledge guiding right action
new.description: The inner moral knowing that directs the righteous person — knowing what is acceptable, what is just, what is right. Its absence names moral blindness; its presence names moral clarity.

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:7 Then the eyes of both were opened , and they knew that they were naked . And they sewed fig leaves together and made themselves loincloths .

current.analysis_note: "they knew that they were naked" — ya.da as consequence of the fall; transformative moral self-knowing

meaning.text: ya.da names the immediate experiential moral knowledge produced by eating — self-awareness of nakedness as the first fruit of the transgression.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as self-knowledge: the person's inner knowledge of their own condition
new_vcg.description: The person's inner knowing of their own moral state, situation, character, or experience. Includes both honest and distorted self-knowledge.

db.description: Moral knowledge guiding right action — knowing good, evil, justice

new.title: ya.da as self-knowledge: the person's inner knowledge of their own condition
new.description: The person's inner knowing of their own moral state, situation, character, or experience. Includes both honest and distorted self-knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:22 Then the Lord God said , “ Behold , the man has become like one of us in knowing good and evil . Now , lest he reach out his hand and take also of the tree of life and eat , and live forever —”

current.analysis_note: "the man has become like one of us in knowing good and evil" — ya.da as the newly acquired moral-ontological condition

meaning.text: ya.da names the moral knowledge now acquired — knowing good and evil as a divine-level capacity. The consequence is permanent inner transformation.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as moral knowledge guiding right action
new_vcg.description: The inner moral knowing that directs the righteous person — knowing what is acceptable, what is just, what is right. Its absence names moral blindness; its presence names moral clarity.
new_vcg.verse_annotation: the moral knowledge acquired through the fall
