new.description: The inner moral knowing that directs the righteous person — knowing what is acceptable, what is just, what is right. Its absence names moral blindness; its presence names moral clarity.

verses.verse_text: Gen 4:1 Now Adam knew Eve his wife , and she conceived and bore Cain , saying , “I have gotten a man with the help of the Lord .”
Gen 4:9 Then the Lord said to Cain , “ Where is Abel your brother ?” He said , “I do not know ; am I my brother’s keeper ?”

current.analysis_note: "I do not know; am I my brother's keeper?" — ya.da refused/denied in evasion; the inner knowing that Cain does have is suppressed

meaning.text: ya.da names Cain's claimed ignorance of Abel's whereabouts — a denial of relational knowledge and responsibility. The self-knowledge here is absent or suppressed.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as self-knowledge: the person's inner knowledge of their own condition
new_vcg.description: The person's inner knowing of their own moral state, situation, character, or experience. Includes both honest and distorted self-knowledge.

db.description: Self-knowledge of inner moral condition, guilt, capacity before God

new.title: ya.da as self-knowledge: the person's inner knowledge of their own condition
new.description: The person's inner knowing of their own moral state, situation, character, or experience. Includes both honest and distorted self-knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Gen 4:17 Cain knew his wife , and she conceived and bore Enoch . When he built a city , he called the name of the city after the name of his son , Enoch .
Gen 4:25 And Adam knew his wife again , and she bore a son and called his name Seth , for she said, “ God has appointed for me another offspring instead of Abel , for Cain killed him.”
Gen 8:11 And the dove came back to him in the evening , and behold , in her mouth was a freshly plucked olive leaf . So Noah knew that the waters had subsided from the earth .
Gen 9:24 When Noah awoke from his wine and knew what his youngest son had done to him,
Gen 12:11 When he was about to enter Egypt , he said to Sarai his wife , “ I know that you are a woman beautiful in appearance ,

meaning.text: ya.da names Abram's self-aware assessment of his situation — knowledge of a fact that produces anxiety and strategic deception.

verses.verse_text: Gen 15:8 But he said , “O Lord God , how am I to know that I shall possess it ?”

current.analysis_note: "how am I to know that I shall possess it?" — Abram seeking assurance from God; relational ya.da as the basis for trust

meaning.text: ya.da names Abram's seeking of covenantal assurance — a request for knowledge of God's promise that goes beyond verbal affirmation.

verses.verse_text: Gen 15:13 Then the Lord said to Abram , “ Know for certain that your offspring will be sojourners in a land that is not theirs and will be servants there, and they will be afflicted for four hundred years .

current.analysis_note: "Know for certain that your offspring will be sojourners" — divine communication to Abram; ya.da as the content of covenant revelation

meaning.text: ya.da names God's disclosure to Abram of what is coming — covenantal foreknowledge given as inner certainty.

verses.verse_text: Gen 18:19 For I have chosen him, that he may command his children and his household after him to keep the way of the Lord by doing righteousness and justice , so that the Lord may bring to Abraham what he has promised him .”

current.analysis_note: "I have chosen him / known him" — God's elective knowing of Abraham; covenantal knowing from the divine side

meaning.text: ya.da here is rendered "chosen" — God's knowing of Abraham is an election knowing, the covenantal intimacy that results in appointment and purpose.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as covenantal knowing between God and Israel: relational election knowing
new_vcg.description: The most intimate form of ya.da — God's elective knowing of Israel and Israel's knowing of God as the substance of the covenant relationship. Not merely informational but constitutively relational and moral.

db.description: Covenantal/relational knowing between God and Israel

new.title: ya.da as covenantal knowing between God and Israel: relational election knowing
new.description: The most intimate form of ya.da — God's elective knowing of Israel and Israel's knowing of God as the substance of the covenant relationship. Not merely informational but constitutively relational and moral.

verses.verse_text: Gen 18:21 I will go down to see whether they have done altogether according to the outcry that has come to me. And if not , I will know .”

meaning.text: ya.da names God's investigative knowing — not ignorance but the divine process of attending to and fully comprehending the situation.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as God's comprehensive knowing of persons: divine inner knowledge of the human
new_vcg.description: God's knowing of the person's inner life — motives, character, acts, plans, future tendencies. Comprehensive, attentive, penetrating.

db.description: God's comprehensive knowledge of the inner person — motives, character, inner states

new.title: ya.da as God's comprehensive knowing of persons: divine inner knowledge of the human
new.description: God's knowing of the person's inner life — motives, character, acts, plans, future tendencies. Comprehensive, attentive, penetrating.

verses.verse_text: Gen 19:5 And they called to Lot , “ Where are the men who came to you tonight ? Bring them out to us, that we may know them .”

Gen 19:8 Behold , I have two daughters who have not known any man . Let me bring them out to you, and do to them as you please . Only do nothing to these men , for they have come under the shelter of my roof .”
Gen 19:33 So they made their father drink wine that night . And the firstborn went in and lay with her father . He did not know when she lay down or when she arose .
Gen 19:35 So they made their father drink wine that night also . And the younger arose and lay with him, and he did not know when she lay down or when she arose .

Gen 20:6 Then God said to him in the dream , “ Yes , I know that you have done this in the integrity of your heart , and it was I who kept you from sinning against me. Therefore I did not let you touch her .

current.analysis_note: "yes, I know that you have done this in the integrity of your heart" — God's knowing of Abimelech's inner condition and motivation

meaning.text: ya.da names God's comprehensive inner knowledge of Abimelech's motives — the integrity of the heart is known to God.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as God's comprehensive knowing of persons: divine inner knowledge of the human
new_vcg.description: God's knowing of the person's inner life — motives, character, acts, plans, future tendencies. Comprehensive, attentive, penetrating.

db.description: God's comprehensive knowledge of the inner person — motives, character, inner states

new.title: ya.da as God's comprehensive knowing of persons: divine inner knowledge of the human
new.description: God's knowing of the person's inner life — motives, character, acts, plans, future tendencies. Comprehensive, attentive, penetrating.

verses.verse_text: Gen 20:7 Now then, return the man’s wife , for he is a prophet , so that he will pray for you , and you shall live . But if you do not return her, know that you shall surely die , you and all who are yours.”
Gen 21:26 Abimelech said , “I do not know who has done this thing ; you did not tell me, and I have not heard of it until today .”
Gen 22:12 He said , “Do not lay your hand on the boy or do anything to him, for now I know that you fear God , seeing you have not withheld your son , your only son, from me .”

current.analysis_note: "now I know that you fear God" — God's knowing through the test; relational-covenantal assessment

meaning.text: ya.da names God's knowing confirmed through action — the test has produced knowledge of Abraham's character. Covenantal knowing verified through obedience.

verses.verse_text: Gen 24:14 Let the young woman to whom I shall say , ‘ Please let down your jar that I may drink ,’ and who shall say , ‘ Drink , and I will water your camels ’—let her be the one whom you have appointed for your servant Isaac . By this I shall know that you have shown steadfast love to my master .”

current.analysis_note: "by this I shall know that you have shown steadfast love" — servant discerning divine appointment through covenant-love

meaning.text: ya.da names the knowledge of God's covenantal faithfulness that the sign will confirm — recognition of steadfast love through a particular event.

verses.verse_text: Gen 24:16 The young woman was very attractive in appearance , a maiden whom no man had known . She went down to the spring and filled her jar and came up .
Gen 24:21 The man gazed at her in silence to learn whether the Lord had prospered his journey or not .

current.analysis_note: "Joseph made himself known to his brothers" — relational knowing restored after alienation

meaning.text: ya.da names the discerning attention directed toward discovering God's will — attentive observation as a form of knowing.

verses.verse_text: Gen 25:27 When the boys grew up, Esau was a skillful hunter , a man of the field , while Jacob was a quiet man , dwelling in tents .
Gen 27:2 He said , “ Behold , I am old ; I do not know the day of my death .

current.analysis_note: "I do not know the day of my death" — Isaac's honest self-awareness of his epistemic limit

meaning.text: ya.da names Isaac's self-awareness of the limit of his knowledge — he knows he is old but not when he will die.

verses.verse_text: Gen 28:16 Then Jacob awoke from his sleep and said , “ Surely the Lord is in this place , and I did not know it.”

current.analysis_note: "the Lord is in this place, and I did not know it" — relational/theological knowing

meaning.text: ya.da names the absence and then presence of covenantal awareness — Jacob had not known God's presence in the place until the dream.

verses.verse_text: Gen 29:5 He said to them, “Do you know Laban the son of Nahor ?” They said , “We know him.”
Gen 30:26 Give me my wives and my children for whom I have served you, that I may go , for you know the service that I have given you .”
Gen 30:29 Jacob said to him, “ You yourself know how I have served you, and how your livestock has fared with me .
Gen 31:6 You know that I have served your father with all my strength ,
Gen 31:32 Anyone with whom you find your gods shall not live . In the presence of our kinsmen point out what I have that is yours, and take it.” Now Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them .
Gen 33:13 But Jacob said to him, “My lord knows that the children are frail , and that the nursing flocks and herds are a care to me. If they are driven hard for one day , all the flocks will die .
Gen 38:9 But Onan knew that the offspring would not be his. So whenever he went in to his brother’s wife he would waste the semen on the ground , so as not to give offspring to his brother .

current.analysis_note: "Onan knew that the offspring would not be his" — ya.da as the inner knowledge of moral situation that determines sinful action

meaning.text: ya.da names Onan's knowledge of the levirate situation — moral knowledge that guides (or in this case, subverts) right action.

verses.verse_text: Gen 38:16 He turned to her at the roadside and said , “ Come , let me come in to you,” for he did not know that she was his daughter-in-law . She said , “ What will you give me, that you may come in to me ?”
Gen 38:26 Then Judah identified them and said , “She is more righteous than I, since I did not give her to my son Shelah .” And he did not know her again .
Gen 39:6 So he left all that he had in Joseph’s charge , and because of him he had no concern about anything but the food he ate . Now Joseph was handsome in form and appearance .
Gen 39:8 But he refused and said to his master’s wife , “ Behold , because of me my master has no concern about anything in the house , and he has put everything that he has in my charge .
Gen 41:21 but when they had eaten them no one would have known that they had eaten them , for they were still as ugly as at the beginning . Then I awoke .
Gen 41:31 and the plenty will be unknown in the land by reason of the famine that will follow , for it will be very severe .
Gen 41:39 Then Pharaoh said to Joseph , “ Since God has shown you all this , there is none so discerning and wise as you are.

current.analysis_note: "God has shown you all this" — divine disclosure; God knowing and showing

meaning.text: ya.da names divine disclosure — God has shown Joseph, and this showing has produced knowledge beyond human capacity.

verses.verse_text: Gen 42:23 They did not know that Joseph understood them, for there was an interpreter between them .

Gen 42:33 Then the man , the lord of the land , said to us, ‘ By this I shall know that you are honest men: leave one of your brothers with me, and take grain for the famine of your households , and go your way.
Gen 42:34 Bring your youngest brother to me. Then I shall know that you are not spies but honest men, and I will deliver your brother to you, and you shall trade in the land .’”

Gen 43:7 They replied , “The man questioned us carefully about ourselves and our kindred , saying , ‘Is your father still alive ? Do you have another brother ?’ What we told him was in answer to these questions . Could we in any way know that he would say , ‘Bring your brother down ’?”
Gen 43:22 and we have brought other money down with us to buy food . We do not know who put our money in our sacks .”
Gen 44:15 Joseph said to them, “ What deed is this that you have done ? Do you not know that a man like me can indeed practice divination ?”
Gen 44:27 Then your servant my father said to us, ‘ You know that my wife bore me two sons.
Gen 45:1 Then Joseph could not control himself before all those who stood by him. He cried , “Make everyone go out from me.” So no one stayed with him when Joseph made himself known to his brothers .

meaning.text: ya.da names the relational self-disclosure — Joseph's revealing of himself as a covenantal act of reconciliation.

verses.verse_text: Gen 47:6 The land of Egypt is before you. Settle your father and your brothers in the best of the land . Let them settle in the land of Goshen , and if you know any able men among them, put them in charge of my livestock .”

Gen 48:19 But his father refused and said , “I know , my son , I know . He also shall become a people , and he also shall be great . Nevertheless , his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his offspring shall become a multitude of nations .”

current.analysis_note: "I know, my son, I know. He also shall become a people" — Jacob's inner certainty about the future; ya.da as settled conviction

meaning.text: ya.da names Jacob's prophetic self-knowledge — he knows what he is doing in crossing his hands. The knowing is inner conviction about what God has shown him.

verses.verse_text: Exo 1:8 Now there arose a new king over Egypt , who did not know Joseph .
Exo 2:4 And his sister stood at a distance to know what would be done to him .
Exo 2:14 He answered , “ Who made you a prince and a judge over us? Do you mean to kill me as you killed the Egyptian ?” Then Moses was afraid , and thought , “ Surely the thing is known .”
Exo 2:25 God saw the people of Israel —and God knew .

current.analysis_note: "God saw the people of Israel — and God knew" — divine covenantal knowing as the ground of redemption

meaning.text: ya.da names God's covenantal attending — the knowing is relational recognition of his covenant people in their suffering.

verses.verse_text: Exo 3:7 Then the Lord said , “I have surely seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt and have heard their cry because of their taskmasters . I know their sufferings ,

current.analysis_note: "I know their sufferings" — God's ya.da as the compassionate ground of redemptive action

meaning.text: ya.da names God's comprehensive inner knowledge of Israel's affliction — not distant observation but intimate knowing of their condition.

verses.verse_text: Exo 3:19 But I know that the king of Egypt will not let you go unless compelled by a mighty hand .

current.analysis_note: "I know that the king of Egypt will not let you go" — God's foreknowing of Pharaoh's resistance

meaning.text: ya.da names God's foreknowing of Pharaoh's resistance — inner knowledge of the king's character and what will be required.

verses.verse_text: Exo 4:14 Then the anger of the Lord was kindled against Moses and he said , “Is there not Aaron , your brother , the Levite ? I know that he can speak well . Behold, he is coming out to meet you, and when he sees you, he will be glad in his heart .

current.analysis_note: "I know that he can speak well" — God's knowing of Aaron's inner capacity

meaning.text: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of Aaron's capacity — inner knowledge of another person's gift.

verses.verse_text: Exo 5:2 But Pharaoh said , “Who is the Lord , that I should obey his voice and let Israel go ? I do not know the Lord , and moreover , I will not let Israel go .”

current.analysis_note: "I do not know the Lord" — relational knowing refused by Pharaoh

meaning.text: ya.da names Pharaoh's refusal of covenantal knowing — his denial of the Lord is both ignorance and defiance.

verses.verse_text: Num 10:31 And he said , “ Please do not leave us, for you know where we should camp in the wilderness , and you will serve as eyes for us.

Num 11:16 Then the Lord said to Moses , “ Gather for me seventy men of the elders of Israel , whom you know to be the elders of the people and officers over them, and bring them to the tent of meeting , and let them take their stand there with you .
Num 12:6 And he said , “ Hear my words : If there is a prophet among you, I the Lord make myself known to him in a vision ; I speak with him in a dream .

current.analysis_note: "I make myself known to him in a vision" — God's self-disclosure as the mode of prophetic knowing

meaning.text: ya.da names God's self-disclosure to prophets — revelatory knowing through vision and dream. The mode of God making himself known.

verses.verse_text: Num 14:31 But your little ones , who you said would become a prey , I will bring in , and they shall know the land that you have rejected .

meaning.text: ya.da names the experiential covenantal knowing of the land — the children will know it by inhabiting it, which the faithless generation refused.

verses.verse_text: Num 14:34 According to the number of the days in which you spied out the land , forty days , a year for each day , you shall bear your iniquity forty years , and you shall know my displeasure .’

meaning.text: ya.da names the experiential knowledge of God's judgment — the forty years will produce knowing of what it means to have God against them.

verses.verse_text: Num 16:5 and he said to Korah and all his company , “ In the morning the Lord will show who is his, and who is holy , and will bring him near to him. The one whom he chooses he will bring near to him .

meaning.text: ya.da names divine self-disclosure through vindicating action — God will make known who belongs to him.

verses.verse_text: Num 16:28 And Moses said , “ Hereby you shall know that the Lord has sent me to do all these works , and that it has not been of my own accord .

current.analysis_note: "hereby you shall know that the Lord has sent me" — ya.da through divine authentication of the prophet

meaning.text: ya.da names the knowledge of divine authority that the sign will produce — covenantal self-authentication through event.

verses.verse_text: Num 16:30 But if the Lord creates something new , and the ground opens its mouth and swallows them up with all that belongs to them, and they go down alive into Sheol , then you shall know that these men have despised the Lord .”

current.analysis_note: "you shall know that these men have despised the Lord" — divine act producing covenantal knowing

meaning.text: ya.da names the knowledge that the judgment will produce — the event makes the inner spiritual reality visible and knowable.

verses.verse_text: Num 20:14 Moses sent messengers from Kadesh to the king of Edom : “ Thus says your brother Israel : You know all the hardship that we have met :

Num 22:6 Come now , curse this people for me, since they are too mighty for me. Perhaps I shall be able to defeat them and drive them from the land , for I know that he whom you bless is blessed , and he whom you curse is cursed .”

meaning.text: ya.da names Balak's knowledge of Balaam's covenantal efficacy — recognition of the power God has given him.

verses.verse_text: Num 22:19 So you, too , please stay here tonight , that I may know what more the Lord will say to me .”

meaning.text: ya.da names Balaam's seeking of divine disclosure — waiting for God to make his will known.

verses.verse_text: Num 22:34 Then Balaam said to the angel of the Lord , “I have sinned , for I did not know that you stood in the road against me. Now therefore, if it is evil in your sight , I will turn back .”
Num 24:16 the oracle of him who hears the words of God , and knows the knowledge of the Most High , who sees the vision of the Almighty , falling down with his eyes uncovered :

current.analysis_note: 1506 | Same verse: "knows the knowledge of the Most High" — both ya.da (covenantal knowing) and da.at (divine knowledge) present; two separate entries

meaning.text: ya.da names the moral certainty being asserted — this is knowledge of moral consequence.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as moral knowledge guiding right action
new_vcg.description: The inner moral knowing that directs the righteous person — knowing what is acceptable, what is just, what is right. Its absence names moral blindness; its presence names moral clarity.

verses.verse_text: Judg 3:1 Now these are the nations that the Lord left , to test Israel by them, that is, all in Israel who had not experienced all the wars in Canaan .

meaning.text: ya.da names the knowledge that the testing will produce — covenantal obedience made visible and knowable.

verses.verse_text: Judg 6:37 behold , I am laying a fleece of wool on the threshing floor . If there is dew on the fleece alone , and it is dry on all the ground , then I shall know that you will save Israel by my hand , as you have said .”

Judg 13:21 The angel of the Lord appeared no more to Manoah and to his wife . Then Manoah knew that he was the angel of the Lord .

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal recognition produced by the ascending flame — seeing the divine act produces knowing of the divine identity.

Judg 16:9 Now she had men lying in ambush in an inner chamber . And she said to him, “The Philistines are upon you, Samson !” But he snapped the bowstrings , as a thread of flax snaps when it touches the fire . So the secret of his strength was not known .

Judg 16:20 And she said , “The Philistines are upon you, Samson !” And he awoke from his sleep and said , “I will go out as at other times and shake myself free .” But he did not know that the Lord had left him .

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal absence that Samson is unaware of — the Lord's departure is an inner spiritual reality he cannot perceive.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as Israel's failure to know God: broken or absent covenantal knowing

meaning.text: ya.da names the moral knowledge of what has happened — discerning the source of the sin through inquiry.

meaning.text: ya.da names divine disclosure of the specific act required — God will make known through the occasion what Samuel must do.

Neh 4:11 And our enemies said , “They will not know or see till we come among them and kill them and stop the work .”
Neh 4:15 When our enemies heard that it was known to us and that God had frustrated their plan , we all returned to the wall , each to his work .

Neh 6:16 And when all our enemies heard of it, all the nations around us were afraid and fell greatly in their own esteem , for they perceived that this work had been accomplished with the help of our God .

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of divine action — the enemies perceive God's involvement in the work.

meaning.text: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of Pharaoh's arrogance — inner covenantal knowing of the oppressor's character and act.

verses.verse_text: Neh 9:14 and you made known to them your holy Sabbath and commanded them commandments and statutes and a law by Moses your servant .

meaning.text: ya.da names God's revelatory self-disclosure — making the Sabbath known as a gift of divine self-revelation.

verses.verse_text: Neh 10:28 “The rest of the people , the priests , the Levites , the gatekeepers , the singers , the temple servants , and all who have separated themselves from the peoples of the lands to the Law of God , their wives , their sons , their daughters , all who have knowledge and understanding ,

meaning.text: ya.da names knowledge as a defining inner quality of those who separate themselves to the Law — the covenant community characterised by knowing.

But as for me, I have not been called to come in to the king these thirty days .”

Est 4:14 For if you keep silent at this time , relief and deliverance will rise for the Jews from another place , but you and your father’s house will perish . And who knows whether you have not come to the kingdom for such a time as this ?”

verses.verse_text: Job 5:24 You shall know that your tent is at peace , and you shall inspect your fold and miss nothing .

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal experiential knowledge of God's blessing — the restored person will know peace and security by experience.

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal experiential knowledge of God's fruitfulness — blessing known through lived experience.

meaning.text: ya.da names the knowledge being urged on Job — receiving the wisdom of experience as covenantal knowing.

meaning.text: ya.da names Job's inner conviction about how God will treat him — self-knowledge of his vulnerability.

meaning.text: ya.da names the wicked man's inner knowledge of his own peril — a dark self-awareness.

verses.verse_text: Job 19:6 know then that God has put me in the wrong and closed his net about me.

meaning.text: ya.da names the inner covenantal conviction Job urges — a recognition of his situation before God.

Job 19:14 My relatives have failed me, my close friends have forgotten me .
Job 19:25 For I know that my Redeemer lives , and at the last he will stand upon the earth .

current.analysis_note: "I know that my Redeemer lives" — ya.da as ultimate inner conviction

meaning.text: ya.da names the knowledge of divine judgment that the warning aims to produce — inner recognition of moral accountability.

verses.verse_text: Job 20:4 Do you not know this from of old , since man was placed on earth ,
Job 20:20 “ Because he knew no contentment in his belly , he will not let anything in which he delights escape him.
Job 21:19 You say, ‘ God stores up their iniquity for their children .’ Let him pay it out to them, that they may know it.

meaning.text: ya.da names the experiential knowing of God's judgment — recompense producing inner recognition of justice.

verses.verse_text: Job 21:27 “ Behold , I know your thoughts and your schemes to wrong me.

current.analysis_note: "I know your thoughts and your schemes to wrong me" — divine penetrating ya.da of human scheming

verses.verse_text: Job 22:13 But you say , ‘ What does God know ? Can he judge through the deep darkness ?

meaning.text: ya.da names the knowledge being denied to God — the challenge to divine omniscience by those who think he cannot see.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:3 Oh , that I knew where I might find him, that I might come even to his seat !

meaning.text: ya.da names the longed-for covenantal knowing of God's location — Job's agonised desire to encounter the God who seems hidden.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:5 I would know what he would answer me and understand what he would say to me .

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge Job seeks — hearing God's answer as the goal of his seeking.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:10 But he knows the way that I take ; when he has tried me, I shall come out as gold .

current.analysis_note: "he knows the way that I take; when he has tried me, I shall come out as gold" — God's ya.da of Job as the ground of Job's confidence

meaning.text: ya.da names God's comprehensive covenantal knowledge of Job's path — the comfort is that God knows even when Job cannot find him.

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal relationship of those who know God — those in relationship with him who cannot understand his timing.

meaning.text: ya.da names moral knowledge absent in the wicked — they do not know the light; their moral orientation is darkness.

meaning.text: ya.da names self-knowledge of one's own speech — Elihu's inner honesty about his incapacity for flattery.

meaning.text: ya.da names the knowledge of the wise — moral and experiential knowing that qualifies for hearing.

Job 36:26 Behold , God is great , and we know him not ; the number of his years is unsearchable .

meaning.text: ya.da names the divine purpose in sealing human hands in winter — all created persons are to know God's sovereign power through it.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:15 Do you know how God lays his command upon them and causes the lightning of his cloud to shine ?

meaning.text: ya.da names God's governing command — knowledge of how God directs natural phenomena is beyond human reach.

meaning.text: ya.da names Elihu's admission of human ignorance — he asks Job to teach what to say to God, knowing that they cannot approach unaided.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:23 If you turn at my reproof , behold , I will pour out my spirit to you; I will make my words known to you .

meaning.text: ya.da names Wisdom's self-disclosure — she will reveal her words to those who turn at her reproof. Covenantal disclosure.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:6 In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths .

meaning.text: ya.da names acknowledgment of God as the relational covenantal orienting — the knowing that shapes the whole direction of life.

verses.verse_text: Pro 4:1 Hear , O sons , a father’s instruction , and be attentive , that you may gain insight ,

meaning.text: ya.da names the knowing that comes from attentiveness — receiving what the father offers through focused receptivity.

verses.verse_text: Pro 4:19 The way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know over what they stumble .

meaning.text: ya.da names the absence of moral self-knowledge in the wicked — they cannot perceive their own stumbling.

verses.verse_text: Pro 5:6 she does not ponder the path of life ; her ways wander , and she does not know it.

meaning.text: ya.da names the absence of moral self-knowledge in folly personified — she wanders without perceiving it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 7:23 till an arrow pierces its liver ; as a bird rushes into a snare ; he does not know that it will cost him his life .

meaning.text: ya.da names the absence of moral foresight — the young man lacks the inner knowledge of consequence.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:9 Give instruction to a wise man , and he will be still wiser ; teach a righteous man, and he will increase in learning .

new_vcg.title: Wisdom as constituted inner quality of character: cha.kham as holistic inner endowment
new_vcg.description: Wisdom as a defining inner quality of the person — not a skill or technique but a whole-person orientation that shows in perception, speech, conduct, and relationships. The quality is positive and characterises those who are genuinely wise.

new.title: Wisdom as constituted inner quality of character: cha.kham as holistic inner endowment
new.description: Wisdom as a defining inner quality of the person — not a skill or technique but a whole-person orientation that shows in perception, speech, conduct, and relationships. The quality is positive and characterises those who are genuinely wise.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:13 The woman Folly is loud ; she is seductive and knows nothing .

meaning.text: ya.da names the total inner ignorance of folly — she has no knowledge; her noise is empty.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:18 But he does not know that the dead are there , that her guests are in the depths of Sheol .

meaning.text: ya.da names the fatal absence of moral knowledge — the person entering folly cannot perceive where it leads.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:9 Whoever walks in integrity walks securely , but he who makes his ways crooked will be found out .
Pro 10:32 The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable , but the mouth of the wicked , what is perverse .

meaning.text: ya.da names moral knowledge that guides speech — the righteous person's inner knowing of what is right shapes expression.

new_vcg.title: a.rum / a.rom: prudent inner disposition as perceptive self-governance
new_vcg.description: The inner quality of prudence in the sense of perceptive self-governance — seeing situations clearly and governing speech and conduct with care. Directionally neutral: serves righteousness (Proverbs uses) or deception (Gen 3:1, Job 15:5).

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:7 Leave the presence of a fool , for there you do not meet words of knowledge .

new_vcg.description: Knowledge as what the wise person holds inwardly — the inner capacity/content that Proverbs aims to produce, that the wise accumulate and guard, and that gives the person strength.

new.description: Knowledge as what the wise person holds inwardly — the inner capacity/content that Proverbs aims to produce, that the wise accumulate and guard, and that gives the person strength.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:10 The heart knows its own bitterness , and no stranger shares its joy .

meaning.text: ya.da names the heart's self-knowledge of its own inner states — bitterness and joy are known inwardly and cannot be fully shared.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:33 Wisdom rests in the heart of a man of understanding , but it makes itself known even in the midst of fools .

meaning.text: ya.da names the visibility of wisdom — even among fools, wisdom makes itself known, though the fools cannot receive it.

new_vcg.description: Understanding as a substantive inner quality of the person — what they are characterised by. verses.verse_text: Pro 22:19 That your trust may be in the Lord , I have made them known to you today , even to you .

meaning.text: ya.da names the teacher's disclosure — making known the thirty sayings as a covenantal transmission of wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:21 to make you know what is right and true , that you may give a true answer to those who sent you ?

meaning.text: ya.da names moral knowing as the goal — the sayings aim to produce inner knowledge of what is right.

verses.verse_text: Pro 23:35 “They struck me,” you will say, “but I was not hurt ; they beat me, but I did not feel it. When shall I awake ? I must have another drink.”

Pro 24:12 If you say , “ Behold , we did not know this ,” does not he who weighs the heart perceive it? Does not he who keeps watch over your soul know it, and will he not repay man according to his work ?

meaning.text: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowing of the inner person — the soul-keeper knows what the person claims not to know.

new_vcg.title: God's perceptive comprehension of the human person: bin / bi.nah as divine knowing
new_vcg.description: God's inner perceptive faculty directed toward the human person — his understanding of thoughts, deeds, plans, and the heart. Divine understanding of the human is comprehensive and intimate.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:14 Know that wisdom is such to your soul ; if you find it, there will be a future , and your hope will not be cut off .

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal inner knowing of wisdom's value — held in the soul as certain hope.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:22 for disaster will arise suddenly from them, and who knows the ruin that will come from them both ?
Pro 27:1 Do not boast about tomorrow , for you do not know what a day may bring .
Pro 27:23 Know well the condition of your flocks , and give attention to your herds ,
Pro 28:2 When a land transgresses , it has many rulers , but with a man of understanding and knowledge , its stability will long continue .

meaning.text: ya.da names moral knowledge as qualification for good governance — the inner capacity that produces stable rule.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:22 A stingy man hastens after wealth and does not know that poverty will come upon him .

meaning.text: ya.da names the absence of moral foresight in the stingy man — he cannot perceive the consequence of his greed.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:7 A righteous man knows the rights of the poor ; a wicked man does not understand such knowledge .

meaning.text: ya.da names the moral knowledge of justice — the righteous person's inner knowing extends to the claims of the vulnerable.

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of the Holy One that Agur lacks — relational inner knowing of God.

new.description: Knowledge as what the wise person holds inwardly — the inner capacity/content that Proverbs aims to produce, that the wise accumulate and guard, and that gives the person strength.

verses.verse_text: Pro 30:4 Who has ascended to heaven and come down ? Who has gathered the wind in his fists ? Who has wrapped up the waters in a garment ? Who has established all the ends of the earth ? What is his name , and what is his son’s name ? Surely you know !
Pro 30:18 Three things are too wonderful for me; four I do not understand :
Pro 31:23 Her husband is known in the gates when he sits among the elders of the land .
Ecc 1:17 And I applied my heart to know wisdom and to know madness and folly . I perceived that this also is but a striving after wind .

current.analysis_note: "I applied my heart to know wisdom and to know madness and folly" — the Preacher's searching ya.da directed at all human experience

meaning.text: ya.da names the deliberate inner pursuit of knowing — turning the heart toward wisdom and its opposites as a quest for self-knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:14 The wise person has his eyes in his head , but the fool walks in darkness . And yet I perceived that the same event happens to all of them.

meaning.text: ya.da names the inner recognition of a pattern — Qoheleth's self-knowing of a bitter truth about mortality.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:19 and who knows whether he will be wise or a fool ? Yet he will be master of all for which I toiled and used my wisdom under the sun . This also is vanity .

new_vcg.title: Wisdom as constituted inner quality of character: cha.kham as holistic inner endowment
new_vcg.description: Wisdom as a defining inner quality of the person — not a skill or technique but a whole-person orientation that shows in perception, speech, conduct, and relationships. The quality is positive and characterises those who are genuinely wise.

new.title: Wisdom as constituted inner quality of character: cha.kham as holistic inner endowment
new.description: Wisdom as a defining inner quality of the person — not a skill or technique but a whole-person orientation that shows in perception, speech, conduct, and relationships. The quality is positive and characterises those who are genuinely wise.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 3:12 I perceived that there is nothing better for them than to be joyful and to do good as long as they live ;

current.analysis_note: "I perceived that there is nothing better than to be joyful" — Qoheleth's inner knowledge of the good life

meaning.text: ya.da names the inner recognition of a practical wisdom — what Qoheleth has come to know about human flourishing.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 3:14 I perceived that whatever God does endures forever ; nothing can be added to it, nor anything taken from it. God has done it, so that people fear before him.

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's permanence — Qoheleth's inner knowing of divine sovereignty.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 3:21 Who knows whether the spirit of man goes upward and the spirit of the beast goes down into the earth ?

meaning.text: ya.da names the limit of self-knowledge before death — no one can know what happens to the spirit.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as limits of human knowing before divine mystery
new_vcg.description: Human ya.da encountering its own limits before God's work, purposes, and nature — cannot comprehend, cannot find, cannot know the end or the route.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 4:13 Better was a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king who no longer knew how to take advice .

meaning.text: ya.da names the moral knowledge of one's own limitation — the king could no longer know how to receive counsel.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 5:1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God . To draw near to listen is better than to offer the sacrifice of fools , for they do not know that they are doing evil .

meaning.text: ya.da names the absent inner moral knowledge of the thoughtless worshipper — they cannot perceive their own wrongdoing.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 6:5 Moreover , it has not seen the sun or known anything , yet it finds rest rather than he.
Ecc 6:8 For what advantage has the wise man over the fool ? And what does the poor man have who knows how to conduct himself before the living ?

Ecc 6:12 For who knows what is good for man while he lives the few days of his vain life , which he passes like a shadow ? For who can tell man what will be after him under the sun ?
Ecc 7:22 Your heart knows that many times you yourself have cursed others .

current.analysis_note: "your heart knows that you yourself have cursed others" — heart-knowing of self-contradictory moral behavior

meaning.text: ya.da names the heart's inner self-knowledge of its own failures — the honest inner recognition that one has done what one is condemning.

meaning.text: ya.da names the deliberate inner quest — turning the heart toward knowing as a comprehensive life pursuit.

Ecc 8:12 Though a sinner does evil a hundred times and prolongs his life, yet I know that it will be well with those who fear God , because they fear before him.

meaning.text: ya.da names the deliberate inner turning toward knowing — the heart applied as a means of pursuit.

Ecc 9:12 For man does not know his time . Like fish that are taken in an evil net , and like birds that are caught in a snare , so the children of man are snared at an evil time , when it suddenly falls upon them.

meaning.text: ya.da names the inner ignorance of one's own end — a universal human condition.

Ecc 10:15 The toil of a fool wearies him, for he does not know the way to the city .
Ecc 11:2 Give a portion to seven , or even to eight , for you know not what disaster may happen on earth .

verses.verse_text: Isa 1:3 The ox knows its owner , and the donkey its master’s crib , but Israel does not know , my people do not understand .”

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal knowing Israel lacks — even animals recognise their master; Israel does not recognise God.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as Israel's failure to know God: broken or absent covenantal knowing
new_vcg.description: Israel's characteristic failure to know God — receiving his gifts without recognising the giver; claiming to know while pursuing other gods; the people who do not perceive.

db.description: Covenantal/relational knowing between God and Israel

new.title: ya.da as Israel's failure to know God: broken or absent covenantal knowing
new.description: Israel's characteristic failure to know God — receiving his gifts without recognising the giver; claiming to know while pursuing other gods; the people who do not perceive.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:5 And now I will tell you what I will do to my vineyard . I will remove its hedge , and it shall be devoured ; I will break down its wall , and it shall be trampled down .
Isa 5:19 who say : “Let him be quick , let him speed his work that we may see it; let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel draw near , and let it come , that we may know it!”

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of God's counsel being sought ironically — the mockers demand what they refuse to receive.

verses.verse_text: Isa 6:9 And he said , “ Go , and say to this people : “‘Keep on hearing , but do not understand ; keep on seeing , but do not perceive .’

current.analysis_note: "keep on seeing, but do not perceive" — ya.da withheld as judicial consequence

meaning.text: ya.da names the judicial withholding of moral knowing — the people will see but their inner perception of meaning will not be activated.

new_vcg.title: Judicial withholding of understanding: suniēmi / bin as blocked comprehension
new_vcg.description: Understanding deliberately withheld by God — the inner faculty is present but its activation is prevented by divine judgment. Hearing without receiving; perception without comprehension.

db.description: Moral knowledge guiding right action — knowing good, evil, justice

new.title: Judicial withholding of understanding: suniēmi / bin as blocked comprehension
new.description: Understanding deliberately withheld by God — the inner faculty is present but its activation is prevented by divine judgment. Hearing without receiving; perception without comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Isa 7:15 He shall eat curds and honey when he knows how to refuse the evil and choose the good .

current.analysis_note: "when he knows how to refuse the evil and choose the good" — moral ya.da as developmental threshold of personhood

meaning.text: ya.da names the moral knowing of good and evil — a developmental inner capacity.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as moral knowledge guiding right action
new_vcg.description: The inner moral knowing that directs the righteous person — knowing what is acceptable, what is just, what is right. Its absence names moral blindness; its presence names moral clarity.

db.description: Moral knowledge guiding right action — knowing good, evil, justice

new.title: ya.da as moral knowledge guiding right action
new.description: The inner moral knowing that directs the righteous person — knowing what is acceptable, what is just, what is right. Its absence names moral blindness; its presence names moral clarity.

verses.verse_text: Isa 7:16 For before the boy knows how to refuse the evil and choose the good , the land whose two kings you dread will be deserted .

current.analysis_note: "before the boy knows how to refuse evil and choose good" — same developmental marker

meaning.text: ya.da names the same moral knowing as a developmental marker — before it is reached, the sign will be fulfilled.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as moral knowledge guiding right action

new.title: ya.da as moral knowledge guiding right action
new.description: The inner moral knowing that directs the righteous person — knowing what is acceptable, what is just, what is right. Its absence names moral blindness; its presence names moral clarity.

verses.verse_text: Isa 8:4 for before the boy knows how to cry ‘My father ’ or ‘ My mother ,’ the wealth of Damascus and the spoil of Samaria will be carried away before the king of Assyria .”
Isa 9:9 and all the people will know , Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria , who say in pride and in arrogance of heart :

meaning.text: ya.da names the knowledge that judgment will produce — the people will know their situation, though they are currently boasting in arrogance.

verses.verse_text: Isa 12:4 And you will say in that day : “Give thanks to the Lord , call upon his name , make known his deeds among the peoples , proclaim that his name is exalted .

meaning.text: ya.da names the transmission of covenantal knowledge — proclaiming among the nations what God has done.

verses.verse_text: Isa 12:5 “Sing praises to the Lord , for he has done gloriously ; let this be made known in all the earth .

meaning.text: ya.da names the universal disclosure of God's glorious acts — covenantal self-disclosure through proclamation.

verses.verse_text: Isa 19:12 Where then are your wise men? Let them tell you that they might know what the Lord of hosts has purposed against Egypt .

new_vcg.title: Divine counsel: God's sovereign purposive intent directed toward nations and history
new_vcg.description: God forms and executes sovereign intention that cannot be annulled or redirected. His counsel is self-originating, irresistible, and encompasses the whole of history. Multiple human terms (ya.ats, cha.shav, za.mam) converge in expressing this.

new.title: Divine counsel: God's sovereign purposive intent directed toward nations and history
new.description: God forms and executes sovereign intention that cannot be annulled or redirected. His counsel is self-originating, irresistible, and encompasses the whole of history. Multiple human terms (ya.ats, cha.shav, za.mam) converge in expressing this.

verses.verse_text: Isa 19:21 And the Lord will make himself known to the Egyptians , and the Egyptians will know the Lord in that day and worship with sacrifice and offering , and they will make vows to the Lord and perform them.

current.analysis_note: "the Lord will make himself known to the Egyptians, and the Egyptians will know the Lord" — eschatological revelatory knowing extending to the nations

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal self-disclosure of God to a foreign nation and their resulting knowing — a remarkable eschatological extension of covenant knowing.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as the "then you shall know" pattern: divine self-disclosure through event producing recognition
new_vcg.description: A repeated pattern across the OT: God acts, and the purpose of the action is that persons will know — know that he is the Lord, that he is present, that he sent his messenger. The knowing is produced by the event, not by the seeker.

db.description: God's revelatory self-disclosure — his acts, name, and purposes made known that the human person may come to recognise, receive, and respond inwardly to God's sovereign purposes.

new.title: ya.da as the "then you shall know" pattern: divine self-disclosure through event producing recognition
new.description: A repeated pattern across the OT: God acts, and the purpose of the action is that persons will know — know that he is the Lord, that he is present, that he sent his messenger. The knowing is produced by the event, not by the seeker.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:11 And the vision of all this has become to you like the words of a book that is sealed . When men give it to one who can read , saying , “ Read this,” he says , “I cannot , for it is sealed .”
Isa 29:12 And when they give the book to one who cannot read , saying , “ Read this ,” he says , “I cannot read .”
Isa 29:15 Ah , you who hide deep from the Lord your counsel , whose deeds are in the dark , and who say , “ Who sees us? Who knows us ?”

meaning.text: ya.da names the divine knowing they deny — those who hide from God claim he cannot know their deeds, which is precisely what God knows.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:24 And those who go astray in spirit will come to understanding , and those who murmur will accept instruction .”

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of God that restoration produces — the erring will come to know.

verses.verse_text: Isa 32:4 The heart of the hasty will understand and know , and the tongue of the stammerers will hasten to speak distinctly .

meaning.text: ya.da names the moral knowing that will come in the renewal — the hasty heart will finally perceive rightly.

verses.verse_text: Isa 33:13 Hear , you who are far off , what I have done ; and you who are near , acknowledge my might .

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's power — those near are called to inner acknowledgment of his acts.

verses.verse_text: Isa 37:20 So now , O Lord our God , save us from his hand , that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you alone are the Lord .”

meaning.text: ya.da names the universal covenantal recognition that Hezekiah's prayer aims for — all nations knowing God's uniqueness through his deliverance.

meaning.text: ya.da names the intergenerational transmission of covenantal knowing — parents passing on knowledge of God's faithfulness.

current.analysis_note: "do you not know? do you not hear?" — appeal to existing revelatory knowing from creation

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge that should have been received from the beginning — Israel has had access to this knowing through tradition.

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's creative acts — seeing, knowing, considering, understanding together as the response to divine action.

Isa 41:23 Tell us what is to come hereafter , that we may know that you are gods ; do good , or do harm , that we may be dismayed and terrified .

Isa 41:26 Who declared it from the beginning , that we might know , and beforehand , that we might say , “He is right ”? There was none who declared it, none who proclaimed , none who heard your words .

Isa 42:16 And I will lead the blind in a way that they do not know , in paths that they have not known I will guide them. I will turn the darkness before them into light , the rough places into level ground . These are the things I do , and I do not forsake them .
Isa 42:25 So he poured on him the heat of his anger and the might of battle ; it set him on fire all around , but he did not understand ; it burned him up, but he did not take it to heart .

Isa 43:10 “ You are my witnesses ,” declares the Lord , “and my servant whom I have chosen , that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed , nor shall there be any after me.

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of God's identity as the goal of Israel's witness — knowing that God is who he is, uniquely.

verses.verse_text: Isa 43:19 Behold , I am doing a new thing ; now it springs forth , do you not perceive it? I will make a way in the wilderness and rivers in the desert .

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal perception of what God is doing — the new thing requires inner perception to be received.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:8 Fear not , nor be afraid ; have I not told you from of old and declared it? And you are my witnesses ! Is there a God besides me? There is no Rock ; I know not any.”

meaning.text: ya.da names God's self-knowledge — his inner knowing finds no other God. The divine self-knowledge is the ground of monotheism.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:9 All who fashion idols are nothing , and the things they delight in do not profit . Their witnesses neither see nor know , that they may be put to shame .

meaning.text: ya.da names the moral blindness of idol-witnesses — they cannot perceive or know the truth.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:18 They know not , nor do they discern , for he has shut their eyes , so that they cannot see , and their hearts , so that they cannot understand .

meaning.text: ya.da names the absent moral knowing of idol-worshippers — God has shut their hearts.

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal knowing Cyrus lacks — God calls him by name without the relationship of knowing.

meaning.text: ya.da names the same absence of covenantal knowing — Cyrus is equipped without the relationship.

meaning.text: ya.da names the absent moral knowing of idol-carriers — the one who prays to a god that cannot save has no inner knowledge.

meaning.text: ya.da names Babylon's self-deceiving inner certainty — she claims she will not experience what she in fact will experience.

verses.verse_text: Isa 47:11 But evil shall come upon you, which you will not know how to charm away ; disaster shall fall upon you, for which you will not be able to atone ; and ruin shall come upon you suddenly , of which you know nothing .

meaning.text: ya.da names the absent inner knowledge of how to avert disaster — Babylon will be helpless.

verses.verse_text: Isa 47:13 You are wearied with your many counsels ; let them stand forth and save you, those who divide the heavens , who gaze at the stars , who at the new moons make known what shall come upon you .
Isa 48:4 Because I know that you are obstinate , and your neck is an iron sinew and your forehead brass ,

meaning.text: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of Israel's character — inner knowing of their stubbornness as the reason for the way he addressed them.

new_vcg.title: Evaluative regard of persons and things: assigning worth or category by reckoning
new_vcg.description: One person (human or divine) evaluates another person, group, or thing by reckoning — assigning them a worth, category, or status. The reckoning is relational and evaluative, not judicial-moral as in VCG-D-01. Includes both positive and negative assessments, and both legitimate and contested evaluations.

db.description: Self-knowledge of inner moral condition, guilt, capacity before God

meaning.text: ya.da names the absent self-knowledge of Ephraim — he cannot perceive his own decline.

meaning.text: ya.da names Israel's claimed covenantal knowing — a claim made while pursuing other gods. The knowing they claim is hollow.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as covenantal knowing between God and Israel: relational election knowing
new_vcg.description: The most intimate form of ya.da — God's elective knowing of Israel and Israel's knowing of God as the substance of the covenant relationship. Not merely informational but constitutively relational and moral.

verses.verse_text: Hos 8:4 They made kings , but not through me. They set up princes , but I knew it not. With their silver and gold they made idols for their own destruction .

meaning.text: ya.da names God's non-acknowledgment — he did not know it, meaning he did not sanction or recognise it.

verses.verse_text: Hos 9:7 The days of punishment have come ; the days of recompense have come ; Israel shall know it. The prophet is a fool ; the man of the spirit is mad , because of your great iniquity and great hatred .

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of judgment that Israel will receive — they will know the days of punishment by experiencing them.

verses.verse_text: Hos 11:3 Yet it was I who taught Ephraim to walk; I took them up by their arms , but they did not know that I healed them .

current.analysis_note: "they did not know that I healed them" — Israel's failure to know the Lord's acts of care

meaning.text: ya.da names the absent covenantal recognition of God's care — the father taught and healed, but was not known as the one doing it.

new_vcg.description: The most intimate form of ya.da — God's elective knowing of Israel and Israel's knowing of God as the substance of the covenant relationship. Not merely informational but constitutively relational and moral.

verses.verse_text: Hos 13:4 But I am the Lord your God from the land of Egypt ; you know no God but me, and besides me there is no savior .

current.analysis_note: "you know no God but me, and besides me there is no savior" — exclusive covenantal knowing

meaning.text: ya.da names the exclusive covenantal knowing — Israel's only knowing of God is in relation to the one who delivered them from Egypt.

verses.verse_text: Hos 13:5 It was I who knew you in the wilderness , in the land of drought ;

current.analysis_note: "It was I who knew you in the wilderness" — God's knowing Israel in the desert; covenantal intimacy

meaning.text: ya.da names God's covenantal election knowing of Israel in their most vulnerable place — intimate relational knowing in the desert.

verses.verse_text: Hos 14:9 Whoever is wise , let him understand these things; whoever is discerning , let him know them; for the ways of the Lord are right , and the upright walk in them, but transgressors stumble in them .

current.analysis_note: "whoever is discerning, let him know them" — knowing the ways of the Lord as the mark of moral discernment

meaning.text: ya.da names the moral knowing of God's ways as what discernment produces — right perception leads to knowing.

new_vcg.title: Wisdom as constituted inner quality of character: cha.kham as holistic inner endowment
new_vcg.description: Wisdom as a defining inner quality of the person — not a skill or technique but a whole-person orientation that shows in perception, speech, conduct, and relationships. The quality is positive and characterises those who are genuinely wise.

verses.verse_text: Joe 2:14 Who knows whether he will not turn and relent , and leave a blessing behind him, a grain offering and a drink offering for the Lord your God ?

meaning.text: ya.da names the divine freedom before human action — no one can claim to know what God will do; he is not compelled.

verses.verse_text: Joe 2:27 You shall know that I am in the midst of Israel , and that I am the Lord your God and there is none else . And my people shall never again be put to shame .

current.analysis_note: "you shall know that I am in the midst of Israel, and that I am the Lord your God" — covenantal knowing as the content of divine self-disclosure

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of God's presence produced by restoration.

verses.verse_text: Joe 3:17 “So you shall know that I am the Lord your God , who dwells in Zion , my holy mountain . And Jerusalem shall be holy , and strangers shall never again pass through it.

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's dwelling — knowing him in his holy mountain.

verses.verse_text: Amo 3:2 “You only have I known of all the families of the earth ; therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities .

current.analysis_note: "You only have I known of all the families of the earth" — God's elective knowing of Israel; ya.da as covenantal election

meaning.text: ya.da names God's elective covenantal knowing of Israel — uniquely known, uniquely accountable. One of the defining statements of election as knowing.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as covenantal knowing between God and Israel: relational election knowing

verses.verse_text: Amo 3:10 “They do not know how to do right ,” declares the Lord , “those who store up violence and robbery in their strongholds .”

meaning.text: ya.da names the absent moral knowledge of the unjust — they lack the inner knowing of what right conduct is.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as moral knowledge guiding right action
new_vcg.description: The inner moral knowing that directs the righteous person — knowing what is acceptable, what is just, what is right. Its absence names moral blindness; its presence names moral clarity.

verses.verse_text: Amo 5:12 For I know how many are your transgressions and how great are your sins — you who afflict the righteous , who take a bribe , and turn aside the needy in the gate .

meaning.text: ya.da names God's comprehensive moral knowing of Israel's sin — specific and complete.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as God's comprehensive knowing of persons: divine inner knowledge of the human
new_vcg.description: God's knowing of the person's inner life — motives, character, acts, plans, future tendencies. Comprehensive, attentive, penetrating.

verses.verse_text: Amo 5:16 Therefore thus says the Lord , the God of hosts , the Lord : “ In all the squares there shall be wailing , and in all the streets they shall say , ‘ Alas ! Alas !’ They shall call the farmers to mourning and to wailing those who are skilled in lamentation ,
Jon 1:7 And they said to one another , “ Come , let us cast lots , that we may know on whose account this evil has come upon us.” So they cast lots , and the lot fell on Jonah .
Jon 1:10 Then the men were exceedingly afraid and said to him, “ What is this that you have done !” For the men knew that he was fleeing from the presence of the Lord , because he had told them .

meaning.text: ya.da names the pagan sailors' recognition of Jonah's situation — they come to covenantal knowledge of the Lord through the events.

verses.verse_text: Jon 1:12 He said to them , “ Pick me up and hurl me into the sea ; then the sea will quiet down for you, for I know it is because of me that this great tempest has come upon you .”

meaning.text: ya.da names Jonah's self-knowledge of his own culpability — settled inner recognition of his responsibility.

verses.verse_text: Jon 3:9 Who knows ? God may turn and relent and turn from his fierce anger , so that we may not perish .”

meaning.text: ya.da names the open covenantal possibility — no one can know for certain what God will do; the appeal to him is worth making.

verses.verse_text: Jon 4:2 And he prayed to the Lord and said , “ O Lord , is not this what I said when I was yet in my country ? That is why I made haste to flee to Tarshish ; for I knew that you are a gracious God and merciful , slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love , and relenting from disaster .

meaning.text: ya.da names Jonah's covenantal knowledge of God's character — the knowing that motivated his flight.

verses.verse_text: Jon 4:11 And should not I pity Nineveh , that great city , in which there are more than 120,000 persons who do not know their right hand from their left , and also much cattle ?”

meaning.text: ya.da names moral immaturity — the Ninevites who cannot distinguish right from left lack the inner moral knowing that would make them accountable.

meaning.text: ya.da names the moral knowledge of justice as the obligation of leaders — they should know it; their failure to practise it is not ignorance but refusal.

meaning.text: ya.da names the absent covenantal knowledge of God's purposes — the nations cannot perceive what God is doing.

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of God's saving history that remembrance produces — recalling Balak and Balaam produces recognition of God's faithfulness.

meaning.text: ya.da names the appeal for divine self-disclosure — Habakkuk asks God to make his work known again in the current time.

meaning.text: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of divine mission that the act will produce.

new_vcg.title: ya.da as the "then you shall know" pattern: divine self-disclosure through event producing recognition
new_vcg.description: A repeated pattern across the OT: God acts, and the purpose of the action is that persons will know — know that he is the Lord, that he is present, that he sent his messenger. The knowing is produced by the event, not by the seeker.

verses.verse_text: Exo 18:19 Now obey my voice ; I will give you advice , and God be with you ! You shall represent the people before God and bring their cases to God ,

current.analysis_note: "I will give you advice, and God be with you" — Jethro's counsel to Moses; ya.ats as deliberate advice from a wise person

meaning.text: ya.ats names counsel given between persons — Jethro offers wisdom to Moses for structuring governance. The counsel is grounded in the advisor's inner orientation and shapes the recipient's.

new_vcg.title: Human counsel: wisdom given between persons shaping deliberation
new_vcg.description: One person gives advice or counsel to another — the inner wisdom of the advisor expressed and offered to shape the recipient's purposes. The outcome depends on whether counsel is received or rejected.

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:1 Now the serpent was more crafty than any other beast of the field that the Lord God had made . He said to the woman , “Did God actually say , ‘You shall not eat of any tree in the garden ’?”

current.analysis_note: "the serpent was more crafty" — a.rum in its dark register; the same shrewdness serving deception

meaning.text: a.rum names craftiness as the serpent's defining inner disposition — perceptive cunning directed entirely toward deception. The same inner quality that is valued as prudence is here the instrument of the fall.

current.analysis_note: — | Also appears in M15-A (tu.shiy.yah, same verse)

verses.verse_text: Jon 1:6 So the captain came and said to him, “ What do you mean, you sleeper ? Arise , call out to your god ! Perhaps the god will give a thought to us, that we may not perish .”

current.analysis_note: "perhaps the god will give a thought to us, that we may not perish" — a.shat as the hoped-for divine purposive attention; the captain petitioning for divine deliberate regard

meaning.text: a.shat names the hoped-for divine inner attending — the captain appeals for the god to direct thought toward the crew in their danger. The appeal is for purposive divine attention to their need.

new_vcg.title: The appeal for divine purposive attending: a.shat as hope for divine attention in crisis
new_vcg.description: A single verse. The captain appeals for the god to give thought to the crew — the hope that divine inner purposing will be directed toward those in need of rescue.

db.description: The hope and cry that God would direct his thought toward persons in their need — the inner appeal for divine attention as a response to crisis (Jon 1:6).

verses.verse_text: Psa 146:4 When his breath departs , he returns to the earth ; on that very day his plans perish .

current.analysis_note: 'when his breath departs, he returns to the earth; on that very day his plans perish' — esh.to.nah as inner plans/thoughts whose existence is entirely dependent on the living breath; at death, they vanish

verses.verse_text: Psa 139:2 You know when I sit down and when I rise up ; you discern my thoughts from afar .

current.analysis_note: 'you discern my thoughts from afar' — re.a as human inner thought-content fully known to God even at distance

meaning.text: re.a names the psalmist's inner thoughts as what God discerns from afar — the formed inner content of the person's mind is fully transparent to divine knowing.

new_vcg.title: Inner thoughts known by God and precious to the psalmist: re.a
new_vcg.description: The psalmist's thoughts are transparent to God, discerned from afar. God's own thoughts are precious and vast beyond counting. The same word applies in both directions, creating a symmetry of mutual thought-content.

new.title: Inner thoughts known by God and precious to the psalmist: re.a
new.description: The psalmist's thoughts are transparent to God, discerned from afar. God's own thoughts are precious and vast beyond counting. The same word applies in both directions, creating a symmetry of mutual thought-content.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:29 To you , O king , as you lay in bed came thoughts of what would be after this , and he who reveals mysteries made known to you what is to be .

current.analysis_note: 'as you lay in bed came thoughts of what would be after this' — ra.yon as thoughts arising spontaneously during sleep; the context of royal anxiety about the future

meaning.text: ra.yon names Nebuchadnezzar's inner thoughts arising in the night — the formed thought-content produced by the king's mind in sleep, which produced the dream.

new_vcg.title: Thoughts alarmed by revelatory dream-visions: ra.yon
new_vcg.description: Inner thought-content produced in response to divine revelation through dreams or visions — in every case the ra.yon disturbs or alarms the person receiving or hearing of it. Includes the thoughts the queen counsels the king not to allow to master him.

db.description: Thoughts alarmed by revelation — the inner mental content produced by dream-visions that disturbs the person's inner state, including the thoughts the king is urged not to let alarm him and Daniel's thoughts kept in the heart

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:30 But as for me , this mystery has been revealed to me, not because of any wisdom that I have more than all the living , but in order that the interpretation may be made known to the king , and that you may know the thoughts of your mind .

current.analysis_note: 'that you may know the thoughts of your mind' — ra.yon of the le.vav (heart/mind); the king's formed inner thoughts about what will come

meaning.text: ra.yon names the king's mind-thoughts as what the revelation will make known — the formed inner content of the royal mind is the object of the interpretation.

verses.verse_text: Dan 4:19 Then Daniel , whose name was Belteshazzar , was dismayed for a while , and his thoughts alarmed him. The king answered and said , “ Belteshazzar , let not the dream or the interpretation alarm you.” Belteshazzar answered and said , “My lord , may the dream be for those who hate you and its interpretation for your enemies !

current.analysis_note: 'his thoughts alarmed him' — ra.yon producing an inner alarm state in Daniel before he speaks; the thought-content is emotionally active

meaning.text: ra.yon names Daniel's formed inner thoughts in response to the dream — the thought-content arising in his mind at the revelation alarms him.

meaning.text: ra.yon names Daniel's inner thoughts produced by the vision — the formed thought-content of the revelation that disturbs and must be held inwardly.

verses.verse_text: Amo 4:13 For behold , he who forms the mountains and creates the wind , and declares to man what is his thought , who makes the morning darkness , and treads on the heights of the earth — the Lord , the God of hosts , is his name !

current.analysis_note: 'declares to man what is his thought' — God revealing the content of human inner thought-life; the inner is made transparent by divine declaration

meaning.text: se.ach names the divine knowledge of human thought — God knowing and declaring what is in the person's inner meditative life. The creative God who forms mountains also knows the inner thought of the creature.

new_vcg.title: God declaring the inner thought of the human person: se.ach

1Ch 16:9 Sing to him, sing praises to him; tell of all his wondrous works !

current.analysis_note: 'tell of all his wondrous works' — si.ach as doxological proclamation; inner musing on God's works made into communal telling

meaning.text: si.ach names the expressive telling of God's wondrous acts — the inner musing poured outward in proclamation. The meditative attending to God's works produces the speech of praise.

new.description: Understanding as a property of God himself — the attribute by which he made the heavens and subdued chaos. Self-originating and infinite.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:19 No one considers , nor is there knowledge or discernment to say , “ Half of it I burned in the fire ; I also baked bread on its coals ; I roasted meat and have eaten . And shall I make the rest of it an abomination ? Shall I fall down before a block of wood ?”

current.analysis_note: "no knowledge or discernment to say..." — te.vu.nah absent in the idolater; understanding cannot perceive the absurdity

meaning.text: te.vu.nah names discernment as the inner capacity that idol-worshippers lack — unable to perceive the absurdity of their practice.

new_vcg.title: da.at as absent in the foolish and hollow
new_vcg.description: Knowledge as what is absent, empty, or distorted — speech without knowledge; wisdom that has become falsehood.

db.description: Term names understanding as a human inner-being capacity — enabling wisdom, right conduct, and discernment, received from God

new.title: da.at as absent in the foolish and hollow
new.description: Knowledge as what is absent, empty, or distorted — speech without knowledge; wisdom that has become falsehood.

verses.verse_text: Jer 10:12 It is he who made the earth by his power , who established the world by his wisdom , and by his understanding stretched out the heavens .

current.analysis_note: "by his understanding stretched out the heavens" — te.vu.nah as the divine attribute producing cosmological order

meaning.text: te.vu.nah names understanding as the divine attribute of creative ordering — by which God establishes cosmic structure.

verses.verse_text: Jer 51:15 “It is he who made the earth by his power , who established the world by his wisdom , and by his understanding stretched out the heavens .

current.analysis_note: Same as Jer 10:12 — "by his understanding stretched out the heavens"

meaning.text: te.vu.nah names the divine creative attribute — repeated from Jer 10:12. Same meaning.

verses.verse_text: Eze 28:4 by your wisdom and your understanding you have made wealth for yourself, and have gathered gold and silver into your treasuries ;

current.analysis_note: "by your wisdom and your understanding you have made wealth" — te.vu.nah as the inner capacity producing wealth in the prince of Tyre; pride in self-understanding

meaning.text: te.vu.nah names understanding as an inner capacity the prince of Tyre claims and uses for material gain — the faculty turned toward self-enrichment.

new_vcg.title: te.vu.nah applied to non-sacred or self-serving purposes
new_vcg.description: The same inner capacity applied outside the sacred context — to idol-making or self-enrichment. The term is directionally neutral; its object determines its moral valence.

db.description: Term names understanding as a human inner-being capacity — enabling wisdom, right conduct, and discernment, received from God

new.title: te.vu.nah applied to non-sacred or self-serving purposes
new.description: The same inner capacity applied outside the sacred context — to idol-making or self-enrichment. The term is directionally neutral; its object determines its moral valence.

verses.verse_text: Hos 13:2 And now they sin more and more , and make for themselves metal images , idols skillfully made of their silver , all of them the work of craftsmen . It is said of them, “Those who offer human sacrifice kiss calves !”

current.analysis_note: "idols skillfully made of their silver" — te.vu.nah in craft-skill sense; **FLAG: this may belong in M15-A Group A-1 (craft-skill) not M15-B Group B-16**

meaning.text: te.vu.nah names the craft skill applied to idol-making — the inner understanding faculty used in service of idolatry.

new_vcg.title: te.vu.nah applied to non-sacred or self-serving purposes
