verses.verse_text: Obd 7 All your allies have driven you to your border ; those at peace with you have deceived you; they have prevailed against you; those who eat your bread have set a trap beneath you— you have no understanding .

current.analysis_note: "you have no understanding" — te.vu.nah absent from Edom; the nation lacks inner perceptive capacity

meaning.text: te.vu.nah names understanding as what Edom lacks — the inner perceptive faculty whose absence left them unable to perceive their allies' deception.

verses.verse_text: Obd 8 Will I not on that day , declares the Lord , destroy the wise men out of Edom , and understanding out of Mount Esau ?

current.analysis_note: "destroy... understanding out of Mount Esau" — te.vu.nah as a national inner resource subject to divine judgment

meaning.text: te.vu.nah names understanding as what God will remove from Edom — the inner perceptive capacity that gave them national strength.

verses.verse_text: Job 5:12 He frustrates the devices of the crafty , so that their hands achieve no success .

current.analysis_note: "frustrates devices of crafty, hands achieve no success" — tu.shiy.yah withheld; the crafty lose their effectiveness

meaning.text: tu.shiy.yah names the effective outcome — success — that God withholds from the crafty. The span points to what their scheming cannot produce: sound effective result.

new_vcg.title: tu.shiy.yah: sound wisdom as effective inner resource
new_vcg.description: Sound wisdom as a substantial, effective inner capacity — held by God, available to the upright, able to sustain and protect, violated by isolation, equated with the fear of God.

db.description: tu.shiy.yah names wisdom in its effectiveness — sound wisdom, effective knowledge, the capacity to produce good outcomes. It can be God's attribute (with him is sound wisdom) or a human resource (keep sound wisdom). Reading the verses shows these are the same characteristic in different registers, not two separate groups.

new.title: tu.shiy.yah: sound wisdom as effective inner resource
new.description: Sound wisdom as a substantial, effective inner capacity — held by God, available to the upright, able to sustain and protect, violated by isolation, equated with the fear of God.

verses.verse_text: Job 6:13 Have I any help in me, when resource is driven from me ?

current.analysis_note: "resource driven from me" — tu.shiy.yah experienced as a resource that can be stripped away; Job's lament

meaning.text: tu.shiy.yah names an inner resource — the effective capacity that sustains. Job asks whether any such resource remains when it has been stripped away.

verses.verse_text: Job 11:6 and that he would tell you the secrets of wisdom ! For he is manifold in understanding. Know then that God exacts of you less than your guilt deserves.

current.analysis_note: "manifold in understanding" — tu.shiy.yah as divine depth of wisdom

meaning.text: tu.shiy.yah names wisdom as something God holds in its fullness — secrets of wisdom, manifold in depth. The span points to the inexhaustible inner resource of divine wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:16 With him are strength and sound wisdom ; the deceived and the deceiver are his.

current.analysis_note: "with him are strength and sound wisdom" — tu.shiy.yah as divine attribute; God possesses it essentially

meaning.text: tu.shiy.yah names sound wisdom as a divine attribute — belonging to God, paired with strength. Both the deceived and deceiver are under his sovereign comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Job 26:3 How you have counseled him who has no wisdom , and plentifully declared sound knowledge !

current.analysis_note: "plentifully declared sound knowledge" — tu.shiy.yah communicated from God as effective wisdom

meaning.text: tu.shiy.yah names sound effective knowledge — the real thing, declared in abundance. The verse is ironic: Bildad has done this poorly, despite claiming to do it.

verses.verse_text: Job 30:22 You lift me up on the wind ; you make me ride on it, and you toss me about in the roar of the storm.

current.analysis_note: "you toss me about in the roar of the storm" — tu.shiy.yah absent; Job's situation of complete unmooring

meaning.text: tu.shiy.yah here is translated as toss — an unusual and disputed rendering. The span appears to carry the meaning of being driven or dissolved rather than sound wisdom. This is a difficult text.

current.analysis_note: "stores up sound wisdom for the upright; a shield" — tu.shiy.yah as divinely stored resource for the righteous

meaning.text: tu.shiy.yah names sound wisdom as something God stores and gives — an inner resource available to those who walk uprightly, held in reserve by God for them.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:21 My son , do not lose sight of these— keep sound wisdom and discretion ,

current.analysis_note: "keep sound wisdom and discretion" — tu.shiy.yah as what the person holds onto; a possession to be guarded

meaning.text: tu.shiy.yah names sound wisdom as something to be held and kept — an inner resource that can be retained or lost, valued for its protective function.

current.analysis_note: "I have counsel and sound wisdom; I have strength" — Wisdom personified claiming tu.shiy.yah as her own

meaning.text: tu.shiy.yah names sound wisdom as one of wisdom's own possessions — Wisdom personified claims it as her inner resource alongside counsel, insight, and strength.

verses.verse_text: Pro 18:1 Whoever isolates himself seeks his own desire ; he breaks out against all sound judgment .

current.analysis_note: "breaks out against all sound judgment" — tu.shiy.yah as what the self-seeking person opposes

meaning.text: tu.shiy.yah names sound judgment as the inner standard that isolation violates — the self-seeking person acts against what is effectively wise.

verses.verse_text: Isa 28:29 This also comes from the Lord of hosts ; he is wonderful in counsel and excellent in wisdom .

current.analysis_note: "wonderful in counsel and excellent in wisdom" — tu.shiy.yah as what characterises the Lord of hosts

verses.verse_text: Mic 6:9 The voice of the Lord cries to the city — and it is sound wisdom to fear your name : “ Hear of the rod and of him who appointed it !

current.analysis_note: "it is sound wisdom to fear your name" — tu.shiy.yah defined by its content: the fear of the Lord

meaning.text: tu.shiy.yah names sound wisdom as what the fear of God is — fearing God's name is not mere piety but the effective inner orientation that constitutes genuine wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Hos 11:3 Yet it was I who taught Ephraim to walk; I took them up by their arms , but they did not know that I healed them .

meaning.text: tir.gal names the intimate teaching-to-walk — God as the parent who holds the child's arms and guides the first steps. The inner relational dynamic is of tender, unrecognised nurture. The pathos is that the one taught did not know who taught them.

new_vcg.title: Intimate teaching-to-walk: tir.gal as tender, unrecognised nurture
new_vcg.description: A single verse. God teaching Ephraim to walk — the intimate relational dynamic of the caring parent taking up the child's arms. The pathos is that the one taught did not know who healed them.

db.description: Term names the intimate act of teaching to walk — evoking the inner-relational dynamic between the caring teacher-parent and the dependent learner, with the poignancy of unrecognised nurture
